title: Built-In Playbooks
content:
Cynet includes built-in playbooks that automatically investigate every triggered alert. For a malicious binary alert, the playbook Investigate Malicious File Alert runs. For any other alert, the playbook Investigate Any Alert runs.
All playbooks, built-in and custom, appear in the Playbooks page, where you can edit, delete, or change the playbook trigger.
Check if malicious scripts were created by the malicious process that triggered the alert.
Check if the malicious process attempted lateral movement. If yes, to which endpoints did the process connect.
Check which files the malicious process used and if these files exist on other endpoints. If found, suggest to quarantine them.
Check if other attack tools were downloaded to the endpoint
All the steps listed above
Check if the malicious file exists on other endpoints and suggest to quarantine it

title: Creating Automated Playbooks
content:
Every playbook is based on an entity: Alert, file, host, user or socket. According to the entity you select, you can later include its parameters in the investigation. Alert parameters are listed here .
For optimal results, enable File Monitoring .
If you manage several sites, you can use Import/Export Playbook to share a playbook between sites. you can also use this functionality to save a playbook while you're creating it.
For all active playbooks, you must select an operation mode. The operation mode determines if remediation actions within the Playbook run automatically or require manual approval. If you select Manual, the action remains pending until you approve it via the Incident View (marked in grey):
To enable Manual operation mode, you must clear the Remediation check box in the Group settings page:
For a detailed example of creating a playbook, click here .
To create a playbook:
In the left navigation pane, click .
In the top-right corner, click New playbook . The Playbook canvas opens.
Select the entity type on which to base the playbook investigation: Alert, file, host, user or socket. The Modules menu opens on the left. See Playbook Modules for details about the different modules.
Drag and drop modules onto the editor in the right order to reflect the required investigation flow. Every module includes different input fields, which you can populate with static content or dynamic parameters. Dynamic parameters are populated with the correct value during runtime. These can include parameters from all the previous steps.
Click .
Enter a name for the playbook and click Save . The playbook appears under Playbooks .
To set the playbook to run automatically when a specific alert is triggered, use the Automatic Trigger toggle, then select the alert type that will initiate the playbook.
Note : You can use an API call as a step within a playbook, which allows you to integrate with any external application. The information you gather can be parsed using the JSON parser and included in the playbook investigation.

Playbook Results

Every alert that triggered a playbook is linked to the playbook run, accessible from the Incident View . You can also access playbook results from the Executed Playbooks tab, where all runs are logged with a completion status.

title: Managing Groups - On-Premise
content:
Cynet 360 settings and security configurations are managed per Group. Use Groups to separate settings between subnets, computer types, and departments.
Every endpoint in your network must be associated to a Group. If, when installing the Cynet agent, you do not specify to which Group the endpoint must be added, Cynet 360 adds the endpoint to the default group. In on-premise installation, Cynet 360 can associate an endpoint with a Group by name, IP, or AD group.
The default group for Mac/ Linux/ MSI installed Windows endpoints is Manually Installed Agents.
The default group for Windows endpoints in which the agent was installed using Cynet Launcher, is Out of Range Agents (relevant to on-premise environments only).
For information, see Single Host Installation .
You can use one of the built-in groups or create a new Group. The built-in Groups cannot be removed.

Populating the Group

You can add hosts to a Group according to these parameters:
Host name: Enter host names or upload a CSV file. After creating a list of host names you can export the list to a CSV file. Host names can include the following characters: Upper case letters, lower case letters, numbers, hyphen (-), and underscore (_).
IP Range: Enter IP addresses or IP range of hosts to add to the group (on-premise only).
Active Directory group: Add Active Directory Organizational Units or Security Groups. To add all Organizational Units or Security Groups enter OR: */ OU: *. You must sync with the AD domain before adding hosts. See the Active Directory Domain section below. You can then exclude specific hosts from the group from being scanned by Cynet 360 (on-premise only).
To move a host from one Group to another, select the host, then click Switch Group .

Group Settings

The following table describes the available settings for a Group:
When creating a Group in an on-premise version of Cynet 360, there are additional settings to set:
Separate credentials are necessary for Windows, Linux, and Mac Groups.

Protection Settings

This section lists the protection features that Cynet 360 provides. There are two operating modes:
Best Practice: The configuration Cynet recommends for maximum threat protection. For configuration details and explanations, see Protection Settings .
Manual selection: Manually enable features and specify if these should operate in Detection and/or Prevention mode.

Limitations

In scenarios where both MSI (Microsoft Installer) and Dispatch components coexist on a site, the following limitation exists:
When the option to uninstall MSI from the user interface (UI) is enabled, there can be complications in removing the Dispatch endpoint, particularly if an uninstall password for MSI is in effect.

title: User Activity Alerts
content:
Cynet 360 triggers the following alerts for suspicious user behavior:
User password age is more than 2 years old
User had 5 high alerts in the last 3 months
User has over 10 failed connection attempts
User has logged in to double the normal amount of machines
User has logged in to more than 10 hosts today
User has logged in to more than 5 hosts in one minute
To create an allowlist profile for these alerts, select Auto Server Alerts.
To create and customize alerts for different user behavior triggers, see UBA Rules .

title: Managing Groups
content:
Cynet 360 agent and security settings are managed per Group. Use Groups to separate settings between subnets, computer types, departments, etc.
Every endpoint in your network must be associated to a Group. You can use one of the built-in Groups or create a new Group. The built-in Groups cannot be deleted. If, when installing the Cynet agent, you do not specify to which Group the endpoint must be added, Cynet 360 adds the endpoint to the Group Manually Installed Agents. For information, see Single Host Installation .

Creating a New Group

To create a new group:
From the Cynet toolbar, navigate to Settings > Groups .
Click + New Group .
Enter a name for the group.
Select the operating system for the group from the dropdown list.
Click Save .

Editing a Group

To edit an existing group:
From the Cynet toolbar, navigate to Settings > Groups .
Hover the mouse over the row of the group you want to edit, and a set of icons appears in the Actions column.
Click the Edit icon.
The Edit Group page is comprised of two pages:
Security Settings: This page also includes settings for the Cynet agent installed on the endpoint.
Endpoints: A list of the Group's endpoints, where you can add/remove endpoints.

Deleting a Group

Before deleting a group, you must ensure all endpoints have been removed from the group.
From the Cynet toolbar, navigate to Settings > Groups .
Click the edit icon for the group that you want to delete.
Go into the Endpoints page in the group settings.
Select all the endpoints in the group from the left hand column.
Click Transfer Endpoints .
Select a group to transfer the endpoints to from the dropdown list.
Click Save .
Once there are no endpoints remaining in the group, return to the Settings > Groups page.
Select the group you want to delete and click Delete .
When asked if you are sure you want to delete the group, click Yes .

Group Security Settings

The security settings are divided into six categories. You can turn each category On or Off using the toggle. For certain categories, if On, select the operation mode: Detect : When a threat is detected, trigger an alert only. Prevent : When a threat is detected, Cynet 360 performs prevention actions on the endpoint. These actions block the malicious activity from occurring and affecting other endpoints in the organization.
To turn off specific capabilities within a category that is turned On, click Advanced Settings . The operation mode of the various capabilities within a category are determined by the operation mode of the main category, as set in the main page (except for antivirus, as detailed below).
At any point you can revert to Cynet’s recommended settings by clicking Reset to Best Practice .
Endpoint protection against cyber threats, file-based malware, and malicious activity from trusted and untrusted applications. EPP includes antivirus settings. If EPP is set to Prevent , you can separately set Antivirus to Detect by navigating to Advanced Settings. Read more here .
Continuous endpoint monitoring for known and unknown threats, and automated response workflows.
EDR settings determine the operation mode of Automated Response Playbooks : Disable : Playbooks are turned off and will not perform an autonomous investigation when an alert is triggered Detect : Playbooks are turned on and will perform an autonomous investigation when an alert is triggered. Playbook actions will remain pending until you approve them. Prevent : Playbooks are turned on and will perform an autonomous investigation when an alert is triggered. Playbook actions will be executed immediately.
Network monitoring to unveil reconnaissance, credential theft, user attempts to access malicious domains, and more. Click here for more information.
deploys deception entities on your network to lure attackers. When these entities are used, Cynet 360 detects them and triggers an alert. To apply this feature, in addition to turning it on, configure its settings under Settings > Deception . Click here for more information.
Monitor if forbidden applications are installed and ensure operating systems and applications are up to date and appropriately patched. To apply this feature, in addition to turning it on, configure its settings under Settings > Vulnerability Management . Click here for more information.
Monitor and block insertion of removable storage devices to endpoints in the organization. To apply this feature, in addition to turning it on, create a profile under Settings > Profiles > Storage Device Control . Click here for more information.

Group Endpoints

The left column lists the endpoints that belong to the Group. You can select one or several endpoints and transfer them to a different Group.
The right column lists the endpoints in your environment that are associated to other Groups. You can select one or several endpoints from this list and move them to the Group you are currently creating/editing.

title: Storage Device Control
content:
Cynet 360's Device Control manages and protects hosts against the insertion of unauthorized external storage devices (devices that are not allowed to be used according to the organization policy), such as USB devices. Device Control provides detailed visibility on global device insertion events, which can be critical information during a forensic investigation. Device Control is supported on both Windows and macOS for USB storage devices, SD cards, and other portable devices. Windows endpoints additionally support detection and control of a wider range of portable device classes.
You can create multiple Device Control profiles which you can apply to Groups via the Settings page. Best Practice profile is the default option. Device Control can be turned on and off per Group.
A Storage Device Control profile gives you three optional levels of protection:
Monitor all storage devices that are connected to hosts in the organization. The data is displayed under Forensic > Hosts > System Overview > Storage Device Control.
This mode is active when creating a profile without enabling Enforcement.
Specify the devices that are unauthorized to connect to hosts in the organization (all or specific devices) and trigger an alert when such device is connected. The device is not denied access to the host at this level.
Block an unauthorized device from being used and triggering an alert when such device is connected.
To configure a Device Control profile:
Navigate to Settings > Profiles > Storage Device Control .
In the top-right corner, click Create .
Enter a profile name. Once you apply a profile to a Group, the agent monitors insertion of storage devices to endpoints in your organization.
In the Enforcement section, select the required enforcement method: Track or Block .
Create a list of Authorized/Unauthorized devices to which Cynet applies the enforcement rules you set: Set the default state for every connected device: Authorized or Unauthorized . Add exceptions to the rule (optional). E.g., if you set the default state to Unauthorize, you can add here specific devices that are allowed.
Set the default state for every connected device: Authorized or Unauthorized .
Add exceptions to the rule (optional). E.g., if you set the default state to Unauthorize, you can add here specific devices that are allowed.
Manage alerts. Click the Enable checkbox to trigger an alert when an unauthorized device is connected. If you choose to trigger an alert when an unauthorized device is connected, select the Alert Severity from the dropdown.
Click the Enable checkbox to trigger an alert when an unauthorized device is connected.
If you choose to trigger an alert when an unauthorized device is connected, select the Alert Severity from the dropdown.
Click Save .
To enable Device Control per Group:
Navigate to Settings > Scan Groups .
Select the Group for which you want to enable Device Control.
Under Autonomous Protection , select Storage Device Control check box. To enable Storage Device Control, Memory Protection Mode must be enabled.
From the dropdown list, select a profile.
Click Save .

Monitoring Device Insertions

The Device Control interface displays a fully comprehensive and detailed historical list of all the device insertion events on each host. For example, if the same device is inserted and removed from the same host repeatedly, each event and its details are listed separately. To access the Device Control tab, navigate to Forensics > Hosts > System Overview .
To display a forensic view of Device Control events on a specific host, click the host. The Host page opens. Navigate to the Device Control tab.

Related Alert

From the Device Control alert, starting in v4.18, it is possible to add the ID of a specific device as an exception for the selected profiles.
Go to the Alerts page.
Select the Device Control alert to which you want to add an exception.
Select the Action icon at the right-hand side of the alert.
Enter the device ID for which you want to create the exception.
[Optional] Enter a description for the exception.
Select which profiles to apply the exception to.
Click Save Exception .

title: Executive Summary Report
content:
The Cynet 360 executive summary provides a big-picture overview of your environment. A report per site is sent automatically by email every month. Additionally, you can generate the executive report on demand from the console for the site or a specific Group.
The executive summary report is available to SaaS customers only.
The first page of the report is a top-level overview, whereas the following pages provide more detailed information about specific topics. Included information:

Page 1

The total number of high and critical severity alerts that were triggered in the specified time period.
The total number of closed alerts.
The number of files for which an alert (of all severities) was triggered and on how many of them remediation action was performed.
The number of endpoints for which an alert (of all severities) was triggered.
The number of automatic investigations performed by playbooks, and the number of alert response actions triggered directly or within playbooks.
* The data displayed is based on the date the report was generated, regardless of the report's date range.
Active Endpoints : The number of active endpoints (endpoints that communicated in the last 30 days), including operating system distribution.
Endpoints in Group : The number of endpoints that were ever seen in the site/Group, regardless of the selected time frame, including operating system distribution.
Active Endpoints missing security patches : The number of currently active Windows endpoints that are missing security patches.
* The data displayed is based on the date the report was generated, regardless of the report's date range.
For every category in the Groups Settings page, the number represents the number of Groups in which the feature is Enabled/Disabled and if in Detect/Prevent. This is regardless of the number of endpoints in the group. For example, the NDR line displays the number of Groups set to Detect, the number of Groups set to Prevent, and the number of Groups in which the feature is disabled.

Page 2

The first section shows the current top alerts in the environment. This is sorted first by severity, then by number of alerts. The report only shows the top 7 alerts - to see all alerts, go to the Alerts page on Cynet.
Alert Count by Severity: The number of triggered alerts, by severity.
Common Remediation Actions: The most commonly executed remediation actions, and the number of high and critical severity alerts that each action remediated.

Page 3

The groups, endpoints, and users that triggered the highest number of high and critical severity alerts.

Page 4

* The data displayed is based on the date the report was generated, regardless of the report's date range.
IT hygiene information is calculated according to Vulnerability Management settings:
Top Unauthorized Applications : Among active Windows endpoints in the environment, the most commonly discovered unauthorized applications.
Top Unpatched Applications : Among active Windows endpoints in the environment, the most commonly discovered applications that are not in the required version (unpatched).
Active Endpoints missing security patches : The active Windows endpoints with the highest number of missing security patches.

Page 5

Overall Summary: The number of emails scanned, the number of threats prevented, number of malicious attachments, and number of malicious URLs detected.
Protected Categories: The top categories in which threats were detected, and how many were detected in each category.
Most Attacked Users: The users who received the highest number of malicious emails and how many were sent to each.
Note: If Email Security is not configured on your system, the Email Security page will not appear in your Executive Report.

Page 6

Overall Summary: The number of Cloud services and SaaS applications protected by Cynet, the number of users of those services and applications, and the number of admin users out of the total number of users.
Top High & Critical Risks: The categories with the largest number of high and critical risks, ordered first by the number of open risks, then by the total number of risks in the category.
High & Critical Risks per Service: The services with the largest number of high and critical risks, ordered first by the number of open risks, then by the total number of risks in the service.
Note: If SSPM is not configured on your system, you will not see the SaaS & Cloud page in your Executive Report.
To generate a report:
From the Cynet toolbar, navigate to Reports > View Reports .
From the list of reports, select Executive Report (1).
In the Date Range fields, enter the start and end date (2).
Generate a report for the site (3) or select a Group and generate a report for the Group (4).

Note: If SSPM is not configured on your system, you will not see the SaaS & Cloud page in your Executive Report.
To generate a report:
From the Cynet toolbar, navigate to Reports > View Reports .
From the list of reports, select Executive Report (1).
In the Date Range fields, enter the start and end date (2).
Generate a report for the site (3) or select a Group and generate a report for the Group (4).

title: How to Connect Microsoft Azure
content:
Connect your Microsoft Azure account to Cynet 360 to view misconfigurations in the SSPM page. To connect Microsoft Azure, you need to provide the following details:
To get Azure details:
Log into your Azure Portal and navigate to the Azure Active Directory service.
Select App registrations, then click New registration .
Enter "CynetAzure" and/or a descriptive name in the Name field.
For Supported account types , leave the default value: Accounts in this organizational directory only (YOURDIRECTORYNAME).
Click Register . A new app is registered. In the Cynet Integrations page, enter the Subscription ID (=Object ID), Application ID, and Directory ID.
Navigate to Certificates & secrets .
Under Client secrets , click New client secret .
Enter a Description (i.e. CynetAzure-2023) and select Expires in 1 year .
Click Add . In the Cynet Integrations page, enter the Client secret (Secret Key). The Client secret value appears only once, make sure you store it safely.
Navigate to Subscriptions .
Click on the relevant Subscription ID.
Click Access Control (IAM) .
Go to the Role assignments tab.
Click Add , then Add role assignment .
In the Role drop-down, select Security Reader .
Leave the default value for Assign access to .
In the right pane, under Select, type the name of the app registration (CynetAzure) you created and select it.
Click Save .
Repeat steps 15-18 for the role Log Analytics Reader.

title: How to Connect Google Cloud Platform
content:
Connect your Google Cloud Platform (GCP) account to Cynet 360 to view misconfigurations in the CSPM&SSPM page.
To get the required details for connecting GCP, follow these steps:
If you don't have a Google project, create one . Cynet CSPM scans every project individually.
Create a security audit role.
Create a service account.
To create a security audit role:
Log into your Google Cloud console and "Activate" your Cloud Shell.
Create a new file called cynet-security-audit-role.yaml. You can use: nano cynet-security-audit-role.yaml .
Copy and paste the following yaml code in the file on your Cloud Shell, press Ctrl + X and type "Y" to save the file.
With an admin user that is also Organization Role Administrator, and using your Organization Id to create the Role at the organizational level, run the following command: gcloud iam roles create CynetCSPMSecurityAudit --organization=YOUR_ORGANIZATION_ID --file=cynet-security-audit-role.yaml Note, you can use --project=YOUR_PROJECT_ID instead of --organization=<YOUR_ORGANIZATION_ID> If you use a Project ID instead of an Organization ID, exclude all rows starting with 'resourcemanager' in the file cynet-security-audit-role.yaml .
Note, you can use --project=YOUR_PROJECT_ID instead of --organization=<YOUR_ORGANIZATION_ID>
If you use a Project ID instead of an Organization ID, exclude all rows starting with 'resourcemanager' in the file cynet-security-audit-role.yaml .
The security audit role is created.
To create a service account:
Log into your Google Cloud console and navigate to IAM Admin > Service Accounts .
Click Create Service Account .
For Service account name, enter Cynet .
In the description, enter Cynet API Access .
Click Continue .
Select the role: Custom > Cynet CSPM Security Audit .
Click Continue .
Click Create Key and leave the default JSON selected.
Click Create . The key is downloaded to your machine.
From the JSON file you created, copy the Project Id, Client Email and Private Key values and paste them in the Cynet 360 console, under Settings > Integrations > Google Cloud .

title: Investigating Network Activity
content:
The Forensic page includes information on all the domains resolved on protected endpoints, as well as all the network sockets created on endpoints in the environment. To customize or search the Domain/Socket list, see Searching the Forensic Page .
Select the domain/socket you want to investigate. The page provides information that Cynet 360 collected about the selected Domain/socket. At the top of the page, a timeline describes the lifecycle of the entity. Below, you can see how the entity is related to other entities in the organization. All the displayed objects are clickable.
The lower section of the page displays multiple tabs providing detailed information about this domain/socket.
A tab does not appear if there is no relevant data to be displayed for that category.
For domain: Information about the domain, including:
The number of endpoints that have accessed the domain
The first/last time the domain was seen
Domain properties
Domain overview
Security intelligence
For socket: Metadata about the host, such as local IP, remote IP, ports used by the socket, first and last seen.

title: Antivirus Scanning
content:
As part of our endpoint protection capabilities, Cynet offers a wide variety of Antivirus scanning abilities. The Antivirus scan is supported on Windows OS, and is available starting from version 4.17.
The Antivirus scans allow you to scan an entire endpoint, or choose specific locations to scan. Every endpoint can run only one scan at a time. This will be determined according to the scan priority, which is detailed in the table at the end of this article.
This feature will be available only if the Antivirus mechanism is enabled. In addition, if a malicious file was found during the scan, the action (detect/prevent) will take place according to the Antivirus settings.

Creating a New Scan Profile

You can create an Antivirus scan profile to match per Group. Every group can have one or more scan profiles.
You can create a new Antivirus profile by going to Settings > Profiles > Antivirus . There you can find all of the existing Antivirus profiles.
To create a new profile, click New Profile .
When you create a scan profile, you will be able to select from the options below.

When to Scan

This allows you to choose the Antivirus scan trigger.
Scheduled scans enable you to set up a date and time to run your scans. Scheduled scans apply on all hosts within a selected scan group. The scans will run in parallel on all hosts, based on the scheduled date and time (unless you have set up gradual scans - detailed below).
In the Profiles menu, under the Antivirus tab, you can schedule (every day, week, or month) a full or custom Antivirus scan from the UI, per scan group.
Daily Scans
Daily scans will run each day at the scheduled time you set.
Weekly Scans
Weekly scans will run one day a week, based on the days you select. You can also choose to run the scan more than once a week (at the time you select).
Monthly Scans
Monthly scans will run on a specific day of the month (for example, every 10th of the month), or on a specified day of the month, by day of the week (for example, the first Monday of every month).
Note : You can also exclude specific paths from the scan. Exclusions will apply to the path and its subfolders.
There is also an option to request that the scheduled scan be gradual. You can decide the gradual scan period, and during this timeframe (in hours), each endpoint on the group will start the scan.
You can also choose a special event as a trigger for a scheduled antivirus scan.
To create a scheduled event-based scan, go to Settings > Profiles > Antivirus .
Create a new profile, and under Where to Scan , select USB insertion .
This will enable you to run a scan on the USB device upon USB insertion.

What to Scan

This allows you to choose the locations of the Antivirus scan.
Running processes
All internal drives
All removable drives
Note: Network drives are not included in the full scan, but can be included in Custom scans.
You can choose specific locations to run an Antivirus scheduled scan on. You can choose pre-defined locations as well as desired locations.
To create a custom scan, go to Settings > Profiles > Antivirus .
In the scan profile, under Where to Scan , click Custom Scan . Then click Add Location to apply the scan to specific locations only. This includes:
External drives
Program folders (Program Data + Program Files)
User profile folders
System drives (including System-level network drives)
System folders
Temp folders
Additionally, you can insert a specific location to scan by choosing "path". You can also include sub-folders here:

Exclusions

You can exclude specific paths from the scan on each profile. Exclusions will apply to the path and its subfolders.
The Antivirus scan exclusion will defer to the Cynet Exclusion feature, so that if you have set an exclusion regardless of the scan profile, it will be excluded from the Antivirus scan as well.

Match Scan per Group

After creating an Antivirus scan profile, you have to match the profile per group, so it will run on the relevant endpoints. You can do this in Settings > Groups .
Under the Endpoint Protection advanced settings, you can enable the Antivirus Scan Profile , and choose the desired profile.
You can match one or more profiles per group:
Note : Do not match two profiles that are scheduled to run at the same time (one of them will be canceled).

Monitoring Scans

You can view all scan logs, including their status per endpoint, in one view, in Actions > Hosts > Antivirus .
You will be able to see the following details regarding each scan:
Endpoint (hostname)
Scan profile - the Antivirus scan profile name
Scan type (scheduled/event based/on-demand)
Group
Scan status - will indicate if the scan is still in progress, completed, canceled or failed.
Scan details - the number of malicious files found in the scan.
Scan start and end time.
Scan start and end time.
Note : You will receive an alert if a malicious file was found during the scan. This alert will continue triggering on each scan, unless the malicious file is removed.

On-Demand Scans

Host actions can be invoked to perform a full Antivirus scan from the UI, for a specific endpoint.
When the endpoint receives the request, the scan will start running immediately. On-demand scans will stop any other running scan and will start running.
Note : The scan type for an on-demand scan will be for a full Antivirus scan only.
You can allow the end-user to initiate or stop an on-demand Antivirus scan locally, from the endpoint itself. This can be done by right-clicking the tray icon.
You can control whether to enable this functionality in the Group settings.
Navigate to Settings > Group and edit the group for which you want to enable the tray icon on endpoint.
Click Manage under Agent Settings .
Enable the Display Tray Icon on Endpoint toggle.
Enable the Enable A ctions on Endpoint toggle.
Select Admins or All Users to determine who can initiate or stop an on-demand Antivirus scan locally.
This will show the Cynet Tray icon in the endpoints, and enable initiating an Antivirus scan. If you select Admins , only local admins will be allowed to initiate an Antivirus scan from the tray icon. If you select All Users , any user will be allowed to initiate an Antivirus scan from the tray icon.

Scan Priorities

As only one Antivirus scan can run on an endpoint at a time, the scans will work according to the following priorities:
On-demand
Event-based scan
Schedules scan
On demand from tray icon
If an on-demand scan is in progress, the only scan that can stop it is another on-demand scan; otherwise, it will be cancelled.
If an event-based scan is in progress, an on-demand scan will cancel it. If another scan is received, it will be cancelled.
If a scheduled scan is in progress, an on-demand scan or event-based scan will cancel it. If an on-demand from a tray icon scan is received, it will be cancelled.
An On-demand from tray icon scan will be available only if there is no other scan running on the endpoint. In addition, if an on-demand from tray icon scan is in progress and another scan is received, the on-demand from tray icon will be cancelled.

title: XDR / ITDR Alerts
content:
Cynet provides threat detection based on correlating forensic events from both endpoints and third-party sources - such as firewalls, Microsoft 365, etc.
This type of threat detection triggers an alert called an XDR / ITDR Alert . XDR / ITDR alerts could be, for example, malicious activity detected on OneDrive or a Fortigate firewall.
XDR / ITDR alerts identify attacks on:
Endpoints: Our platform seamlessly integrates with endpoint devices, collecting and analyzing critical data such as process executions, file modifications, network connections, and system events in real-time.
Network Devices: We support data ingestion from network devices such as firewalls, routers, and switches, enabling comprehensive monitoring of network traffic, firewall logs, and intrusion detection/prevention system (IDS/IPS) logs.
Cloud Environments: Our XDR solution extends its coverage to cloud environments, including major cloud service providers such as Microsoft 365. This integration enables monitoring of user activities, file access events, and configuration changes within cloud environments.
Identity and Access Management (IAM) Systems: Our platform integrates seamlessly with IAM systems such as Active Directory, enabling monitoring of user authentication events, group membership changes, and administrative actions.
Advanced Correlation: Our platform employs sophisticated correlation techniques to analyze data from multiple sources, enabling the detection of complex attack patterns and potential threats that might otherwise go unnoticed.
Real-time Detection: Through continuous monitoring and real-time alerts, our platform enables organizations to detect security threats as they happen, minimizing the impact of cyber incidents on business operations. Our dedicated security expert in CyOps is proactively monitoring the alerts and will let you know when security threats occur.
For more information on 3rd party systems which can connect to Cynet, see Certified Data Sources . N ot all certified data sources currently trigger XDR alerts. They are being added gradually, and eventually all certified data sources will trigger alerts. For additional details, please contact Cynet support .
The team of cybersecurity experts at Cynet employs a dual approach to threat detection: On one hand, connected data sources automatically trigger certain alerts, and Cynet efficiently forwards these alerts to the customer's dashboard for visibility. On the other hand, a significant portion of the alerts result from the expertise of Cynet's cybersecurity team.
Our cybersecurity team meticulously designs and implements specific detection logic tailored to identify nuanced threats, ensuring a proactive and comprehensive defense against potential security breaches. This combination of automated alerts and expert-crafted logic enhances the overall effectiveness of the threat detection capabilities within Cynet's platform.
XDR / ITDR Alerts include the following alert types:
Microsoft 365
Firewalls
VPN
VMWare ESXi

Creating an Allowlist Profile for XDR / ITDR Alerts

You can also create an Allowlist profile for XDR / ITDR alerts based on the alert name.
To create an Allowlist profile:
Go to Settings > Allowlist & Exclusions > Allowlist .
Click + New to create a new profile or click Edit on an existing profile.
Enter a name for the profile.
From the Applied on alert dropdown, select XDR / ITDR Alerts .
From the Type dropdown, select the alert type (available alert types are Alert Name , IP , User , and Other Fields ).
Enter one or more values for your alert and click the plus icon.
Optionally specify the Groups and Hosts to which the rule should be applied.
Click Save .

Creating an Alert Simulation for XDR / ITDR Alerts

To simulate an XDR / ITDR alert for Azure Active Directory :
Log in to Microsoft Azure: https://portal.azure.com/#home
Log in to Microsoft Azure: https://portal.azure.com/#home
Go to the Users section in Azure.
Go to the Users section in Azure.
Click on ‘New User’ to create a new user in Azure.
Create a new user with the principal name "Cynet360-test" (with a capital letter C).
Save the new user.
An Informative Alert will be generated in the Cynet Alerts View.
To simulate an XDR / ITDR alert for Fortigate Firewall :
Go to the Fortigate console.
Attempt to log into the Fortigate console with the user Cynet360-test@cynet.com (with a capital letter C).
An Informative Alert will be generated in the Cynet Alerts View.

An Informative Alert will be generated in the Cynet Alerts View.

title: How to Integrate Cynet 360 with ConnectWise
content:
Integrating Cynet with ConnectWise enables you to sync alert management in Cynet 360 with ticket management in ConnectWise. A new ticket is created when an alert is triggered and closed when you change the alert status to Closed or Ignore. Closing a ConnectWise ticket closes the corresponding alert in Cynet 360. Updating an alert (with the same alert ID) updates the existing ConnectWise ticket, unless it was already closed, in which case the closed ticket will change to new. If the ticket was deleted in ConnectWise, updating the alert in Cynet 360 will open a new ticket in ConnectWise.
Alerts are sent to ConnectWise from the time you completed setting up the integration and onwards. Cynet does not send previously triggered alerts.

Configuring the Connection in Cynet

You must create new API keys for Cynet in the ConnectWise Manage system and make a note of both the Private and Public API keys.
You must configure the permissions for Cynet in the ConnectWise Manage system .
To configure the ConnectWise integration:
Navigate to Settings > Integrations .
Click the ConnectWise button.
Enter the details of your ConnectWise account. Server: The ConnectWise server site URL without the protocol or WWW. For example : helpdesk.customer.com Company: The ConnectWise company ID. Public Key: The ConnectWise API public key (copied after creating new API keys for Cynet in ConnectWise ). Private Key: The ConnectWise API private key (copied after creating new API keys for Cynet in ConnectWise ).
Server: The ConnectWise server site URL without the protocol or WWW. For example : helpdesk.customer.com
Company: The ConnectWise company ID.
Public Key: The ConnectWise API public key (copied after creating new API keys for Cynet in ConnectWise ).
Private Key: The ConnectWise API private key (copied after creating new API keys for Cynet in ConnectWise ).
Click Authenticate .
Once authentication is successful, continue to configure the integration.

Configuring the ConnectWise Integration

The ConnectWise integration contains three sections that need configuring.
In the Configuration section, select the following: Company: Select the Company from the dropdown list. Board: Select the servce board from the dropdown list.
Company: Select the Company from the dropdown list.
Board: Select the servce board from the dropdown list.
In the Tickets Status section, select the following: New Tickets: Select the status in Cynet for New tickets in ConnectWise. Resolved Tickets: Select the status in Cynet for Resolved tickets in ConnectWise.
New Tickets: Select the status in Cynet for New tickets in ConnectWise.
Resolved Tickets: Select the status in Cynet for Resolved tickets in ConnectWise.
[Optional] In the Billing section, set the Connect Billing toggle to On , and then select the following: Agreement: Select an Agreement from the dropdown. Product: Select a Product from the dropdown.
Agreement: Select an Agreement from the dropdown.
Product: Select a Product from the dropdown.
In the Priority Levels section, select the ConnectWise P riority Levels that correspond to each of the Cynet Priority levels.
Click Save .
Once the integration is created, new alerts in Cynet will be created as new tickets in the ConnectWise console.

Verifying the Integration

One the integration is complete, you can verify it by clicking on the 3 dots next to the integration name, and clicking Create Test Ticket , then Send Test Alert .

title: Agent Settings
content:
As part of the group settings, you can configure agent settings for each group. Within these settings, there are different options you can toggle on or off, depending on your needs:
Automatic Agent Update
Display Alerts on Endpoint
Display Tray Icon on Endpoint
Unscanned Group Alert
Set Agent Uninstall Password
Perform backups to enable rollback
Toggling this to On enables the endpoint to automatically update the Cynet agent when a new version is available.
Toggling this to On displays an alert on the affected endpoint when a threat is detected. An example can be seen in the following image.
Toggling this to On displays a Cynet tray icon on endpoints where the agent is installed. Hovering over the tray icon provides information on the agent version, AV version, and last AV update.
Right-clicking on the tray icon enables the end-user to initiate or cancel an on-demand Antivirus scan locally, from the endpoint itself.
To enable on-demand scans from the endpoint tray icon:
Go to Settings > Group and select the group on which you want to enable display tray icon on endpoint.
Within the Agent Settings, click Manage.
Enable the Display Tray Icon on Endpoint toggle.
Enable the Enable Actions on Endpoint toggle.
Click OK .
Toggling this to On triggers an alert in the Cynet console if none of the endpoints in this group were scanned in the last hour.
Toggling this to On requires the user to enter a password when attempting to uninstall the Cynet agent. The password must be set by the admin when turning the toggle on.
When this option is on, if an end-user wants to uninstall the Cynet agent from their endpoint, they are prompted to enter the password before they can complete the uninstallation process.

Perform Backups to Enable Rollback

Toggling this to On tells Cynet to perform regular backups to enable customers to revert an encrypted endpoint to its healthy state from before the encryption.
For more information about endpoint rollback, see Endpoint Rollback .

title: Enhanced Multi-Site Platform
content:
Cynet offers a multi-site platform built to fit the requirements of MSP and large enterprise customers.
The multi-site platform offers a hierarchical view of your users and sites, which lets you more easily manage them.
Via the account level, MSP admins will have the ability to view and take actions on the entire hierarchy, such as specifying a central security policy or creating a new site. Data presented at the account level is aggregated from the site level. Cynet is in continuous development and enhancement of account level capabilities. Currently supported are Alerts, Forensic, Audit, Reports and Settings.
Cynet multi-site hierarchy includes three entities.
MSP/Account Level: At this level, the MSP admin manages customer site creation and has visibility of resource consumption.
Site/End Customer: At this level, the MSP admin manages the end customer's environment.
Site Manager: This entity enables MSPs to manage end customers with multiple sites.
Selecting a site at the lowest level of the hierarchy gives access to all components of the site.
This multi-tenant approach enables the MSP to provide a single surface for all activities related to operating multiple sites.

Site Navigation

Through the navigation bar, the MSP admin can navigate between sites. Sites will be visible based on the user role. You can search for a specific site, or scroll to see all the sites you have access to.
For more information, see Managing Users and Managing Sites .

title: Managing Users
content:
Cynet offers enhanced user management capabilities, allowing you to use the Users tab in the Settings to manage your users.
From this tab you can perform the following actions:
Add, edit and delete users.
Manage roles (see Role Based Access Control ).
Manage multi-factor authentication .
The Users tab includes a number of different columns providing information about the existing users:
Email. The user's email address.
Name. The user's name.
Role. The role the user holds.
Sites. Which sites the user has access to.
Last Session. The date that the user last logged into the system.
Login method. The current login method for the user. Possible methods are Local and SSO.
MFA Status. The current status of the user's multi-factor authentication token. Possible statuses are Pending and Synced .
Actions. When hovering over a particular user, this shows the actions that can be taken for that user. These include editing, deleting, resending the registration email, and renewing the MFA token.
To create a user:
From the Global level in Cynet, go to Settings > Users & Roles
Click Add User .
Enter the new user's email address and name in the appropriate fields.
Select a role for the user from the User Role dropdown.
Select the login method for the user from the Login Method dropdown.
Select whether the user should have access to All Sites , or select Custom , and choose specific sites to give the user access to. Note: If the user has access to All Sites , this gives them access to all future sites that are created as well as all existing sites.
Click Add .
The system sends an email invitation to the new user with a link to complete their registration.
Once the user clicks the link in the welcome email, they are prompted to create a new password and send a text message to their mobile phone in order to complete the registration.

title: Allowlisting
content:
Cynet 360 enables you to list resources that are authorized to run in a user’s environment, for which Cynet 360 will not trigger an alert. It is recommended to allowlist known and legitimate resources to prevent false-positive alerts.
As opposed to allowlisting, excluding an activity prevents it from being scanned by the Cynet agent entirely. Exclusion profiles can be applied to a wider set of parameters and are useful for third-party applications running on your environment.
An allowlist profile is based on a rule that combines an alert type, an alert parameter, and its value. For the allowlisting rule to be effective, it's crucial to understand by which parameter to allowlist. The same information can appear differently in different alerts, so creating a rule for a specific alert is preferable to a rule that applies to all alerts. See allowlisting examples below.
There are two ways to create an Allowlist profile (detailed below):
From a specific alert, in the Alert page. Cynet 360 automatically populates the profile parameters according to the selected alert.
From the Settings page.
Quick allowlisting (from an alert) allows allowlisting based on file path or sha256 only. When creating an allowlisting profile from the Settings page, you can create an allowlisting rule based on other alert parameters (such as process command line).
Note: V alues in allowlists are case sensitive.
To create an Allowlist profile from an alert:
In the Alert page, select an alert and click .
In the Allowlist tab, enter a descriptive profile name.
Under Description select whether to allowlist the File SHA256 , the file path/partial path , or the Process SHA256 . To allowlist by other parameters, create an allowlisting profile from the Settings page.
Apply to is populated according to the host and group that triggered the alert. Select whether to apply the profile to the H ost or the G roup . If you do not select a check box, the allowlist profile applies to all protected endpoints.
Click Create Profile .
When allowlisting a file from a specific alert, you are preventing this specific alert type from being triggered for this file in the future. The file can still trigger different alert types.
To create an Allowlist profile from the Settings page:
Navigate to Settings > Allowlist & Exclusions > Allowlist .
Click New .
Enter a profile name.
Under Rules : From the list of alert names in the Applied on alert dropdown, select the alert for which to create an allowlist rule. The Type dropdown list is populated with parameters according to the alert you selected. From the Type dropdown, select an alert parameter. Additional Fields are parameters listed under Extra Info in the alert description (see image). Enter the parameter Value and click + . Click the plus icon to the right of the Value field. Repeat steps 4a - 4d to create additional rules. OR is applied between rules in the same profile. Supported wildcards are asterisk "*" and question mark "?".
From the list of alert names in the Applied on alert dropdown, select the alert for which to create an allowlist rule. The Type dropdown list is populated with parameters according to the alert you selected.
From the Type dropdown, select an alert parameter. Additional Fields are parameters listed under Extra Info in the alert description (see image).
Enter the parameter Value and click + .
Click the plus icon to the right of the Value field.
Repeat steps 4a - 4d to create additional rules. OR is applied between rules in the same profile. Supported wildcards are asterisk "*" and question mark "?".
If you want to apply the allowlist profile to specific Groups or endpoints, select these from the Group and Hosts dropdown lists. If no Groups/endpoints are specified, the rule applies to all protected endpoints.
Optionally add a description.
Click Save . The Allowlist rule is set. Alerts that match the rule are not generated.
Note: Parameter values in allowlists are case sensitive.
To export Allowlists:
Navigate to Settings > Allowlist & Exclusions > Allowlist .
Click on the three dots menu icon at the right-hand side of the screen.
Click Export . The JSON file is downloaded.
Exporting includes all Allowlist profiles that exist on the current site. It includes all the values contained in the following fields:
Profile Name
Profile Description
Applied on alert
Type
Value
To import an Allowlist:
Navigate to Settings > Allowlist & Exclusions > Allowlist .
Click on the three dots menu icon at the right-hand side of the screen.
Click Import .
Drag and drop a file onto the import box, or click Browse to select a file from your computer.
Choose whether to keep existing profiles with the same name, or replace existing profiles with the same name.
Click Continue . The profiles are imported and applied to all groups.
Note: Since details of the Hosts or Groups to which a profile was applied are not included when exporting profiles, when importing, the profiles will be applied to all Groups.
Allowlisting directly from an alert by file path or SHA256: Useful for alerts triggered by a file, such as File Dumped on Disk.
Allowlisting based on other alert parameters: For example, in case of a Powershell Malicious Command alert, the parameter for allowlisting is the command line.
Create the following allowlisting rule:
Allowlisting of unauthorized file operation attempts (allowlisting the Unauthorized File Operation Attempt alert).
Allowlisting based on XDR alerts . You can create an allowlist profile for XDR alerts, based on the alert name, IP, User, or Other Fields.

Allowlist Profiles in Global View

Global operators can create allowlist profiles that apply to all environments in the Global Manager, or to specific sites.
The Global tab displays the list of allowlist profiles that apply to all sites. To create an allowlist profile that applies to all environments, click Create .
To create an allowlist profile that applies to all environments, click Create .
The Site tab displays the list of allowlist profiles that apply to a specific site. To create an allowlist profile for a specific site, navigate to the individual site dashboard.
To create an allowlist profile for a specific site, navigate to the individual site dashboard.

title: Managing Sites and Resource Usage
content:
The Sites page at the MSP/Account level allows users with the Global Manager and Operator Manage Sites roles to monitor, manage and create the sites they have access to from a single location. To view these settings, navigate to Settings > Sites .
At the top of the Sites page, you can see a summary of your resource usage, including the following:
Sites: The total number of managed sites in your hierarchy, excluding Site Managers.
Active Endpoints: The total number of active endpoints across all sites. Active endpoints include all endpoints that were seen in the last 30 days.
Total Unique Integrations: The total number of unique integrations across all sites.
CLM 14 day, 30 day, and 90 day retention: Shows the amount of CLM data ingested in the last 30 days, which is subject to each retention period.
Next, you can see the list of sites, including the following data for each site:
ID: The ID specified for each site reflects the ID that the customer instance has with Cynet relating to the license activation process and contact ID for the CyOps team.
Name: The site name.
Type: Site or Site Manager.
Endpoints: The total number of endpoints on the site. This includes both active and inactive endpoints. Additionally, if you are tracking endpoint usage for the site and have used 100% of more of the endpoints currently allocated, an icon is displayed next to the number of endpoints alerting you to this.
Assigned Endpoints : The total number of endpoints allocated to the site. This column only contains values for sites for which you are tracking endpoint usage.
Billing Type: The site's billing type. Possible billing types are: Paying: For paid customers. POC: A temporary site limited for 14 weeks with the option to request extensions, typically used for demonstrating to prospective customers. NFR: A tenant used by partners for education and demo purposes.
Paying: For paid customers.
POC: A temporary site limited for 14 weeks with the option to request extensions, typically used for demonstrating to prospective customers.
NFR: A tenant used by partners for education and demo purposes.
CLM Retention: The site's current CLM retention. Possible options are 14 day, 30 day or 90 day.
CLM Data: The amount of CLM data that was ingested in the last 30 days.
Integrations: The number of unique integrations connected to the site.
Status: The current site status. Possible options include Active, Failed and Pending.

Creating and Editing Sites

To create a new site:
Navigate to Settings > Sites .
Click + Add Site .
Select if the site should be a Site or Site Manager.
Enter a name, contact name, contact email, contact phone and optional comment in the General Details section.
For a site (but not Site Manager), select the Billing Type (options include POC , Paying and NFR ), Package (options include Elite , Protect , and All-in-One ) and CLM Retention License (options include CLM Trial, CLM 14 Days Retention, CLM 30 Days Retention, CLM 90 Days Retention and CLM Long Term Retention) from the dropdown lists.
For a site (but not Site Manager), select whether or not to track the site's Endpoint Usage . If tracking, enter the number of endpoints assigned to the site. (see also Assigning Endpoint Limits Per Site , below).
Click Save .
For a full explanation and feature comparison of the different packages, see Cynet Packages .
To edit an existing site:
Navigate to Settings > Sites .
Select a site to edit.
Click Edit Site or select the Edit icon from the actions.
Edit the site details as desired.
Click Save .
Note: When upgrading a site from the Protect package to Elite or All-in-One, or from Elite to All-in-One, it can take up to 4 hours for features of the new package to be available in the Cynet Console.

Retention Policy

MSPs that manage their customers' sites can specify their CLM retention policy (according to their license).
The options include:
None = limited CLM retention.
30 days = CLM with 30 days of hot retention.
90 days = CLM with 90 days of hot retention.
>90 days = CLM 90 days hot retention plus long-term cold log retention.
Note that all of the settings above can be applied during creation of a new site (via the Add Site button), or later during the editing of an existing site (via the Edit Site button).

Assigning Endpoint Limits Per Site

Site-specific endpoint limits provide you with the ability to track resource consumption for each site you manage. This capability enables you to optimize service delivery costs while ensuring each client site receives appropriate endpoint protection coverage based on their service agreement and business requirements.
On the 1st of each month, you will receive an email about any sites which have gone over their allocated consumption. The email is sent to the email of the MSP Account contact you provided to Cynet. Details of this contact information can be seen in the Site Details shown in the site picker, when you hover over the top level account.
To enable and manage Endpoint Usage Tracking, either select it when creating a new site, or when editing an existing site, as detailed in Step 6 of Creating and Editing Sites above.
To change the contact information, please contact Cynet Support or your Customer Success representative.

sections.text:
The Sites page at the MSP/Account level allows users with the Global Manager and Operator Manage Sites roles to monitor, manage and create the sites they have access to from a single location. To view these settings, navigate to Settings > Sites .
At the top of the Sites page, you can see a summary of your resource usage, including the following:
Sites: The total number of managed sites in your hierarchy, excluding Site Managers.
Active Endpoints: The total number of active endpoints across all sites. Active endpoints include all endpoints that were seen in the last 30 days.
Total Unique Integrations: The total number of unique integrations across all sites.
CLM 14 day, 30 day, and 90 day retention: Shows the amount of CLM data ingested in the last 30 days, which is subject to each retention period.
Next, you can see the list of sites, including the following data for each site:
ID: The ID specified for each site reflects the ID that the customer instance has with Cynet relating to the license activation process and contact ID for the CyOps team.
Name: The site name.
Type: Site or Site Manager.
Endpoints: The total number of endpoints on the site. This includes both active and inactive endpoints. Additionally, if you are tracking endpoint usage for the site and have used 100% of more of the endpoints currently allocated, an icon is displayed next to the number of endpoints alerting you to this.
Assigned Endpoints : The total number of endpoints allocated to the site. This column only contains values for sites for which you are tracking endpoint usage.
Billing Type: The site's billing type. Possible billing types are: Paying: For paid customers. POC: A temporary site limited for 14 weeks with the option to request extensions, typically used for demonstrating to prospective customers. NFR: A tenant used by partners for education and demo purposes.
Paying: For paid customers.
POC: A temporary site limited for 14 weeks with the option to request extensions, typically used for demonstrating to prospective customers.
NFR: A tenant used by partners for education and demo purposes.
CLM Retention: The site's current CLM retention. Possible options are 14 day, 30 day or 90 day.
CLM Data: The amount of CLM data that was ingested in the last 30 days.
Integrations: The number of unique integrations connected to the site.
Status: The current site status. Possible options include Active, Failed and Pending.

sections.heading: Creating and Editing Sites
sections.text:
To create a new site:
Navigate to Settings > Sites .
Click + Add Site .
Select if the site should be a Site or Site Manager.
Enter a name, contact name, contact email, contact phone and optional comment in the General Details section.
For a site (but not Site Manager), select the Billing Type (options include POC , Paying and NFR ), Package (options include Elite , Protect , and All-in-One ) and CLM Retention License (options include CLM Trial, CLM 14 Days Retention, CLM 30 Days Retention, CLM 90 Days Retention and CLM Long Term Retention) from the dropdown lists.
For a site (but not Site Manager), select whether or not to track the site's Endpoint Usage . If tracking, enter the number of endpoints assigned to the site. (see also Assigning Endpoint Limits Per Site , below).
Click Save .
For a full explanation and feature comparison of the different packages, see Cynet Packages .
To edit an existing site:
Navigate to Settings > Sites .
Select a site to edit.
Click Edit Site or select the Edit icon from the actions.
Edit the site details as desired.
Click Save .
Note: When upgrading a site from the Protect package to Elite or All-in-One, or from Elite to All-in-One, it can take up to 4 hours for features of the new package to be available in the Cynet Console.

sections.heading: Retention Policy
sections.text:
MSPs that manage their customers' sites can specify their CLM retention policy (according to their license).
The options include:
None = limited CLM retention.
30 days = CLM with 30 days of hot retention.
90 days = CLM with 90 days of hot retention.
>90 days = CLM 90 days hot retention plus long-term cold log retention.
Note that all of the settings above can be applied during creation of a new site (via the Add Site button), or later during the editing of an existing site (via the Edit Site button).

sections.heading: Assigning Endpoint Limits Per Site
sections.text:
Site-specific endpoint limits provide you with the ability to track resource consumption for each site you manage. This capability enables you to optimize service delivery costs while ensuring each client site receives appropriate endpoint protection coverage based on their service agreement and business requirements.
On the 1st of each month, you will receive an email about any sites which have gone over their allocated consumption. The email is sent to the email of the MSP Account contact you provided to Cynet. Details of this contact information can be seen in the Site Details shown in the site picker, when you hover over the top level account.
To enable and manage Endpoint Usage Tracking, either select it when creating a new site, or when editing an existing site, as detailed in Step 6 of Creating and Editing Sites above.
To change the contact information, please contact Cynet Support or your Customer Success representative.

title: SSPM Reports & Notifications
content:
Cynet provides the option to send emails to specified people about risks. You can either send an email with all results every 24 hours, or starting in version 4.18, you can setup the option to send an immediate email notification to specific people about all newly discovered risks.
To setup email notifications for risks:
Navigate to the Cloud and SaaS Security Posture Management page in Cynet.
Set the filters according to your preferences (or leave filters empty to show all risks).
Click Save Filter .
Enter a name for your filter.
To send notifications for newly discovered risks, mark the checkbox for Send email for newly discovered risks .
To send an email notification once every 24 hours containing all risks for a set filter, mark the checkbox for Send email with results every 24 hours .
Enter the email addresses that you want to receive notifications.
Click Save Filter .
