title: Top Risks Reports
content:
The Top Risks Report displays reports for the files, hosts, users, and network traffic with the highest risk levels.
The 10 Most Risky Files chart displays the riskiest objects based on the selected type.
The bottom part of this report displays the riskiest objects and additional details.
To generate a Top Risks report:
In the Cynet toolbar, click Reports > View Reports .
Select Top Risks Report from the list.
Select a tab at the top of the screen, either Files , Hosts , Users or Networks .
The report is displayed on the screen and can be downloaded to PDF by clicking the PDF icon in the top-right corner.

title: IT Hygiene Assessment Reports
content:
IT Hygiene Assessment reports (formerly Vulnerability Assessment reports or VA reports) produce operational data generated by the system based on the VA configuration .
There are four types of IT Hygiene reports:
Missing KBs on Host (Microsoft OS patch)
Agent Validation (Security policy compliancy)
Application Patch Validations (3rd party application patch validation, for example, Java, Adobe)
Unauthorized Applications
Each report is generated and downloaded as a CSV file which can be sent to the IT team for resolution.
To generate an IT Hygiene Assessment report:
Select IT Hygiene Assessment Report from the reports list.
Click on the report to generate.

title: Inventory Reports
content:
Inventory Reports provide operational data generated by the system based on the Cynet collection. The Inventory report lists all the hosts that have ever communicated with the Cynet server, regardless of the time of the last heartbeat or if the EPS was uninstalled. Inventory reports include information on Windows hosts only.
Typical use cases are:
Creating and maintaining Configuration Management Database (CMDB)
Discovering hosts with old or non-supported OS
Memory consumption data is provided by the operating system and collected from the EPS every five minutes. The data includes the total memory available on the host and the percentage of it that is currently in use.
To generate an Inventory report:
In the Cynet toolbar, click Reports > View Reports .
Select the Inventory Report from the dropdown list.
Click Inventory Per Host .
A CSV file is generated with the report.

title: Cloud & SaaS Security Posture Management
content:
The Cloud & SaaS Security Posture Management (CSPM & SSPM) periodically scans your SaaS applications and cloud platforms to detect misconfigurations and risks. The detected issues are based on Cynet's predefined set of configuration rules. The CSPM & SSPM interface also enables you to easily fix these issues by applying Cynet's recommended secure configuration.
CSPM is available to SaaS customers only.
After a 14-day free trial, CSPM is sold under a separate license.
Before using CSPM & SSPM, you must configure the integration with third-party services . Once Cynet 360 connects successfully to the third-party service, it immediately scans the service for misconfigurations. Following the initial scan, Cynet 360 performs a scan every several hours or when you click the Refresh button (see below). If you use Groups to manage different customers, you can manage integrations with different services by Group. In this case, a Group filter appears at the top of the SSPM page and you must select the Group for which to display data.
To access CSPM & SSPM and view configuration issues and risks, in the side navigation panel, click . See detailed information on viewing and fixing risks and CSPM/SSPM user risks .
You can work on several SSPM misconfigurations in parallel, by taking multiple long-running actions (e.g., fix, ignore, unfix) on several risks. Once a misconfiguration is resolved, you do not have to wait until it's completed to be able to do anything else on the page.

Supported Services

Click here for supported services for CSPM & SSPM and information on connecting these services.

Compliance Standards

The various CSPM & SSPM rules identify security risks that are in accordance with the following compliance standards:
APRA
CAF
CIS
Cyber Essentials
FFIEC
GDPR
HIPAA
ISO27001
MAS
NIS2
NIST
PCI

Detecting and Remediating Attacks on Your Cloud

In addition to our CSPM and SSPM capabilities, we also provide threat detection and remediation against attacks that could compromise your SaaS or Cloud, such as suspicious login attempts.
For more information about threat detection and remediation, see Remediating Alerts and XDR Alerts .

title: Integrating Cynet 360 with 3rd-Party Applications
content:
You can integrate Cynet 360 with 3rd-party SaaS applications, which enables features such as SSPM , CLM , and SOAR .
If you use Groups to manage different customers, you can connect applications for each Group. To manage integrations for different Groups, activate the toggle, select a Group from the dropdown list, then connect the relevant application.
To connect an application:
Navigate to Settings > Integrations .
Click on the application tile.
Turn on the toggles for the services you want to connect to.
When prompted, enter your username and password for the SaaS application.
Security best practice is to provide credentials of a user that has the lowest possible privileges, but can manage configuration. Some cloud/SaaS applications have the appropriate roles built-in (e.g, AWS_ConfigRole policy for AWS).
Cynet does not store the credentials, only receives an access token from the application. A green icon indicates that the connection is successful. The icon turns red when the access token expires or if the connection fails.
To disconnect the application or view connection settings, click on the application tile.

title: Centralized Log Management
content:
Cynet Centralized Log Management (CLM) displays data and logs collected by Cynet from firewalls, endpoints, and applications and stores them for forensic investigations and incident response. Data can also be collected from mobile endpoints and the mobile management console.
By default, the data is stored for 14 days and can be extended to 90 days for additional cost. Long-term storage is available with some limitations. See Long-Term Storage for CLM .
Log Management is available to SaaS customers only, under a separate license. On-premise support will be added in future versions.
Customers using Cynet 360 Mobile can send logs from the mobile application to CLM.

Data Sources

To collect information, you must install Logstash on-premise or configure webhooks . The data is transferred from your network to Cynet via https and stored in Open Distro data lake (by Elasticsearch). Within your network, you can configure to transfer data from the data source to Logstash via TCP, rather than UDP. Contact Cynet for details. Once you configure the data transfer, you will see events in the Forensic page. Learn more about viewing CLM data in the Cynet 360 console .
Your Technical Account Manager can provide information on the amount of data that was sent to Cynet CLM each month, per data source.
The data sources below are certified data sources for Cynet CLM. For each data source, you must open a designated communication port between the data source and the Logstash server, and set the required log format. Communication can be via TCP (recommended) or UDP.
By default, Windows Events and File Monitoring data is displayed in Log Management, with no required license or configuration. This data also appears in the Forensic page. By purchasing a CLM license you extend the default retention period of this data.
For more information on setting up the different data sources to send logs to CLM, see How to Send Logs to Cynet's CLM .
No change required for customers using an external SIEM: Customers that have configure Cynet 360 to send information to an external SIEM, can continue to do so using the Cynet SIEM API.

Operational Notifications for CLM

Cynet sends email notifications to customers about Operational events requiring their attention which are not security incidents (for which alerts are already generated).
CLM customers receive an email notification when external data sources stop sending data to CLM, which may indicate a configuration or networking issue. These notifications are only sent when the data source stops sending data for a significant length of time (by default, more than 12 hours). Once the data ingestion restarts, an additional email notification is sent.

title: Fortigate - Block IP
content:
Use the Fortigate Block IP remediation action to block a source/destination IP address in your organization. This procedure was verified on Fortigate version 6.4.6.
In the current Beta version, the SOAR remediation actions are provided as scripts, per customer request. For more information, contact Cynet.
You must store your Fortigate credentials in the Security Vault before creating a new Custom Remediation action. In the newly created vault object:
For name and key enter: Fortigate Account
Activate the vault object and enable agent remediation
Add the following variables: Name: Credentials Key: Credentials Value: Your Fortigate credential in the format: <IP>:<PORT>, <USER>, <PASSWORD>
Name: Credentials
Key: Credentials
Value: Your Fortigate credential in the format: <IP>:<PORT>, <USER>, <PASSWORD>
Now you must create a new custom remediation action to run on an execution server connected to Fortigate using the following parameters:
You must enter all the arguments, in the order in which they appear

title: SOAR
content:
Cynet's SOAR (Security Orchestration, Automation, and Response) streamlines security operations by automating threat detection, investigation, and response, reducing response times from hours to minutes.
Customers benefit from enhanced efficiency, minimized alert fatigue, and improved threat management without the need for extensive security teams.
With playbooks and integrations, Cynet ensures a proactive and cost-effective approach to cybersecurity, allowing businesses to focus on growth while staying protected.
Cynet recommends running SOAR remediation actions on execution servers , rather than on an endpoint, as these are highly available and secure.
SOAR remediation actions are available to SaaS customers only.
In the current Beta version, the SOAR remediation actions are provided as scripts, per customer request. For more information, contact Cynet Support .

Cynet recommends running SOAR remediation actions on execution servers , rather than on an endpoint, as these are highly available and secure.
SOAR remediation actions are available to SaaS customers only.
In the current Beta version, the SOAR remediation actions are provided as scripts, per customer request. For more information, contact Cynet Support .

title: Assessing Endpoint Vulnerability
content:
Cynet searches for vulnerabilities regarding the operating system and application installed on the endpoint. Under Settings > IT Hygiene , you can configure the following functions:
Windows Patch Validation
Unauthorized Applications
Application Path Validation
Agent Validation
The default scan interval is 1440 minutes (24 hours). You can view Vulnerability Assessment results in several places in the console. See details below.

Windows Patch Validation

The Cynet agent queries the Windows Update subsystem on the endpoint and maps all the available Knowledge Bases (KBs) that are ready to be installed.
You can allowlist Windows KB updates, which are known to be missing or uninstalled. To allowlist Windows KBs, type the name of the KB. Each KB displays in a new line.

Unauthorized Applications

The Cynet agent compares the list of programs on the endpoint to the list of applications that are unauthorized to run on the endpoint, and reports if any unauthorized application is installed at the endpoint.
You can add or remove applications from the Unauthorized Applications list. The list includes applications that Cynet has classified as potentially restricted.
To add an application to the Unauthorized Applications list, add the name of the application in a new line. Enter the name of the application exactly as it is displayed in Add/Remove Programs in Windows.

Application Patch Validation

The Cynet agent verifies that the latest patched version of the specified application is installed on the endpoint.
For example, if the organization’s security team defines that an Internet Explorer version below 11 is considered vulnerable, Cynet scans all the endpoints in the organization and reports of all the endpoints containing an Internet Explorer version below version 11.
You can add or remove applications that must be installed with a minimum version. Enter the name of the application exactly as it is displayed in Add/Remove Programs in Windows.

Agent Validation

You can insert a list of third-party applications and processes, which are defined as crucial applications on the endpoint. The Cynet scanner queries the Windows task manager and Add/Remove programs to verify that the third-party application is installed and running.
Cynet scans all the endpoints in the organization to check for:
Applications installed but not running.
Applications not installed and not running.
You can add or remove applications from the important third-party applications list. When adding applications to the list:
Enter the name of the application as it is displayed in Windows Add/Remove Programs.
Enter the process of the application displayed in Windows Task Manager.

Viewing IT Hygiene Data

There are several ways in which you can view Vulnerability Assessment results:
Generate an IT Hygiene Report via the Reports section.
View vulnerabilities per host via the Forensic section. The Vulnerability Assessment tab appears only if there is relevant data to display.
Add IT hygiene information to the Hosts Forensic display , via the Advanced Search.

title: UBA Rules
content:
Using the Cynet's User Behavior Analytics (UBA) rules, you can create and customize alerts for different triggers, such as host or user related activities. Create a query to run on the Cynet database, every set interval, to find suspicious behavior.
The UBA management includes an SMS verification option. Cynet SMS verification messages are sent from the phone number +1 (646) 846-8440 (a different phone number may appear in certain countries and networks outside the US)/ +44 7449 854440 (UK)/ +39 33 99958442 (Italy) , and are similar to the following examples:
You must make sure user data includes a mobile phone number before configuring SMS verification. See Importing User Data .
The UBA Management enables you to:
Edit a predefined UBA event trigger
Enable/ disable event triggers
Create a new UBA event trigger
UBA rules are based on SQL queries and required knowledge of the database.
To create a new UBA event trigger:
Navigate to Settings > UBA Management , then click Create .
Fill in the following parameters: Name : Name of the rule. This name is also the name of the alert that is triggered if the rule is matched. Description: You can include variables in the rule description by using {key}, for example: {FileName}, {HostID}. Query String: The SQL query that will run on the Cynet DB. To create or customize an SQL query, contact Cynet Support. Keys: Enter unique identifiers that will appear in the verification SMS sent to the user. Verification by: Select the checkbox to enable the SMS verification option: When an unusual event occur, Cynet alerts the user via SMS and requests the user to confirm or decline the action. If the user declines the action, Cynet 360 generates an alert. If you enable SMS verification, you must enter the question that will be presented to the user via SMS. This question can include the keys from the previous field. Severity level: The severity level of the triggered alert. Time interval in minutes: The time interval in minutes for executing the UBA query.

This question can include the keys from the previous field.
Severity level: The severity level of the triggered alert.
Time interval in minutes: The time interval in minutes for executing the UBA query.
Click Save . The new rule is added to the UBA Management.

title: Sending Syslog to 3rd-Party SIEM
content:
You can configure the Cynet server to send alerts and audit logs via syslog to a 3rd party SIEM.
Cynet can send syslog to an external SIEM server using one of the following transmission methods:
UDP
TCP: Use this method for testing purposes
When using TCP, you can also enable a secure connection: Cynet uses external SSL encryption between the Cynet server and the SIEM server.
Global operators can send syslog that includes information on all sites, or configure integration for each site separately. Sending syslog for each site can be configured from the global Settings page, or from each site, as detailed below.
To send syslog to an external SIEM:
Navigate to Settings > Configuration > SIEM Settings .
Select a transmission method: UDP or TCP.
If you selected TCP, you can enable Secure Connection .
Enter the IP address of the SIEM server. The default port is 514. If you enter a port other than 514, you must restart the Cynet services on the server to apply the change.
Click Add . The server is added to the list of configured SIEM servers.
Navigate to Settings > Advanced .
Select the Send Audit Records to SIEM check box. The external SIEM integration is set up.
If your organization runs your SIEM solution behind a firewall, add the following IPs to the allowed list of IPs that can communicate with the SIEM solution: EU customers: 18.200.154.75 US customers: 18.210.90.90
EU customers: 18.200.154.75
US customers: 18.210.90.90
Note: In addition to the general alerts, if you want to send Audit Logs and non-Endpoint alerts via Syslog, please contact Cynet Support to receive additional IPs to add.

title: Global Views
content:
A global operator can access summarized data for alerts and forensic artifacts from the Global Manager in all dashboards. Each dashboard provides different aspects of visibility towards the environment.

Alerts

The global Alerts view displays all alerts triggered in environments across all sites. Each alert is presented as a single-site alert is displayed, with additional information regarding the site displayed on the right.

Forensic

The global Forensic view displays the various forensic artifacts collected by Cynet, divided into files, hosts, users, and domains. The information collected holds metadata to provide a quick check across the environment. Clicking a forensic object directs you to the full forensic data in the specific site.

Audit

The Global Manager server collects auditing information, which includes access across all sites and performed activities. You can send this information to a third-party SIEM using Syslog .

Reports

The Global reports view lets you see all reports that are generated on the global level. You can schedule reports to be sent on a daily, weekly or monthly basis, and see a list of the scheduled reports. Reports can be scheduled on the global level or on the site level. On the site level, you can schedule a report to be sent for multiple sites at once.

title: Data Retention
content:
Cynet stores data according to data privacy requirements. Once a category’s retention period expires, the data is purged.
Cynet manages data retention according to categories. The General category is the default data category in the system, unless otherwise specified below. It also includes the retention history and purge results from the last few months.
The default amount of storage per month is 15GB.
If you have a license for All-in-One, this includes CLM with the default of 14 days retention period.
* If you have a license for Cynet CLM , data retention for these categories is determined by the license type.
The retention periods for on-premise customers can be adjusted according to free disk space on their DB. For more information, contact Cynet Support.

title: File Filtering
content:
*Deprecated in version 4.5*
File Filtering is a detection mechanism that enables you to protect selected files and folders from ransomware attacks. Using File Filtering you can allow only specific applications to read, write, create, or modify specific file types saved on the Desktop or in the Documents folder. This prevents other processes, such as ransomware processes, from encrypting files. When an unauthorized application tries to access a protected file type, Cynet 360 triggers an Attempt to modify Protected File alert and can also block the process.
To configure File Filtering:
Navigate to Settings > Profiles > File Filtering . The list of saved profiles is displayed in which you can edit or delete existing profiles.
The list of saved profiles is displayed in which you can edit or delete existing profiles.
In the top-right corner, click Create .
Enter a profile name.
Select to if to block applications that try to access the specified files, or allow access but trigger an alert.
Select the severity of the triggered alert.
Select if to filter files saved on the desktop, in Documents, or both.
Select the check boxes of the file types you want to protect and the applications that you trust to access these file types. If there are file extensions or applications that do not appear in the list, you can add them manually.
Under Silent Block Applications you can add applications for Cynet 360 to block from accessing the selected file type, without triggering an alert.
Click Save . The File Filtering profile is saved.
The File Filtering profile is saved.
Navigate to Settings > Scan Groups .
Select the Scan Group for which you want to enable File Filtering.
Select the File Filtering Ransomware Protection check box and and select a File Filtering profile.
Click Save .

title: Global Security Settings
content:
The Global Manager provides central administration to all the sites enabling a quick way to set the security policy for all the sites. Operators can establish a global policy to enforce a template of all of the sites or modify the configuration per-site via the Central Groups tab.
Selecting a feature from the list of Cynet capabilities present various options of the feature as check boxes that you can enforce globally or per scan group on each site.
Each site connected to the Global Manager provides a list of all the scan groups and their settings updated to the current configuration on the site. This enables users to see the settings applied and configure them from a single dashboard.

title: Fortigate - Unblock IP
content:
Use the Fortigate Unblock IP remediation action to unblock a destination IP address in your organization that was previously blocked by Cynet 360 . This procedure was verified on Fortigate version 6.4.6.
In the current Beta version, the SOAR remediation actions are provided as scripts, per customer request. For more information, contact Cynet.
You must store your Fortigate credentials in the Security Vault before creating a new Custom Remediation action. In the newly created vault object:
For name and key enter: Fortigate Account
Activate the vault object and enable agent remediation
Add the following variables: Name: Credentials Key: Credentials Value: Your Fortigate credential in the format: <IP>:<PORT>, <USER>, <PASSWORD>
Name: Credentials
Key: Credentials
Value: Your Fortigate credential in the format: <IP>:<PORT>, <USER>, <PASSWORD>
Now you must create a new custom remediation action to run on an execution server connected to Fortigate using the following parameters:
You must enter all the arguments, in the order in which they appear

title: SaaS Communication Requirements
content:
Internet Connectivity

The endpoint must be connected to the internet. Typical bandwidth consumption is 10MB a day per EPS.

Firewall Configuration

Note that the top part of the table applies to all customers, while the bottom part of the table differs between EU and US customers.
The Cynet agent does not support communication via HTTP/S proxy. A Traffic Router can be used to enable a firewall between the organization's endpoints and the Cynet cloud. For more information, contact Cynet Support.

title: Excluding Cynet from 3rd-Party AV
content:
This article lists the Cynet files, paths, and processes that you must exclude from a 3rd-party antivirus running simultaneously with the Cynet agent.
Performance and deployment issues may arise if a 3rd-party antivirus detects Cynet processes as malicious and blocks them. Best practice is to remove any 3rd-party antivirus before installing Cynet 360.

Cynet 360 Server

Exclude the entire Cynet360 directory.

Windows Endpoints

%windir%\CynetLauncher.exe
%windir%\CynetEPS.exe
%windir%\CynetMS.exe
%windir%\CynetRunner.exe
%windir%\CynetRunner64.exe
%windir%\CynetConsole.exe
%programdata%\cynet\AVCynetBroker.exe
%windir%\System32\drivers\cdrv.sys
%windir%\System32\drivers\CyNetDrv.sys
%programdata%\cynet
C:\ProgramData\Cynet\Data\PDScripts\*
%programdata%\Cynet\Data\PDscripts\*
% ProgramData%\Cynet\CynetUiApp\CynetUiApp.exe
%windir%\Temp\*\CynetRS*.exe

Linux Endpoints

/tmp/CynetEPSArguments.txt
/opt/Cynet
In the folder in which the installation package is unzipped:
cyneteps.rpm
cyneteps.deb
cy_uninstall.sh
CynetEPSArguments.txt

Mac Endpoints

/opt/Cynet
In the folder in which the installation package is unzipped:
CynetEPSArguments.txt
cyneteps_service.pkg
cy_uninstall.sh

title: Hardware Requirements for Agent Installation
content:
* Sysdig reserves one core of the CPU for its function
Note: The operating system needs to have SHA-2 code signing support for the agent to work properly. If your OS does not natively support this algorithm (specifically, Windows Server 2008 and Windows 7), you must install the following KBs so that the OS can verify Cynet's digital signatures:
KB4474419 – Updates Windows to support SHA-2 code signing.
KB4490628 – Updates the servicing stack to handle SHA-2 updates properly.
Important! Hosts that do not meet the recommended hardware requirements will not be able to run certain features, including CyAI, Rollback, Endpoint Misconfiguration Checks, Domain Filtering, Web Content Filtering, Identity Protection, and possibly others.

title: Installation Guide for Linux Components
content:

title: Installing the Cynet Agent on VDI
content:
You can deploy the Cynet agent in persistent or non-persistent virtual environments. The Cynet agent supports VDI environments that operate with Windows 10 and Windows 11.
Cynet identifies endpoints by the HD serial number and hostname. As long as the HD serial number and hostname remain unchanged, Cynet considers the VDI instance as the same endpoint (consumes one license, listed as one endpoint in the Forensic page, etc.).
When creating a Golden Image, install the Cynet agent using MSI installer with the flag -vdi=<int> as an argument * , for example:
1 = Persistent desktop
2 = Non-persistent desktop
* Available from V4.6
To easily manage VDI hosts, It is recommended to associate these hosts to a designated Scan Group.
To avoid overloading the system during restart, Cynet’s cached data folder %programdata%\cynet\data must be persistent.

content:
If the need arises, you can exclude paths and folders of certain operating system components. Latency and performance issues may arise when using the components listed below simultaneously with the Cynet EPS.

SCCM

%Windir%\CCM
C:\Windows\CCMCache
C:\Windows\CCMSetup

Windows Updates and Upgrades

C:\Windows\CbsTemp\
C:\$WINDOWS.~BT\

title: Traffic Router
content:
A Traffic Router (TR) is an optional component you can use when deploying Cynet in your environment. It is required when you must enforce network separation between components or when a DMZ is available to accept external traffic.
Cynet uses a TR as a bridge and forwards traffic from Cynet agents to their Cynet server.
The main use cases of Traffic Routers are:
An on-premise environment where the Cynet Server is not connected to the internet. All the traffic from the endpoints, that are connected to the internet, goes to the TR and from there to the Cynet Server. The above use case can be implemented to any scenario in which a section of the environment must be air-gapped from the rest of the environment.
A SaaS customer whose endpoints are connected to the internet via proxy. In this case, all the endpoint traffic goes to the TR and from there to the Cynet Cloud.
For the TR installation guide, click here .

Limitations

A TR cannot be installed on an existing proxy. The TR does not support proxy chaining and requires direct outbound access.
Decoy files and remote shell sessions are not supported when using a TR.

Architecture

The following diagrams describe the two use cases for a Traffic Router in environments in which Cynet 360 is deployed.

title: System Requirements for a Traffic Router
content:
This article lists the hardware and firewall requirements when combining a Traffic Router in your organization's Cynet 360 deployment.

Hardware Requirements

16
Cynet supports a docker based topology that eliminates the need to setup a server for every new customer. This supports very large deployments by having multiple docker servers.
When working with more than one Traffic Router, the Traffic Router’s load must be balanced by the customer’s Load Balancer

Supported Operating Systems

Traffic Router is supported on the following operating systems:
Linux: See supported versions here .
Windows Server 2019 and above - Windows support is currently in beta for customers with fewer than 1,000 endpoints.

title: System Requirements for On-Premise Global/Site Architecture
content:
This article lists the requirements for installing Cynet 360 with global/site architecture. This architecture requires a global server and a separate Linux/Window server for OpenSearch installation.
It is recommended to restrict network access to the Cynet server to authorized personnel only.

Global Server

The global server and the sites must be set to the same time zone.
The following hardware requirements are tailored for a physical or virtual server.
4 cores
Intel x64 compatible
24GB RAM
300GB HDD SSD
The following applications must be installed before installing the Cynet 360 server. These applications are not included in the Cynet 360 installation package, but can be found in the Windows Server installation or downloaded from the Microsoft website:
.Net Framework 4.8
Internet Information Services (IIS)
TCP/135 TCP/49152-65535 (For older windows versions: TCP/1024–5000)
1 If deployment is performed using MSI, this port is not required 2 If the Cynet server is unavailable, the agent communicates directly with sav.cynet.com.
The Cynet agent does not support communication via HTTPs proxy. A Traffic Router can be used to enable a firewall between the organization's endpoints and the Cynet server. For more information, contact Cynet Support.
The Cynet 360 server supports the following Windows operating systems (64 bit only):
Windows Server 2016
Windows Server 2019
Windows Server 2022
The base language of the operating system must be English.

HDD Performance Requirements

Backup storage requirements are not included.
Maximum single server capacity is 20k endpoints.

OpenSearch Server

The following hardware requirements are tailored for a physical or virtual server. The number of endpoints is per one server as additional (non-OS) storage.
5,000 - 10,000
>10k
Contact your Sales representative
It is highly recommended to install OpenSearch on a Linux server (ubuntu or centos). The supported OpenSearch version is 1.* (click here to download).

Additional Requirements

User account with local administrative permissions on the scanned nodes and read permissions to AD.
ADMIN$ share (administrative share) for Cynet EPS deployment.
C$ share for decoy file deployment.
Enable SMB on the Cynet server. The SMB version must be the same as enabled on the endpoints.

title: How to Download an Agent Installation File
content:
Use these instructions to obtain the Cynet installation package or generate an Group-specific installation file.

Cynet Installation Package

The Cynet installation package includes installation files for all operating systems. When using this method, the Cynet agent is installed on the endpoint and added to the default Group - Manually Installed Agents.
Note: To successfully generate and download the file, the logged-in user must have "operator" or higher permissions.
To download the installation file (available only to SaaS customers), from the bottom of the left navigation pane, click Install. Select Cynet Package and extract the downloaded ZIP folder.

Group-Specific Installation File (On-Prem Only)

Before creating the Group-specific file, it is recommended to check the exact name of the Group in the Group Settings page.
To generate a Group-specific installation package/MSI file:
Navigate to the Scanner page and click Generate EPS. A dialog box opens.
From the OS dropdown list, select the required operating system.
Enter the name of the Group to which the endpoint must be added.
Click Generate. A folder/MSI file is downloaded to your computer.

Group-Specific Installation File (SaaS Only)

To generate a Group-specific installation package/MSI file for SaaS customers:
Navigate to the Settings > Groups page.
Click on the three dots icon on the right-hand side of the row of the group.
Click Download Cynet Package . The folder/MSI file is downloaded to your computer.

title: Hardware Requirements for Analytics Server
content:
The Cynet Analytics server is based on OpenSearch and requires the following:
Linux Server ( RHEL 8.x, 9.x or Ubuntu 22.04 )
64GB memory
8 CPU
2TB HDD SSD additional (non OS) storage
Typically, 10,000 endpoints require one Analytics server, but this may vary between environments. For information specific to your environment, contact Cynet Support.
For information on how to set up the Analytics server in the Cynet 360 console, click here .

title: Multiple Endpoint Installation for Mac with MDM
content:
The Cynet Installer for Mac Device Management (MDM) Integration on Mac includes a pre-installation script which creates the CynetEPSArguments.txt file in the tmp folder as the first step of the installation. After this, the MDM deploys the uploaded compliant package.

Intune

There are two supported ways to deploy Full Disk Access (FDA) for Cynet EPS on macOS with Microsoft Intune. Installing via the Settings Catalog is the recommended approach because it is easier to maintain in Intune and clearly maps to Apple's Privacy Preferences Policy Control (PPPC) service System Policy All Files . Installing via a Custom Profile is also valid when you prefer to upload a ready-made .mobileconfig that combines FDA with Service Management rules.
Both options assume that /opt/Cynet/CynetAV is not required as a separate PPPC entry and that the approved identity is the app bundle com.cynet.cynetepsapp .
Key values used in both options:
Bundle ID: com.cynet.cynetepsapp
Identifier Type: bundleID (for the app bundle)
Allowed: True
Static Code: False / Not configured
Code Requirement: exact designated requirement from codesign -dr - /Applications/CynetEPS.app/Contents/MacOS/CynetEPS
Use the Intune Settings Catalog to configure Apple PPPC > Services > System Policy All Files for the Cynet app bundle.
1. Go to Devices > macOS > Configuration Profiles .
2. Full Disk access: Create a New Policy .
3. Select Platform: macOS and Profile Type: Settings Catalog .
4. Add Privacy Preferences Policy Control > Services > System Policy All Files .
Create an entry with the following entry values:
Name : CynetEPS
Allowed: True
Identifier Type : Bundle ID
Identifier : com.cynet.cynetepsapp
Static Code: False / Not configured
Code Requirement: anchor apple generic and identifier "com.cynet.cynetepsapp" and (certificate leaf[field.1.2.840.113635.100.6.1.9] /* exists */ or certificate 1[field.1.2.840.113635.100.6.2.6] /* exists */ and certificate leaf[field.1.2.840.113635.100.6.1.13] /* exists */ and certificate leaf[subject.OU] = "895URTZF36")
5. Click Save .
6. Add a new MacOS app from the Microsoft Intune admin center.
7. Select macOS app (PKG) as the app type.
8. Upload Cynet's PKG installation file:
9. In the Program tab, set the Pre-install script (copy the content of “mdm_pre_install.sh” from package):
10. In the Requirements tab, select the Minimum OS requirements:
11. In the Detection rules tab, configure the settings as shown in the following screenshot:
The outcome should be as shown in the following screenshot:
Note: When installing CynetEPS by MDM, you will not see Full Disk Access by the end user in the macOS GUI. You can test it after few minutes or after restarting the computer by downloading EICAR zip or another test file.
Use a custom Apple configuration profile when you want to upload a ready-made PPPC payload, optionally bundled together with a Service Management payload.
PPPC / TCC payload type: com.apple.TCC.configuration-profile-policy
FDA Service: SystemPolicyAllFiles
App identity: com.cynet.cynetepsapp with IdentifierType=bundleID
Service Management payload can be included in the same profile if background item approval is required.
You can download the following custom profile, and edit it to replace all instances of the string Replace with UUID with your own UUID. Cynet_EPS_Allow_FDA 2.mobileconfig

Hexnode

1. Browse to Apps .
2. Click +add Apps > Enterprise App .
3. Choose the macOS icon.
4. Give the app a name in the App Name field.
5. Upload with the PKG/MPKG/DMG file.
6. Choose a category and description .
7. Choose the PKG file you downloaded and add it.
8. On the EPS App under Required Apps , click Scripts > Configure .
9. In the pre-install script, attach the “argsScript_unix.sh” file in the installation download.
10. Go to Policies > New Policy > New Black Policy .
11. Give it a name and choose macOS .
12. Go to Required Apps from the left menu.
13. Click Configure > +Add > Add App .
14. Choose the app you added.
15. Full Disk Access: Go to Security from the left menu.
16. Click Privacy Preferences > + Add new preference .
17. Click All Files and Allow , and click Select Apps .
For this step, you have two options:
a. If you already installed Cynet’s EPS on one computer with Hexnode, you will have the option to select Cynet EPS.
b. If you did not install it, go to Specify BundleIDs/Path.
Identifier Type : Path
Identifier* : /Applications/CynetEPS
Code Requirement* : identifier CynetEPS and anchor apple generic and certificate 1[field.1.2.840.113635.100.6.2.6] /* exists */ and certificate leaf[field.1.2.840.113635.100.6.1.13] /* exists */ and certificate leaf[subject.OU] = "895URTZF36"
Click Add .
18. Click Save .

title: Single Endpoint Installation - Linux Unified Installer
content:
The Cynet agent can be easily installed - with all of the required dependencies - on a Linux endpoint using an installation script. In addition to the general and operating system requirements , there are several Linux components that are required for Cynet 360 operation and features. See the details below.
Note that some of these components are interchangeable.
Note: Cynet agent installation or upgrade does not require a system reboot.
Note: To install a version of Cynet lower than v4.28 for SaaS or lower than v4.27 for On-Prem, refer to the instructions in Single Endpoint Installation - Linux.
Follow these instructions to obtain the installation files.
Transfer the EPS_LINUX_*.zip file to the host on which you want to install the agent.
Using elevated context ( sudo or root ), extract the files from the EPS_LINUX_*.zip file to a local folder on the host machine.
In the extracted files, there is another zipped file called cy_easyinstaller.zip . Unzip this file into the same folder as the other files. Note : For more information on the supported script distributions, see Supported Operating Systems .
The extracted archive should contain the following files: cyneteps.deb cyneteps.rpm CynetEPSArguments.txt cy_easyinstall.sh cy_easyinstall cy_uninstall.sh sysdig.deb sysdig.rpm test_chisel.lua
cyneteps.deb
cyneteps.rpm
CynetEPSArguments.txt
cy_easyinstall.sh
cy_easyinstall
cy_uninstall.sh
sysdig.deb
sysdig.rpm
test_chisel.lua
Using elevated context ( sudo or root ), execute sudo ./cy_easyinstall The installation script will generate a log file that will be written to /opt/Cynet/EngineInstallationInfo.txt
Using elevated context ( sudo or root ), execute sudo ./cy_easyinstall
The installation script will generate a log file that will be written to /opt/Cynet/EngineInstallationInfo.txt
Once the installation is complete, a summary appears in the terminal with all the details of what was installed, what modes were used, etc.
Note: Depending on the specific configuration of the host, Secure Boot may interfere with the CynetEPS installation and require manual modifications. For more details, contact your support team.

Verifying the Cynet EPS Installation Verify that CynetEPS is running: pgrep CynetEPS This should output the CynetEPS process ID. If not, it means that CynetEPS is not running properly.

Verify that CynetEPS is running:
This should output the CynetEPS process ID. If not, it means that CynetEPS is not running properly.

Verify that the service is healthy: systemctl status cyservice.service The service should be in a Running state. Verify that sysdig is running (if sysdig was selected with the installation script):

Verify that the service is healthy:
The service should be in a Running state. Verify that sysdig is running (if sysdig was selected with the installation script):
This should output the sysdig process ID. If not, it means that sysdig is not running properly.

sections.heading: Verify that the service is healthy: systemctl status cyservice.service The service should be in a Running state. Verify that sysdig is running (if sysdig was selected with the installation script):
sections.text:
Verify that the service is healthy:
The service should be in a Running state. Verify that sysdig is running (if sysdig was selected with the installation script):
This should output the sysdig process ID. If not, it means that sysdig is not running properly.

title: Multiple Endpoint Installation for Windows with Intune
content:
This guide explains how to deploy the Cynet agent to Windows endpoints using Microsoft Intune (Microsoft Endpoint Manager) .
The procedure supports automated rollout, simplified scaling, and central management for Windows 10/11 endpoints.

Prerequisites

Before starting, ensure:
You have access to the Microsoft Intune Admin Center with permissions to add and assign applications.
You have the Cynet MSI installer , downloaded from the Cynet Management Console (see Step 1 below) .
Windows devices are: Azure AD Joined / Hybrid joined Managed by Intune Running Windows 10/11 versions that are supported by Cynet (see Supported Operating Systems ).
Azure AD Joined / Hybrid joined
Managed by Intune
Running Windows 10/11 versions that are supported by Cynet (see Supported Operating Systems ).

Step 1: Download the MSI from the Cynet Console

In the Cynet console, navigate to Settings > Groups .
Select the Group to which you want new endpoints to be added (the Group must be configured for Windows).
Click the menu icon on the right-hand side of the group.
Click Download Cynet Package .

Step 2: Upload the MSI to Intune

Sign into the Microsoft Intune Admin Center: https://intune.microsoft.com/
In the left-hand menu, select Apps > Windows > Add .
Under Select app type , choose Line-of-business app .
Click Select , then upload your Cynet EPS MSI file.
Intune will automatically read the MSI metadata:
App name
Version
Publisher
Product code

Step 3: Configure Application Information

Fill in or adjust the application metadata as follows, then click Next :
Name: CynetEPS
Description: Cynet agent for Windows. Provides real-time protection, behavioral monitoring, anti-ransomware, and threat detection.
Publisher: Cynet
Category (Optional): Computer management
Logo (Optional): Upload the Cynet logo

Step 4: Program Settings

For MSI packages, Intune automatically generates the installation command.
Recommended install command (default):
msiexec /i "CynetMSI.msi" /quiet
Uninstall command (default):
Automatically populated with the MSI Product Code:
msiexec /x "{PRODUCT-CODE-GUID}" /quiet
No further customization is required.
Click Next .

Step 5: Requirements

Recommended configuration:
Operating system architecture: 64-bit
Minimum operating system: Windows 10, or Windows 11 22H2 or later (see also Supported Operating Systems ).
Click Next .

Step 6: Detection Rules

No manual changes are needed.
Intune will detect installation status based on the MSI's Product Code .
Click Next .

Step 7: Dependencies (Optional)

Cynet does not require additional dependencies.
Leave empty and click Next .

Step 8: Assignments

1. Choose how the Cynet agent will be deployed:
Required
Assign to:
All Devices
or
Specific device groups (e.g., Windows Workstations , Servers , Laptops )
This ensures the agent installs automatically.
Available for Enrolled Devices (Optional)
Allows users to install manually from the Company Portal .
Uninstall
Use only if intentionally removing the agent from specific groups.
2. Click Next .

Step 9: Review and Create

Review the configuration and click Create .
Intune will begin distributing the installer based on your assignments.

Step 10: Verify Deployment

From Intune:
Go to Apps > Windows > Cynet EPS Agent > Device install status .
You should see: Installed Pending Failed (with error codes)
Installed
Pending
Failed (with error codes)
From the Windows Endpoint:
Check that Cynet appears in the Start Menu .
Check that CynetEPS appears in the Windows Services .
Check that the Cynet processes are running, such as CynetEPS.exe , CynetSensor.exe , etc.
Check that Cynet appears in the Applications and Services Logs in the Windows Event Viewer.
In the Cynet Console:
From the Cynet toolbar, navigate to Endpoints > Management .
Verify the device appears and its Status is OK .

Troubleshooting

Possible reasons:
MSI blocked by policy
Damaged file upload
Device not yet synced with Intune
Possible reasons:
Endpoint did not fully complete installation
Reboot pending
Network restrictions blocking Cynet cloud communication
Possible reasons:
Corrupted MSI Product Code entry
Re-upload the MSI and redeploy

title: System Requirements for On-Premise Server Installation
content:
This article lists the various requirements for installing a Cynet 360 server on-premise.
It is recommended to restrict network access to the Cynet server to authorized personnel only.

Hardware Requirements

The following hardware requirements are tailored for a physical or virtual server. The number of endpoints is per one server.
5,000 - 10,000
>10k
Contact your Sales representative

Software Requirements

The following applications must be installed before installing the Cynet 360 server. These applications are not included in the Cynet 360 installation package, but can be found in the Windows Server installation or downloaded from the Microsoft website:
.Net Framework 4.8
Internet Information Services (IIS)

Firewall Configuration

1 If deployment is performed using MSI, this port is not required 2 If the Cynet server is unavailable, the agent communicates directly with sav.cynet.com.
The Cynet agent does not support communication via HTTP/S proxy. A Traffic Router can be used to enable a firewall between the organization's endpoints and the Cynet server. For more information, contact Cynet Support.

Supported Operating Systems

The Cynet 360 server supports the following Windows operating systems (64 bit only):
Windows Server 2019
Windows Server 2022
The base language of the operating system must be English.

HDD Performance Requirements

Backup storage requirements are not included.
Maximum single server capacity is 15k endpoints.
Cynet provides a global architecture for environments with a higher number of endpoints. The architecture must be sized by a Cynet representative.

Additional Requirements

User account with local administrative permissions on the scanned nodes and read permissions to AD.
ADMIN$ share (administrative share) for Cynet EPS deployment.
C$ share for decoy file deployment.
Enable SMB on the Cynet server. The SMB version must be the same as enabled on the endpoints.

title: Supported Operating Systems
content:
This article lists the operating systems that the Cynet 360 agent supports, as well as the CISO kit and Cynet 360 mobile . For Cynet's Support Policy, click here .
Note: Cynet agent installation or upgrade does not require a system reboot.

Windows

Cynet supports the following Windows operating systems (32/64 bit):
Important! Windows Version 1607 (for Windows Server 2016 / Windows 10) is not supported due to known stability issues with security drivers.

Linux

Installation on Linux requires the following prerequisites:
SystemD
Glibc 2.17 or above
Note: In Linux installations on operating systems based on host special configuration, some capabilities like Secure Boot or SELinux/AppArmor with special configurations might interfere with the CynetEPS installation. Additionally, non-GA kernel versions are not officially supported.
Cynet supports the following operating systems for Linux (x64 architecture only):
For more information, see Single Endpoint Installation - Linux .

Mac

Cynet supports the following operating systems for Mac:
MacOS 12 and above.
For endpoints running versions of Cynet up to and including v4.31 , versions of MacOS between MacOS 10.15 and 12 are also supported.
Note: Supported processors include Intel based chips with 64bit architecture or Apple M series chips.
iOS 10.0 and above
iPadOS 10.0 and above
watchOS 4.3 and above

Cynet 360 Mobile

iOS 11.0 and above
Android 5.1 and above
Chrome OS that supports Android apps

Supported Browsers

Cynet supports all modern and chromium-based browsers.

title: SSL Pinning
content:
The SSL Pinning functionality strengthens the security of encrypted channels
 by pinning specific certificates between Cynet hosts and the server (on-prem
 or SaaS).
Certificates can be uploaded to the server, deployed to hosts and enforced
 in SSL communication.
By default, certificates are self-signed by Cynet.
Certificates can be customized.
Customers can approve or reject certificates for SSL communication.
To add and manage certificates for SSL pinning:
Navigate to Settings > Security .
Click Add and select a file to upload.
From the Status dropdown list, select a status.

title: Getting Started
content:
This article guides you though the steps to per form when using Cynet 360 for the first time.

Log In to the Console

Use the following links to log into the Cynet 360 console:
USA Clients
Europe Clients
MSP: Use the link you received from Cynet Support.
If you do not have login credentials, contact Cynet Support .

System Overview

The All-in-One Dashboard is the landing page when you enter the Cynet 360 console. Use the Dashboard to get a general overview of your network.

Deploy the Cynet EPS

Cynet includes an installation package for easy deployment of the EPS on all hosts in your network. Alternatively, you can install the EPS on a single host. From the Installation category in the Cynet Online Help, select an installation guide according to the operating system.
To obtain the installation package, from the Dashboard, click the Download icon at the top right hand side, then next to the Cynet Package, click Download . Follow the instructions in the Installation section of the Cynet Online Help.
Add hosts directly to Groups , using host names, IP, or Active Directory.

Check Connected Hosts

Navigate to the Endpoint Management page. The Endpoint Management page displays all the hosts that have sent a heartbeat, and their status. If there are hosts that do not appear in the Endpoint Management page, you must check their connection.
You can verify that a host is sending data by navigating to the Forensic page and selecting the host. You should see information on files, user logins, etc. related to the host.

Check Security Settings

Navigate to Settings > Groups .
Group settings include the various security features that Cynet provides. You can map the hosts in your network to different Groups and apply different security settings to each group. Cynet recommend using Best Practice configuration for your Groups.

Optimize your System

To optimize your work alongside the protection of the Cynet EPS and avoid performance issues, it is recommended to exclude certain processes from the EPS scan. See the article on how to create an exclusion profile as well as some recommended exclusions .

Start Investigating

Once you have set up Cynet 360, you can dive into alerts, forensic investigations, and more. To learn more, see How to Investigate an Alert and explore the different categories of the User Guide .

title: Chief Information Security Officer Kit
content:
The CISO kit integrates Cynet 360 with the Cynet mobile application, to enable CISOs to receive information and alerts to their mobile device.
The CISO kit is compatible with Apple devices, and allows connecting one device of each type (iPhone or iPad) per account. The CISO kit is available to users with Operator permissions, from Cynet 360 v3.6.2 and above. Click here for supported Apple operating systems.
If you have an Apple watch it will automatically connect to Cynet 360 when you connect your iPhone. If your Apple watch is connected to the internet it will receive updates independently of the iPhone.
Note: To enable the CISO Kit in your environment, please contact your Cynet Support Team .
To connect Cynet 360 with the Cynet application:
In the Cynet 360 console, navigate to Settings > Users .
Under CISO Kit , select the check box for iPhone or iPad. A QR code appears. The code is valid for one hour and can be used to connect a single device only.
A QR code appears. The code is valid for one hour and can be used to connect a single device only.
Using the device you want to connect, scan the QR code.
From the App Store, download Cynet Dashboard .
Open the application and tap the QR code.
Scan the QR code displayed in the Cynet 360 console. The Cynet Dashboard application connects to Cynet 360 and the dashboard is displayed on your mobile device.
The Cynet Dashboard application connects to Cynet 360 and the dashboard is displayed on your mobile device.

The Cynet Dashboard Application

The Cynet Dashboard application includes information about the protected hosts in your organization, as well as total number of alerts and their severity, and number of alerts per type.
Tap an alert type/severity to view the full list of alerts sorted by type/severity and perform actions on an alert. You can also filter the list of alerts by date and use a free text search. The application does not include all the alert information that exists in the Cynet 360 console.
Cynet Dashboard included the option to escalate an alert to CyOps. To do so, tap the alert, then tap Escalate to CyOps .

title: Network Installation Guide
content:
Use these instructions to install and deploy the Cynet EPS on your network. To install the EPS on a single host, see the Single Host Installation guides for each operating system. You can also use the CynetMSI file, found in the Cynet Installation Package, to install the Cynet EPS on your network using a Software Deployment Tool.
Note: To successfully generate and download the file, the logged-in user must have "operator" or higher permissions.
To deploy the Cynet EPS on a network:
Note: Before beginning installation with the Cynet Distribution Tool, ensure that the following ports are open on your firewall:
TCP/135
TCP/ 49152-65535
TCP/ 1024–5000 (for machines running older versions of Windows)
From the right-hand side of the top navigation bar, click the Download button.
Next to Cynet Distribution Tool , click Download . The installation file is downloaded to your computer.
Double-click the Cynet Scanner.
Select Network Deployment , then click Next . The distribution tool scans the network for computers in two methods: A Network Discovery Protocol Query of the AD domain (an AD scan does not reveal the computer from which the scan is running. If your computer is not discovered by the Network Discovery Protocol, you can add it manually). You can set the timeout of these scans by clicking Advanced Settings. The computers found during the scan are added to the list, separated according to the mechanism in which they were discovered and operating system.
A Network Discovery Protocol
Query of the AD domain (an AD scan does not reveal the computer from which the scan is running. If your computer is not discovered by the Network Discovery Protocol, you can add it manually). You can set the timeout of these scans by clicking Advanced Settings. The computers found during the scan are added to the list, separated according to the mechanism in which they were discovered and operating system.
You can set the timeout of these scans by clicking Advanced Settings.
The computers found during the scan are added to the list, separated according to the mechanism in which they were discovered and operating system.
If there are hosts missing from the list, you can add them manually via AD (1) or by IP address / host name (2) (host name is available for Windows only). computers added by IP address or host name are added to a Manually Added category. If you try to add a computer that already appears in the list, you will receive an error message.
If you want to automatically install the EPS on any host that joins the network, select the Continuous Deployment check box.
Click Next . The distribution tool installs the Cynet EPS on the list of computers. The status of the installation appears by every host.
The distribution tool installs the Cynet EPS on the list of computers. The status of the installation appears by every host.

title: Single Endpoint Installation - Mac
content:
The Cynet agent can be easily installed on a Mac endpoint using an installation wizard. Use the following instructions to obtain the installation package and install the Cynet agent.
Note: Cynet agent installation or upgrade does not require a system reboot.
A new Cynet Installer is now available for Mac Device Management (MDM) Integration on MAC. The installer includes a pre-installation script which creates the CynetEPSArguments.txt file in the tmp folder as the first step of the installation. After this, the MDM deploys the uploaded compliant package.
For installation instructions for the MDM integration, see Multiple Endpoint Installation Using MDM Integration .
Follow these instructions to obtain the agent installation package.
In the downloaded folder, double-click cyneteps_service.pkg and follow the instructions in the installation wizard.
Mac customers with Apple M2 chip: Before installing the Cynet agent, you need to install Rosetta on the endpoint.
If you receive an error during the installation, you can view the error details in /tmp/CynetInstall.log. A possible cause for an error could be a missing CynetEPSArguments.txt file.
Once the installation is complete, you are prompted to provide Cynet with full disk access.
Full disk access is necessary for full detection capabilities.
To enable full disk access:
Click the Apple icon, then navigate to System Preferences .
Navigate to Security & Privacy , then select the Privacy tab.
In the left navigation pane, click Full Disk Access .
In the bottom-left corner, click the lock icon.
Enter your system administrator credentials or use your touch ID.
Click + .
To show hidden files, press Command + Shift + period key.
Navigate to the /Applications folder and select CynetEPS .
Close the Security & Privacy window. The Cynet agent is granted with full disk access.
The Cynet agent is granted with full disk access.

title: Uninstalling the Cynet Agent- Mac
content:
You can uninstall the Cynet agent from a Mac endpoint in two ways:
From the Cynet 360 console (Operators only)
From the command line, using an interactive shell.
When uninstalling the Cynet agent from an endpoint, customers are guaranteed that they will no longer be charged for Cynet services for the selected endpoint. From this point, the endpoint is not counted as an active endpoint .

Uninstalling from the Cynet 360 console

From the Cynet toolbar, navigate to Endpoints > Management .
Select one or multiple endpoints from which you want to uninstall the Cynet agent.
Click Remove in the top-right corner.
When prompted to confirm that you want to uninstall Cynet from the selected endpoint, verify it is the correct endpoint and click Continue . The server marks the endpoint as inactive and the agent attempts to remove itself from the selected endpoint. The endpoint can not regain communication with the server, even if the uninstall operation fails, therefore the customer will no longer view activity for the endpoint. The endpoint no longer appears in the Endpoints Management page, but past activity can still be seen in the Forensics page.

Uninstalling from the command line

1. To uninstall the agent, using an interactive shell on the endpoint you want to uninstall, run the following command with root privileges:
2. If prompted, enter the uninstall password (as defined in Settings > Groups > Agent Settings in the Cynet console).
The Cynet agent is removed from the endpoint.

title: Single Endpoint Installation - Linux
content:
Versions 4.17 to 4.27

The Cynet agent can be easily installed - with all of the required dependencies - on a Linux endpoint using an installation script. In addition to the general and operating system requirements , there are several Linux components that are required for Cynet 360 operation and features. See the details below.
Note that some of these components are interchangeable.
Note: To install SaaS Version 4.28 and above (On-Prem version 4.27 and above) of the Cynet Agent, see Single Endpoint Installation - Linux Unified Installer .
Note: Cynet agent installation or upgrade does not require a system reboot.
Follow these instructions to obtain the installation files.
Unzip the EPS zip file that you downloaded: EPS_ZIP_*.zip.
Transfer the exported LINUX_CynetEPSInstaller.zip file to the Linux machine.
Unzip LINUX_CynetEPSInstaller.zip on the Linux machine. Supported Script Distributions unzip LINUX_CynetEPSInstaller.zip Note : For more information on the supported script distributions, see Supported Operating Systems .
The extracted archive should contain: cy_easyinstall.sh cy_uninstall.sh cyneteps.deb cyneteps.rpm CynetEPSArguments.txt
cy_easyinstall.sh
cy_uninstall.sh
cyneteps.deb
cyneteps.rpm
CynetEPSArguments.txt
Using elevated context ( sudo or root ), place all of the files above in the same folder as cy_easyinstall.sh, and execute cy_easyinstall.sh. The installation script will generate a log file that will be written to /tmp/.cinstres .
Using elevated context ( sudo or root ), place all of the files above in the same folder as cy_easyinstall.sh, and execute cy_easyinstall.sh.
The installation script will generate a log file that will be written to /tmp/.cinstres .
Follow the output from the installation script. The successful installation should result in: Installation Completed Successfully.
Follow the output from the installation script. The successful installation should result in: Installation Completed Successfully.
The following are the script options and command line examples and what will they do:
Will install Sysdig and CynetEPS. This is the default behavior.
Will install auditd and CynetEPS.
Note: Depending on the specific configuration of the host, Secure Boot may interfere with the CynetEPS installation and require manual modifications. For more details, contact your support team.

Verifying the Cynet EPS Installation Verify that CynetEPS is running: pgrep CynetEPS This should output the CynetEPS process ID. If not, it means that CynetEPS is not running properly.

Verify that CynetEPS is running:
This should output the CynetEPS process ID. If not, it means that CynetEPS is not running properly.

Verify that the service is healthy: systemctl status cyservice.service The service should be in a Running state. Verify that sysdig is running (if sysdig was selected with the installation script):

Verify that the service is healthy:
The service should be in a Running state. Verify that sysdig is running (if sysdig was selected with the installation script):

pgrep sysdig This should output the sysdig process ID. If not, it means that sysdig is not running properly.

This should output the sysdig process ID. If not, it means that sysdig is not running properly.
Note : The sysdig version installed depends on the EPS version installed. Starting in Cynet version 4.18, Sysdig version 0.34.1 is installed. For version 4.17 and below, Sysdig v0.28.0 is installed. The minimum sysdig version supported is 0.28.0; however, there are operating systems that may require a more recent version. For example, Red Hat 9.4 must run Sysdig version 0.38.1.

Version 4.16 and Earlier

In addition to the general and operating system requirements , there are several Linux components that are required for Cynet 360 operation and features. See the details below.
Note that some of these components are interchangeable.
To install the Cynet Agent:
Follow these instructions to obtain the installation files.
Move the file CynetEPSArguments.txt from the downloaded folder to the folder /tmp.
Run the following command (you must be signed in as a root user):
If you receive an error during the installation, you can view the error details in /tmp/CynetInstall.log.
Advanced detection and forensic capabilities
Improved antivirus file tracking
If Sysdig is installed, auditd and minimum Kernel version are not required.

title: Single Endpoint Installation - Windows
content:
The Cynet agent can be installed using the Cynet installer, using MSI Installer, or via the Command Line.
For installing the Cynet EPS on Windows XP or on Windows Server 2003, use the MSI file WinXP_CynetMSI.msi.
From the bottom of the left navigation pane, click Install.
Select Cynet Distribution Tool. An installation file is downloaded to your computer.
Click the downloaded file.
Follow the instructions in the Installation Wizard. The Cynet agent is installed on the endpoint, which is added to the default Group - Manually Installed Agents.
If you want to send the installation file to a different user by email, download the Installation Package, then select Email Deployment: Enter the email addresses to which Cynet must send the Cynet Installer. The user receives an invitation to install Cynet.
Note: Cynet agent installation or upgrade does not require a system reboot.
Follow these instructions for generating an MSI file, then double-click the MSI file to install the Cynet agent on the endpoint.
You must have CynetMSI file, found in the Cynet Installation Package. Make sure you are executing the command in an Administrator command prompt window or with Administrator privileges. The MSI requires elevated privileges to create a service on the host, to ensure the Cynet EPS will be persistent.
Run the following command:
On-premise:
SaaS:
If you want to add the host to a specific Scan Group (rather than the default group Manually Installed Agents), add the Group argument to the command:
If the Group name contains a space or (-), you must encode it as a base64 string and use the argument -group64.
On-premise:
SaaS:
Make sure that the Cynet agent is running on the endpoint by opening the Task Manager. There should be two processes running in memory:
CynetEPS.exe - Cynet 360 Main process
CynetMS.exe - Cynet 360 memory scanner

title: End of Life V4.5
content:
Cynet aims to deliver the best quality product to its growing customer community, and constantly improve its offering. To this end, Cynet periodically publishes an End of Life statement, which includes previous Cynet versions, legacy features and agent support for operating systems.
The End-of-Life process will be performed gradually, based on industry standards, and will be a part of the official release of the latest version.
This announcement will be in affect with the full release of Cynet Version 4.5.

Supported Cynet Version

In accordance with Cynet's Support Policy , with the release of Cynet 360 V4.5, older Cynet 360 versions reach the following stages:
Cynet 360 V4.3: End of software maintenance
Cynet 360 V4.1: End of support

Deprecated Features

As of Cynet version 4.5, the following features are no longer available:

title: Release Notes V4.5
content:
New and Improved Features

Cynet version 4.5 includes new and improved features:
Cynet 360 now includes playbooks for automating a cyberbreach response. Playbooks allows you to transform your security operations with scalable, automated workflows for any security use case. Our upcoming 3rd party integrations, extensive forensic collection and automation capabilities make it easy to create any investigation and remediation flow you want. No coding needed.
Playbooks enable you to investigate Cynet’s full forensic data. Collect the desired data, add further conditions on the receive results, then perform actions on assets at scale. Creating a playbook is performed using a friendly and easy to use Playbook Canvas.
The playbook investigation results appear in the Incident View of the alert on which the investigation is based.
As of version 4.5, alert information can include the MITRE ATT&CK tactics and techniques related to the alert. This is also available in the Alert Global view.
This feature is disabled by default. To add MITRE ATT&CK information, contact Cynet.
Cynet is expanding the investigation scope offered to users to include processes that run on hosts. As of version 4.5, forensic data includes process occurrences, accessed from an alert or file. For example, if PowerShell runs in two different occurrences, one occurrence that is benign and the other malicious, you can view each of these occurrences separately and investigate its origin.
To differentiate it from the File forensic page, the Process Occurrence page includes a heading, indicating the specific process instance, the occurrence creation time in the top-right corner, and a Link to the File SHA256 page.
The process tree displayed in every alert is now fully linkable and enables you to investigate all the processes involved in the incident.
A new security mechanism has been added to Cynet 360 that further enhances its protection capabilities. File Operation Protection is a Driver based mechanism that correlates between a process, a file, and the operation the process is trying to perform on the file (such as Read, Write, Execute, Change Attributes). The mechanism monitors protected files and allows only certain processes to perform specific operations on them. All other operations are blocked.
To enable File Operation Protection, you must activate Memory Protection.
Cynet 360 offers a Best Practice configuration that you can apply to a Scan Group. The following changed have been made in Best Practice configuration:
For the complete list of mechanisms and their Best Practice configuration, click here .
When creating a Custom Remediation Action, you can add arguments to the remediation script. In addition to the existing types of arguments, a new type of argument is now available: Runtime arguments.
Runtime arguments allow you to add an argument when creating the Custom Remediation action, but enter its value only when executing the action.
Until now, costumers could not include several hosts with the same host name in Cynet 360, even in these hosts were assigned to different Scan Groups. Hosts with identical host names would appear in the Scanner page as one host.
Cynet 360 now supports having different hosts with the same host name, as long as these hosts are assigned to different Scan Groups.
To support duplicate host names, a Scan Group column has been added to the Forensic and Actions pages. Scan Group data has also been added to information sent via the Cynet API (syslog and data sent to AWS S3 bucket).
Customers can now uninstall the Cynet EPS directly from the Cynet 360 console.
When uninstalling a host, Cynet 360 attempts to connect to the host and uninstall the EPS. If the operation is unsuccessful, for example due to network disconnection, the EPS is blocked from resuming activity. From this point, Cynet will not count this host as an active host .
Using the new Uninstall button, customers are guaranteed that they will no longer be charged for Cynet services for the selected host.
Cynet 360 includes the option to add an uninstall password for the Cynet EPS. The password applied to all site Scan Groups. This feature is now enhanced to allow setting a different uninstall password per Scan Group.
Integrating Cynet with SaaS applications can now be configured per Scan Group. You can now also filter the SSPM page to display information per Scan Group.
New integrations have been added to Cynet 360. Customers using SSPM can now also manage misconfigurations for these SaaS applications:
Salesforce
Google Workspace
Newly supported operating systems:
MAC OS 12
Windows 11
Windows server 2022
Customers that want to try Log Management free for 14 days, can now easily do so without contacting Cynet. By default, Log Management displays data collected from hosts (Windows Events and File Monitoring events), which is added and correlated to data from integrated apps.
Partners can now easily track their business activity and compared it to their Cynet 360 license. For example, this information includes number of endpoints active in last calendar month, compared to the number of endpoints in the license.
To make valuable information more accessible, improvements have been made to the following screens:
Single alert view (single site and Global view): New location for alert status and Incident View
Forensic screen: Top 10 results chart is removed
Integration settings screen: AWS S3 integration settings moved to the Configuration tab.
To better secure the access to the Cynet 360 console, MFA is now a mandatory step in user authentication. In addition, all user passwords expire after 90 days. When attempting to login after password expiration, users will be prompt to reset their password.

Bug Fixes

The following issues were fixed in this version of Cynet 360:

Known Issues

The Details tab in Forensic > Hosts details the host operating system. Windows 11 hosts are wrongly listed as Windows 10 Pro.
Configuring MFA requires initial SMS verification. This SMS is not received in the United Arab Emirates. Suggested workaround: Select Voice Call.
Suggested workaround: Select Voice Call.
Customers can save up to 100 custom policies .
If you have enabled an Uninstall Password and you want to manually upgrade the Cynet 360 version using Microsoft SCCM, you must either add an additional command with the Uninstall Password (see below) or disable the Uninstall Password 10 minutes before performing the upgrade. Auto-upgrade, enabled from the Cynet 360 console, is not affected by an Uninstall Password. Suggested workaround : If an Uninstall Password is configured, run the following command to uninstall the previous package: msiexec /x "{product_code}" /q PASS=UninstallPassword * product_code is auto-generated Then run the following command to upgrade the existing package (use this command alone if an Uninstall Password is not configured): msiexec /i CynetEPS.msi /q
* product_code is auto-generated
Then run the following command to upgrade the existing package (use this command alone if an Uninstall Password is not configured):

title: Release Notes V4.5.X
content:
V4.5.15 - On-Premise

This version includes the following fix:

V4.5.11/4.5.13 - On-Premise

Before upgrading to this version, customers who have an exceptionally large volume of forensic data are required to clear data. Cynet Support will contact the relevant customers and perform the cleanup before the upgrade session.
This version includes the following improvements:
To improve service and support for large scale on-premises environments, Cynet is converting o n-premises customers who have Master/Slave architecture to Global/Sites architecture. This infrastructural change will provide customers with a Global Manager server that allows operators to manage alerts from all sites and apply settings to the entire environment.
Cynet Support will contact the relevant customers and accompany them though the transition.
You can now edit log configuration and memory consumption using t wo new/improved fields. These changes are made in the configuration file cynet_local.config , located in /opt/Cynet/Data :
enable_log_debug : When = True, the EPS log level is Debug. B y default, this is set to False.
memsize : L imit s the EPS memory consumption . Enter the memory usage limit in MB.

V4.5.10

This version includes the following enhancement and fixes:
When creating a Storage Device Control profile, you can add exceptions to the general rule you selected (Block or Allow). A new Description field has been added to the Exceptions screen, making it easy for you to monitor which devices you are allowing/blocking.

V4.5.6 SaaS / 4.5.7 On-Premise

This version includes the following improvements and fix:
A search bar has been added to the Advanced Search section of the Forensic page. This search bar enables you easily find a specific policy or saved search using a free-text search.
MSSPs managing their customers using Groups can now filter the SaaS Security Posture Management to display data separately for every Group. This required setting up 3rd-party integrations separately for each Group.
Optimization was performed to reduce network traffic between the Cynet EPS and server and minimize the required disk space.
Cynet 360 on-premises no longer supports Elastic versions below 7.

V4.5.4

This version includes the following fixes:
This version includes the following improvements and fixes:
Up to now, customers could configure the antivirus to automatically delete malicious files. To avoid irreversible loss of important files, Cynet has removed the Delete remediation option. Cynet is automatically shifting all environments in which Delete was selected, to the remediation action Rename File.
The Device Control page has been redesigned to make it simple for customers to create a Device Control profile.
The Cynet Installation Package, which SaaS customers can download from the Cynet 360 console, now includes information about the EPS version it contains.
You can configure Cynet 360 to send a triggered alert by email. The alert email now includes a link to view the alert in the Cynet 360 console, allowing you to easily start investigating the incident.

V4.5.1

This version includes the following fixes:
