title: Release Notes V4.6
content:
Released August 2022

New and Improved Features

Cynet version 4.6 includes new and improved features listed below. For deprecated features, see End of Life .
Cynet Automated Response Playbooks are now released for Public Preview.
Cynet Automated Response Playbooks relieve the pressure on security analysts that must now investigate an ongoing torrent of alerts and then remediate discovered threats. Playbooks define the investigation steps to be automatically taken for any alert type, includes conditional logic, and can include remediation actions based on findings. Playbook can be automatically triggered by an alert or other condition to fully uncover and remediate the full security incident associated with an alert. Every time a playbook is activated, the entire workflow is graphically depicted in the Cynet Incident View so you can easily see every investigation step, the findings and all remediation and operational actions taken.
Cynet provides pre-built Automated Response Playbooks as well as an intuitive drag-and-drop playbook builder that enables security analysts to quickly and easily build custom playbooks based on their company’s particular needs. Playbooks are launched with an out-of-the box investigation scenario for malicious file detection. This playbook will be automatically triggered for every alert that detects a malicious file and will search for the file across the organization. It will also detect if other scripts, that are currently on disk, were involved in the attack.
Beyond freeing up significant analyst time, Playbooks upscale the security team’s capabilities by providing a defined, consistent response process to make more accurate decisions and ensure alerts are properly addressed.
Cynet 360 includes many remediation actions that you can easily perform directly from an alert or the Forensic page. A new remediation action has been added under Host remediation actions, enabling you to open a live remote shell session on the host. The new Remote Shell remediation action can simplify many scenarios, such as: If a suspicious file was dumped on the disk, you can use Remote Shell to run a command on the host and easily discover the other files in the folder.
This remediation action is currently available for Windows hosts only. The remote shell session can take up to five minutes to connect. In future versions, we plan to make the connection immediate.
On-premise support: This feature requires direct connection between the endpoint and the Cynet SaaS. See requirements here .
Cynet SSPM now includes new rules for securing your AWS instance, taking this feature to the next level of Cloud Security Posture Management.
Many improvement to the SSPM page are included in this version of Cynet 360, such as: Selective export to Excel, application filter, clickable widgets, and more.
A significant improvement has been implemented for organization using non-persistent VDI environments. You can now add a new argument to the EPS installation in the golden image, which will improve host initialization and performance.
You can now edit log configuration and memory consumption using t wo new/improved fields. These changes are made in the configuration file cynet_local.config , located in /opt/Cynet/Data :
enable_log_debug : When = True, the EPS log level is Debug. B y default, this is set to False.
memsize : L imit s the EPS memory consumption . Enter the memory usage limit in MB.
Using real and common usernames (such as Administrator) for decoy users generates many false alerts following sign-in of legitimate users. Therefore, certain usernames can no longer be used for decoy users. When attempting to create a decoy user with a forbidden username, you will receive an error message.
When creating a new custom remediation action , users are required to upload a file that is executed as part of the remediation action. This process has been improved so that users can now upload several files, edit the files, and more.

Fixed Issues

The following issues were fixed in this version of Cynet 360:

title: End of Life V4.6
content:
Cynet aims to deliver the best quality product to its growing customer community, and constantly improve its offering. To this end, Cynet periodically publishes an End of Life statement, which includes previous Cynet versions, legacy features and agent support for operating systems.
The End-of-Life process will be performed gradually, based on industry standards, and will be a part of the official release of the latest version.
This announcement will be in affect with the full release of Cynet 360 Version 4.6.

Supported Cynet Version

In accordance with Cynet's Support Policy , with the release of Cynet 360 V4.6, older Cynet 360 on-premise versions reach the following stages:
Cynet 360 V4.4: End of software maintenance
Cynet 360 V4.2: End of support

Deprecated Features

As of Cynet version 4.6, the following features are no longer available:

title: Release Notes V4.7
content:
Released October 2022

New Features

Cynet has recently been rebranded and the Cynet 360 console is joining the change! Over the following months, various screens in the console will be improved and updated to make security management easier and more intuitive.
This version includes a new sidebar and login page.
We are launching a new API reference , which provides clear and comprehensive documentation of Cynet's web APIs. All endpoints will be gradually added to this site, which will eventually obsolete the old API documentation.
New capabilities have been added to Cynet Automated Response Playbooks, released in Version 4.6 . These capabilities expand the scope of Playbooks so that they not only automate investigations, but also automate the response by performing actions on findings. The added actions are:
Include remediation actions within the Playbook, to be applied on investigation findings
Select if remediation actions within the Playbook run automatically or require manual approval
Send an automated email with investigation findings to selected recipients
Export and import a Playbook
Delete a step or an entire branch of a Playbook
A new integration of Cynet 360 with Datto Autotask allows you to manage all your tasks in one place. This integration enables a bi-directional sync between Cynet alerts and your organization's Autotask PSA; Every triggered alert creates a new Autotask ticket, which will automatically close when the alert is closed. You can also close tickets directly in Autotask, which will close the corresponding alert in Cynet 360.
MSSPs can now change the site type (e.g., from POC to Paying) directly in the Site Management page.
For better and clearer user and permission management, When adding a new user, you can no longer assign to it a custom permissions set. Every user must be assigned a role, which defines the user's permissions.
The Storage Device Control information in the Forensic page now includes the user that was logged into the endpoint when the device was inserted.
New data sources are continuously being certified for CLM, such as Cisco Firepower and SonicWall Firewall. For each data source, you must open a designated communication port between the data source and the Logstash server, and set the required log format. For more information, click here .
Cynet now offers CLM customers cold storage of data for the life of the contract, for compliance purposes . Click here for details.
Cynet CLM maps incoming data so that a single parameter (e.g., username) appears once, rather than in several different versions (e.g., user_name, user, etc.). Field names are defined by Elastic Common Schema. For more information, click here .
Endpoint information that appears in the response of the API call GET Scanner now also includes the Group to which the endpoint belongs.
Optimization was performed to reduce the amount of saved data by improving process identification and aggregation.

Fixed Issues

The following quality and stability issues were resolved:

Known Issue

On domain controllers with Windows Server 2012 OS, all login/logoff events of endpoints protected by the Cynet 360 agent are being logged.

title: Release Notes V4.8
content:
Released February 2023. This version is SaaS only.

New Features

Cynet 360 includes many remediation actions that you can perform from the console on a protected endpoint. These actions are now executed with no delay time, allowing you faster response to threats.
The new alert emails are easy to read, mobile friendly, and include useful information about the attack. The new format also includes a link so you can easily navigate to the Cynet 360 console and investigate further.
This version of Cynet 360 includes significant enhancements to the detection capabilities of the following attacks:
Lateral movement via network shares
Process injection
Credential theft
Anti-tampering
Logstash is required for collecting data from various data sources to Cynet CLM. A new Logstash installer for Windows is now available in the Cynet installation package. The installer includes the default configurations, it creates Windows Firewall rules to allow incoming traffic, and will automatically apply updates, when these are available.
Collecting logs from Microsoft 365 is now a one-click procedure, available from the Integrations page.
The following changes are implemented in this version:
The Cynet agent for Linux endpoints supports only GLIBC version 2.12 and above. To check your GLIBC version, run the following command:
The Cynet agent no longer spawns the sub-process CynetAV. This process is now included in the process CynetEPS. Combining these processes makes the agent more efficient and does not affect the agent's performance or resource consumption.
We continue to expand and improve the abilities of Cynet Automated Response Playbooks. This version includes a new built-in playbook that performs a full alert investigation. The new built-in playbook c hecks network connections created by the process, d etects lateral movement, d etects dump of attack tools, and s uggests remediation actions. This playbook will run automatically for every triggered alert.
In addition, the following modules have been added to the playbook canvas:
New queries:
Get related alerts
Get File instances
Get child processes
Get connections
New actions:
Add comment to alert
Close alert
New dynamic parameters:
Alert.Endpoint_remediation: Indicates if a remediation action was performed on the alert.
Alert.Endpoint_remediation_status: The status of the remediation action that was performed (succeeded or failed)
Until now, decoy files for ransomware protection could be applied locally on protected endpoints. To detect threat actors' activities across in the organizational environment, this feature has been expanded to include decoy files for shared folders.
Customers using SSPM can now manage misconfigurations for these SaaS applications:
Dropbox
WebEx
Slack
See the complete list of services here .
Customers using CLM can now import logs from these data sources:
Cisco Meraki
Cisco Meraki
IBM 3PAR
IBM 3PAR
IBM AS400
IBM AS400
QNAP NAS
QNAP NAS
See the complete list of data sources here .
To increased security, sending syslog to an external SIEM via TCP now supports TLS 1.2.

title: Release Notes V4.9 (SaaS)
content:
Released April 2023. Version 4.9 (SaaS) includes the following features:

Domain Filtering (Beta version)

Cynet’s network detection capabilities now include the ability to block access to unsafe domains. When a user tries to access a risky domain, Cynet will block the operation and trigger an alert. Cynet will manage domain categorization. User customization will be available it later versions.

Port Scanning

A new port scanning capability has been added to Cynet SSPM. This feature scans your network and notifies you of risky exposure to the internet. Configuring the Port Scanner is via the Integrations page.

User Risks and Saved Filters

A new tab in the SSPM page provides visibility into user risks in SaaS applications. In addition, you can now also save your filters and searches in the SSPM page for future use.

Logstash Installer for Linux and Group Support

A new Logstash installer for Linux is now available in the Cynet installation package. The installer includes the default configurations, and will automatically apply updates, when these are available.
MSSPs that use Groups to manage their customers, can now configure a separate Logstash server for each Group. Events appear in CLM with the Group tag, allowing you to easily correlate each event with its source.

Detection Enhancements

Improved protection capabilities for p rocess injection techniques (MITRE T1055) and payloads within the injection.
Enhance our antivirus capabilities for memory-only malware.

Email Notification of Playbook Findings

A new email will notify customers of playbook findings when a playbook run is complete. The email includes a link, so you easily navigate to the console to view the investigation and perform actions.

Changes in SaaS Communication Requirements

To improved bandwidth performance and reduce latency, we are adding two domains to our cloud environment. Following this change, which will be implemented gradually, customers will need to adjust their firewall settings according to these requirements .

title: Release Notes V4.11 (SaaS)
content:
Released July 2023. Version 4.11 (SaaS) includes the following features:

New Group Management

A new, simplified Group Settings page allows you to easily manage the Groups in your environment and their settings. The new page also provides a clear view of the endpoints in the Group, including IP addresses, where you move endpoints to/from the Group. Security settings are now divided into main categories that you can simply turn On/Off and select a mode of operation. Advanced configuration is optional. Read more in the Migrating to the New Settings Page article. This feature will be rolled out gradually.

Executive Report

A new executive summary report provides a big-picture overview of your environment, including inventory, alerts, and more. You can generate the executive report on demand from the console for a site or a specific Group. Additionally, a report per site will be sent automatically by email every month.

Enhancements to Exclusion Profiles

Exclusion profiles define items on the endpoint that you don't want the Cynet agent to scan.
In addition to the existing options, you can now exclude processes based on their command line. Also, when creating exclusion rules based on a process path or command line, you can now choose if to exclude child processes as well.

Enhanced Detection

Version 4.11 includes enhancements, together with improved accuracy, to our detection capabilities. This version focuses mainly on protection against a variety of exploit frameworks and techniques that threat actors use to execute malicious processes.

New Data Source for CLM

Nutanix AHV has been certified as a data source for Centralized Log Management.

End-User License Agreement in Console Login

When signing in to the Cynet 360 console, users are now required to agree to Cynet’s Terms and Conditions.

Supported Mac Operating Systems

From January 2024, Cynet will no longer support MacOS 10.13 and 10.14. For the full list of supported operating systems, click here .

Known Limitation

Only global operators can view and manage global profiles (whitelisting and exclusions). Users with role Operator Manage Sites will get an error message when navigating to the global profiles page.

title: Release Notes V4.13 (SaaS)
content:
Released August 2023. Version 4.13 (SaaS) includes the following features:

Agent Version Updated in Windows Registry

Until now, the original agent version that was installed on the endpoint was recorded in the Windows registry. As of Version 4.13, when the agent version is updated, the registry record will be updated as well. This allows third-party tools like Microsoft SCCM to correctly monitor Cynet's versions.

Findings Page in Incident View (Beta Version)

Every playbook run is summarized in an Incident View , available from the alert that triggered the playbook.
The Incident View now includes an actionable Findings page, which shows all of the entities (files, network connections, endpoints, etc.) involved in the attack - as well as the actions that need to be taken.
This feature will be rolled out gradually.

API Custom Remediation (Beta Version)

You can create a custom remediation to integrate with a third-party system API (API custom remediation). This will allow you to perform orchestration between Cynet and the third-party system, by using this in a playbook or automatic remediation rule.
When using this in a playbook, you can also analyze the third-party response with the new JSON parser and consider the response in your investigation flow.
This feature will be rolled out gradually.

Select All Option in Global Filters

MSSP and multi-site customers now have a Select All option available in the global Alerts , Forensic , and Audit pages. This allows you to easily clear the default selection and select specific sites.

Playbook Improvements

JSON parser - When using API remediation, you can analyze the third-party response with a new JSON parser, and consider the response in your investigation flow.
Within the Condition module, multiple conditions with an AND/OR relationship are now available.

Cynet 360 Mobile Data in Centralized Log Management (CLM) Customers using Cynet 360 Mobile can send logs from the mobile application to CLM.

Customers using Cynet 360 Mobile can send logs from the mobile application to CLM.

Cynet CSPM Integration with Microsoft Azure and Google Cloud Platform

You can now integrate Cynet CSPM with Microsoft Azure and Google Cloud Platform, to view and fix misconfigurations in your cloud platform.

New SaaS Security Posture Management (SSPM) Services

The integration with Office 365 now includes E5-related misconfigurations. These misconfigurations are clearly tagged so that you can handle them differently, if necessary.
You can also get the security posture of a complex Google topology. As of Version 4.13, you can get the security posture of multiple Google Workspace projects, to manage their misconfigurations and risks.

title: Release Notes V4.14 (SaaS)
content:
Released October 2023. Version 4.14 (SaaS) includes the following features:

Whitelisting of Unauthorized File Operation Attempts

You can now whitelist the Unauthorized File Operation Attempt alert.

Antivirus Scan Updates (Beta)

A host action can now be invoked to perform a full Antivirus scan from the UI, for a specific endpoint. This can be done through Forensic > Hosts > Actions > Run AntiVirus Scan .
The scan type for an on-demand scan will currently be a full Antivirus scan only. In the future, you will be able to select a scan profile for the job.
When the EPS receives the request, the scan will start running immediately. On-demand scans will stop any other running scan and will start running.
You can now schedule (every day, week, or month) a full AV scan from the UI, per scan group.
You can also exclude specific paths from the scan.
There is also an option to request for the scheduled scan to be gradual. You can decide the gradual scan period, and during this timeframe (in hours), each endpoint on the group will start the scan.
You can cancel an actively running scan from the Action page.
For scans with In Progress status, you can check the scan checkbox and stop the scan by clicking Actions > Cancel Antivirus Scan .

New Group Management

A new, simplified Group Settings page allows you to easily manage the Groups in your environment and their settings. The new page also provides a clear view of the endpoints in the Group, including IP addresses, and where you move endpoints to/from the Group. Security settings are now divided into main categories that you can simply turn On/Off and select a mode of operation. Advanced configuration is optional. Read more in the Migrating to the New Settings Page article.
This feature is now enabled to all customers.

Enhanced Email Anti-Flooding

Emails are no longer sent if the anti-flooding limit is reached.
All alerts of the same type will be consolidated into one email after the 30-email threshold is met for the first time. This will limit the maximum number of emails to 31 per alert type per day.
If emails from the same alert were sent more than 30 times per hour:
An email will be sent saying that over 30 alerts were triggered in the last minute, and we will stop sending emails for 24 hours.
An email will be sent saying that over 30 alerts were triggered in the last minute, and we will stop sending emails for 24 hours.
After the 24 hours, an aggregated email will go out.
After the 24 hours, an aggregated email will go out.
Rate limiting will then be re-assessed.
Rate limiting will then be re-assessed.
The full list of alerts can be seen in the Cynet console.

Trigger Playbooks on New Alert Types

Two new alert types were added to Cynet, with the ability to trigger playbooks on both:
Domain Filtering
Domain Filtering
XDR Alerts
XDR Alerts

Improved Sysdig Deployment

Sysdig will now be loaded by the Cynet endpoint, even if Sysdig is installed after the endpoint is installed.

Cloud & SaaS Security Posture Management Enhancements

We have enhanced the number of rules that can be displayed for GCP.
This capacity will make it more in line with what is offered for AWS and Azure.
A new inventory tab called Ports and Subdomains :
Shows the inventory of ports and subdomains exposed to the internet.
Shows the inventory of ports and subdomains exposed to the internet.
Shows the source (i.e., integration) that provided this information: AWS, Port Scanner.
Shows the source (i.e., integration) that provided this information: AWS, Port Scanner.
Shows the exposure status to the internet: open vs. closed.
Shows the exposure status to the internet: open vs. closed.
A new inventory tab called Teams is also available now. This tab displays the inventory of Teams apps.
You can now click inside the Top Risky Services widget, to add a filter for those services.
Customers of 365 E3 licenses can now immediately identify misconfiguration rules relevant for E5; they are clearly marked as such in their name.

Executive Summary Report Replaces Previous Reports

With the introduction of the new Executive Summary report, the Risk report, Threats report, and Inventory and Usage report are being deprecated.
If you need information that you cannot find in the Cynet 360 console, let us know and we will do our best to enhance the existing reports and accommodate your needs.

Additional Supported Device Control

We now support device control on SCSI removable storage devices . You can do this when creating a storage device exception.

title: Release Notes V4.13 (On-Premises)
content:
Released November 2023. Version 4.13 (On-Premises) includes the following features:

Detection Enhancements

Cynet 360 now includes significant enhancements to the detection capabilities of the following attacks:
Lateral movement via network shares
Process injection
Credential theft
Anti-tampering
Improved protection capabilities for p rocess injection techniques (MITRE T1055), and payloads within the injection.
Enhance our antivirus capabilities for memory-only malware.
Protection against a variety of exploit frameworks and techniques that threat actors use to execute malicious processes.

Changes in Linux Agent

The following changes are now implemented for the Linux Agent:
The Cynet agent for Linux endpoints supports only GLIBC version 2.12 and above. To check your GLIBC version, run the following command:
The Cynet agent no longer spawns the sub-process CynetAV. This process is now included in the process CynetEPS. Combining these processes makes the agent more efficient and does not affect the agent's performance or resource consumption.

Decoy Files on Shared Folders

Until now, decoy files for ransomware protection could be applied locally on protected endpoints. To detect threat actors' activities across in the organizational environment, this feature has been expanded to include decoy files for shared folders.

Enhancements to Exclusion Profiles

Exclusion profiles define items on the endpoint that you do not want the Cynet agent to scan.
In addition to the existing options, you can now exclude processes based on their command line. Also, when creating exclusion rules based on a process path or command line, you can now choose whether to exclude child processes as well.

End-User License Agreement in Console Login

When signing in to the Cynet 360 console, users are now required to agree to Cynet’s Terms and Conditions.

Supported Mac Operating Systems

From January 2024, Cynet will no longer support MacOS 10.13 and 10.14. For the full list of supported operating systems, click here .

Agent Version Updated in Windows Registry

Until now, the original agent version that was installed on the endpoint was recorded in the Windows registry. As of Version 4.13, when the agent version is updated, the registry record will be updated as well. This allows third-party tools like Microsoft SCCM to correctly monitor Cynet's versions.

Select All Options in Global Filters

MSSP and multi-site customers now have a Select All option available in the global Alerts , Forensic , and Audit pages. This allows you to easily clear the default selection and select specific sites.

New Alert Email Format

New alert emails are easy to read, mobile friendly, and include useful information about the attack. The new format also includes a link so you can easily navigate to the Cynet 360 console and investigate further.
Note that this will be enabled only to customers that are not using their local SMTP server for sending the emails.

sections.text: Released November 2023. Version 4.13 (On-Premises) includes the following features:

sections.heading: Detection Enhancements
sections.text:
Cynet 360 now includes significant enhancements to the detection capabilities of the following attacks:
Lateral movement via network shares
Process injection
Credential theft
Anti-tampering
Improved protection capabilities for p rocess injection techniques (MITRE T1055), and payloads within the injection.
Enhance our antivirus capabilities for memory-only malware.
Protection against a variety of exploit frameworks and techniques that threat actors use to execute malicious processes.

sections.heading: Changes in Linux Agent
sections.text:
The following changes are now implemented for the Linux Agent:
The Cynet agent for Linux endpoints supports only GLIBC version 2.12 and above. To check your GLIBC version, run the following command:
The Cynet agent no longer spawns the sub-process CynetAV. This process is now included in the process CynetEPS. Combining these processes makes the agent more efficient and does not affect the agent's performance or resource consumption.

sections.heading: Decoy Files on Shared Folders
sections.text: Until now, decoy files for ransomware protection could be applied locally on protected endpoints. To detect threat actors' activities across in the organizational environment, this feature has been expanded to include decoy files for shared folders.

sections.heading: Enhancements to Exclusion Profiles
sections.text:
Exclusion profiles define items on the endpoint that you do not want the Cynet agent to scan.
In addition to the existing options, you can now exclude processes based on their command line. Also, when creating exclusion rules based on a process path or command line, you can now choose whether to exclude child processes as well.

sections.heading: End-User License Agreement in Console Login
sections.text: When signing in to the Cynet 360 console, users are now required to agree to Cynet’s Terms and Conditions.

sections.heading: Supported Mac Operating Systems
sections.text: From January 2024, Cynet will no longer support MacOS 10.13 and 10.14. For the full list of supported operating systems, click here .

sections.heading: Agent Version Updated in Windows Registry
sections.text: Until now, the original agent version that was installed on the endpoint was recorded in the Windows registry. As of Version 4.13, when the agent version is updated, the registry record will be updated as well. This allows third-party tools like Microsoft SCCM to correctly monitor Cynet's versions.

sections.heading: Select All Options in Global Filters
sections.text: MSSP and multi-site customers now have a Select All option available in the global Alerts , Forensic , and Audit pages. This allows you to easily clear the default selection and select specific sites.

sections.heading: New Alert Email Format
sections.text:
New alert emails are easy to read, mobile friendly, and include useful information about the attack. The new format also includes a link so you can easily navigate to the Cynet 360 console and investigate further.
Note that this will be enabled only to customers that are not using their local SMTP server for sending the emails.

title: https://help.cynet.com/en/articles/243-release-notes-v4-17-saas
content:
Released November 2023. Version 4.17 (SaaS) includes the following features:

XDR Alerts

Cynet now provides threat detection based on correlating forensic events from both endpoints and third-party sources (such as firewalls, Microsoft 365, etc.).
The threat detection triggers a new type of alert called XDR Alert . This could be, for example, malicious activity detected on OneDrive or a FortiGate firewall.
You can also now create a whitelist profile for XDR alerts, based on the alert name.
Under Settings > Profiles > Whitelist Alerts , there is a new category called XDR Alert .

Device Control Support for Mac OS

MAC endpoints will now be able to detect device insertion, and trigger an alert according to the storage device control profile.
Note: This feature is currently in beta.
You can enable device control per group, in the Group Settings of your Mac devices.
In Advanced Settings , you can choose the device control storage profile that you want to apply to your group.
The storage device control profile allows for the following to be configured:
Alert severity - the profile will specify in which severity the alert will trigger.
Alert severity - the profile will specify in which severity the alert will trigger.
Ability to block the device - when enabled, the inserted devices will be blocked.
Ability to block the device - when enabled, the inserted devices will be blocked.
Authorized/Unauthorized - the ability to define whether all of the inserted devices are authorized or unauthorized.
Authorized/Unauthorized - the ability to define whether all of the inserted devices are authorized or unauthorized.
Ability to create/add an exception: Authorized - if all devices are authorized, the devices under the exception will be unauthorized. Unauthorized - if all devices are unauthorized, the devices under the exception will be authorized.
Ability to create/add an exception:
Authorized - if all devices are authorized, the devices under the exception will be unauthorized.
Authorized - if all devices are authorized, the devices under the exception will be unauthorized.
Unauthorized - if all devices are unauthorized, the devices under the exception will be authorized.
Unauthorized - if all devices are unauthorized, the devices under the exception will be authorized.
Mac group settings (enable device control):

Centralized Log Management (CLM) Enhancements

MSSPs that manage their customers' sites can specify their CLM retention policy (according to their license).
The options include:
None = limited CLM retention.
None = limited CLM retention.
30 days = CLM with 30 days of hot retention.
30 days = CLM with 30 days of hot retention.
90 days = CLM with 90 days of hot retention.
90 days = CLM with 90 days of hot retention.
>90 days = CLM 90 days hot retention plus long-term cold log retention.
>90 days = CLM 90 days hot retention plus long-term cold log retention.
Note that all of the settings above can be applied during creation of a new site (via the Site > Create dialog), or later during the editing of an existing site (via the Site > Edit dialog).
The Forensics > Analytics view will now display as limited if your CLM is being used without having been purchased.
This is labeled as Limited License on the CLM page.

SSPM Enhancements

You can now manage risks in one grid, with a new Status column and no internal tabs for open/ignored and no pop-up dialogs.
You can now manage risks in one grid, with a new Status column and no internal tabs for open/ignored and no pop-up dialogs.
You can work on several SSPM misconfigurations in parallel, by taking multiple long-running actions (e.g., fix, ignore, unfix) on several risks. Once a misconfiguration is resolved, you no longer have to wait until it's completed to be able to do anything else on the page.
You can work on several SSPM misconfigurations in parallel, by taking multiple long-running actions (e.g., fix, ignore, unfix) on several risks. Once a misconfiguration is resolved, you no longer have to wait until it's completed to be able to do anything else on the page.

New Linux Combined Installer Script Implementation

The Linux Cynet installer now automatically installs important libraries, enabling improved security.
The new installer contains:
Sysdig
Sysdig
AuditD (note that this is optional).
AuditD (note that this is optional).
Cynet’s Endpoint Security (EPS).
Cynet’s Endpoint Security (EPS).
For additional information, see Single Endpoint Installation - Linux .

Antivirus Scan Enhancements

Note : These AV-related features are currently Windows only.
You can now specify specific locations to run an AV scheduled scan on. You can choose pre-defined locations as well as desired locations.
To create a custom scan, go to Settings > Profiles > Antivirus .
In the scan profile, under Where to Scan , you can click Custom Scan . You can then click Add Location to apply the scan to specific locations only, including:
External drives
External drives
Program folders (Program Data + Program Files)
Program folders (Program Data + Program Files)
User profile folders
User profile folders
System drive
System drive
System folders
System folders
Temp folders
Temp folders
Additionally, you can insert a specific location to scan by choosing "path". You can also include sub-folders here:
You can now choose a special event as a trigger for a scheduled antivirus scan.
To create a scheduled event-based scan, go to Settings > Profiles > Antivirus .
Create a new profile, and under Where to Scan , you can select USB insertion .
This will enable you to run a scan on the USB device upon USB insertion.
You can now run or cancel an on-demand Antivirus scan locally, from the endpoint itself.
When right-clicking the tray icon, an on-demand full Antivirus scan can be triggered or cancelled. You can control whether to enable this functionality by going to Settings > Group .
Within the Agent Settings , you can select Trigger actions from endpoint .

Support Exclusion on Domain Filtering

The domain filtering feature now supports process exclusion, including the ability to exclude by domain and IP. IP can be a single IP or a CIDR range.

Playbook Time Format Enhancements

The Cynet autonomous investigation email summary's time format has been updated from a 12-hour format only to a 24-hour format with UTC (i.e., 15:32:37 UTC ).

Adding Additional Information to Datto Alerts

Truncation of Datto alerts are as they were before (i.e., truncated after 8000 characters), but a message has been added to explain why the alert was truncated.

New Action - API Call

The API Call is a new remediation action that can be used like any remediation on demand: as an automatic remediation and also as a step within playbooks.
Within playbooks, you can parse the results using the new JSON parser.
Note: This feature is being rolled out gradually.

title: Release Notes V4.18 (SaaS)
content:
Released February 2024. Version 4.18 (SaaS) includes the following features:

Allowlist and Exclusions Rule per MSSP

MSSPs that are using a shared Cynet domain (mssp.cynet.com and usmssp.cynet.com) can now see/create/edit allowlist and exclusions rules across their customers.
This feature solves the need for an MSSP to easily define an allowlist or exclusion across all of their customers.

SSPM Enhancements

Customers can now get an email notification about new risks found by SSPM by saving an SSPM search filter and configuring the option to send an email notification for newly discovered risks.
New SSPM rules for Microsoft365.

XDR Alert Enhancements

Alerts of the type XDR Alert now have the title "XDR Alert" in the Alerts page.
UI changes: “Host” is now shown as “Asset” if it is not an endpoint (such as in Office365). The Process tree is no longer displayed if not relevant.
“Host” is now shown as “Asset” if it is not an endpoint (such as in Office365).
The Process tree is no longer displayed if not relevant.

Add Exception Rule from Device Control Alert

From the Device Control alert, it is now possible to add the ID of a specific device as an exception for the selected profiles.

ConnectWise PSA Integration with Cynet

Cynet has a new ConnectWise PSA integration that allows customers to manage alerts and tickets in a single system. When a new alert appears in Cynet, a ticket is opened in ConnectWise. When the ticket is closed in ConnectWise, the related alert is also closed in Cynet. If the alert is closed in Cynet, the ticket is also closed in ConnectWise.

MDM Integration on MAC

A new Cynet Installer is now available for MDM Integration on MAC. The installer includes a pre-installation script which creates the CynetEPSArguments.txt file in the tmp folder as the first step of the installation. After this, the MDM deploys the uploaded compliant package.

Sysdig Version 0.34.1 Included in New Linux Installations

Cynet now supports Sysdig version 0.34.1, the latest approved version. Starting in Cynet v4.18, new Linux installations include Sysdig version 0.34.1. Note that Cynet still supports Sysdig versions v28.0 and later.

title: Release Notes V4.20 (SaaS)
content:
Email Security (Gradual Rollout)

Cynet Email Security is a holistic security solution that provides mail protection for Cloud Email Gateways . It combines a variety of capabilities including attachment and URL scanning to ensure your inbox stays safe, real-time link protection which allows scanning the original target in real-time each visit, attachment extension filtering to block risky attachments and avoid malware disguised as harmless files, and policy controls letting you block what's bad and allow what's trusted using customizable allowlists and blocklists.
Notes: Email Security currently supports Office 365; other vendors will be added in the future. Email Security will be gradually rolled out to All-In-One customers once v4.20 is released.

New Users and Sites Management (Gradual Rollout)

Cynet has updated the Users and Sites management user interface. The new UI allows simple and powerful user and site management for all types of users, from the smallest organizations to large MSSPs with partners.
The new Users Management UI allows you an easy way to manage users from the new Users tab. You can add, edit and delete users, manage roles, and manage multi-factor authentication, all from a single convenient place.
The new Sites Management UI offers a hierarchical view of your sites, enabling you to more easily manage them. This multi-tenant approach enables the Global Manager to provide a single surface for all activities related to operating multiple sites.

Endpoint Vulnerabilities (Beta)

Cynet's new Endpoint Vulnerabilities Management service lets you manage Common Vulnerabilities & Exposures (CVE) risks across the site and on specific endpoints.
The service allows you to view vulnerabilities of unpatched binaries across all endpoints. You can see vulnerable software products such as Adobe, Office, Chrome and many others, view impacted endpoints and priority, as well as drilling down to the specific endpoint level.
Note: The Endpoint Vulnerabilities Management service is currently in Beta and will be gradually rolled out to customers. This feature is currently available on Windows OS only.

Endpoint Rollback (Beta)

Cynet's Endpoint Rollback allows customers to revert an endpoint infected with ransomware or other malware to its healthy state from before the infection. It provides t hreat remediation with system snapshots at multiple points in time. This is lifesaving when managing ransomware negotiation because the attacker does not hold the power, since crucial data is not at risk.
Endpoint Rollback allows you to enable backups for all endpoints or for a selected group, to view all available backups per endpoint, and to rollback an endpoint to a selected snapshot.
Note: Endpoint Rollback is currently in Beta and will be gradually rolled out to customers. To join the Beta, contact Cynet. This feature is available on Windows OS only.

Report Scheduling (Gradual Rollout)

Cynet now allows users to schedule periodic reports to be sent by email. Supported reports include the following reports:
Executive Report
Inventory Report
Agent Validation
Missing KBs on Host
Application Patch Validation
Unauthorized Applications

Allowlist and Exclusions Enhancements

Search and Sort abilities have now been added for Allowlist and Exclusion profile management tables.
Addition of Group and Host columns. Group shows the group name to which the profile is applied. Host shows the hostname on which the profile is applied.
Expand ability has now been added. This allows you to peek into the profile without editing it.

Device Control Support for Mac OS

This feature is now available for all customers.
MAC endpoints will now be able to detect device insertion, trigger an alert and block the insertion according to the storage device control profile settings.
This feature is supported for Mass Storage devices such as disk on key, external hard drive and memory cards (SD + microSD).
You can enable device control per group, in the Group Settings of your Mac devices.

SSPM Enhancements

Added new rules for Office365 and Sharepoint.
Added new rules for Google Cloud.
The SSPM Audit Log is now included in the Product Audit Log.
Added the option to add/remove/edit a simple comment for each issue.
Added the option to add/remove/edit a simple comment for each issue.

CLM Enhancements

New out-of-the-box dashboards for CLM! CLM customers can now see pre-configured Kibana dashboards in their environment with rich visuals of forensic events. The dashboards provided include: Office365 Authentication Activity Dashboard Fortigate Firewall Dashboard Meraki Firewall Dashboard Firepower Firewall Dashboard
Office365 Authentication Activity Dashboard
Fortigate Firewall Dashboard
Meraki Firewall Dashboard
Firepower Firewall Dashboard
The following new data sources are now certified: Ubiquiti Dream Machine (network device) Forcepoint firewall Thor (scanner)
Ubiquiti Dream Machine (network device)
Forcepoint firewall
Thor (scanner)
Note: The new built-in dashboards have already been released for existing CLM customers. For new customers the rollout will be gradual.

XDR Alert Emails

XDR Alerts are now integrated with email like the rest of Cynet's alerts.

Distribution Tool Enhancement

Cynet Distribution tool (Scanner tool) now allows customers to have the granularity to specify which group to deploy the Cynet Agent to when using continuous deployment (o ngoing scanning of Active Directory to discover new hosts and automatically deploy Cynet agent on them) . This is done by mapping between Cynet Groups and the Active Directory Organization Unit (OU) or Security Group (SG).

Domain and IP Exclusion Profiles

For Windows OS, exclusion profiles now support exclusions by the IP/IP Range exclusion type and Domain exclusion type for all network alerts.

Playbook Enhancements

Added a new field, OS Version in the Playbooks conditions Resources/Set Variables.

Removal of Generate EPS Button on Scanner Page

To eliminate user error when entering group names, the Generate EPS button on the Scanner page has been removed for SaaS customers. It is still possible to download the EPS for a specific group via the Groups Settings page, or by clicking the Install button at the bottom of the Cynet toolbar.

sections.text: For Windows OS, exclusion profiles now support exclusions by the IP/IP Range exclusion type and Domain exclusion type for all network alerts.

sections.heading: Playbook Enhancements
sections.text: Added a new field, OS Version in the Playbooks conditions Resources/Set Variables.

sections.heading: Removal of Generate EPS Button on Scanner Page
sections.text: To eliminate user error when entering group names, the Generate EPS button on the Scanner page has been removed for SaaS customers. It is still possible to download the EPS for a specific group via the Groups Settings page, or by clicking the Install button at the bottom of the Cynet toolbar.

title: Release Notes V4.21 (SaaS)
content:
Gradual Rollout of New Users and Sites Management

Cynet is gradually migrating customers to our new Users and Sites Management system, essential for new features in versions 4.20 and 4.21. Gradual migration ensures the best possible user experience throughout the transition.
A substantial number of customers have already been migrated, and we hope to complete the migration for all of our customers in the next few months.
Features in Versions 4.20 and 4.21 dependent on the migration include:
Users and Sites Management
Email Security
Reports Scheduling
Aggregated Number of Endpoints for MSSPs
We appreciate your patience while the rollout is in progress, and look forward to completing the migration. You can contact Cynet to expedite the rollout on your environment.

Email Security - Quarantine (Gradual Rollout)

Email Security now includes the ability to quarantine malicious emails or release them from quarantine.
Quarantined emails can be released or deleted from the Quarantine tab for up to 30 days from receipt, after which their status changes to "Quarantine - Expired".

Aggregated Number of Endpoints for MSSPs (Gradual Rollout)

In the new Sites page, MSSP customers can now see the overall consumption of endpoints shown per last 30 days and per calendar month. You can also see the breakdown per site in the new columns added to the sites list.
Note: Email Security and Aggregated Number of Endpoints for MSSPs are both dependent on the new Users and Site Management, and as such these features are part of the gradual rollout to customers.

ConnectWise Integration Enhancements

MSSP users who manage their customers as groups can now connect their customers to ConnectWise using our ConnectWise integration, which now supports management by scan group in addition to management by tenant.

Endpoint Rollback

Cynet's Endpoint Rollback allows customers to revert an endpoint infected with ransomware or other malware to its healthy state from before the infection. It provides t hreat remediation with system snapshots at multiple points in time. Having the option to restore critical data could be lifesaving in case of a ransomware incident .
Endpoint Rollback allows you to enable backups for all endpoints or for a selected group, to view all available backups per endpoint, and to rollback an endpoint to a selected snapshot.
Note: Endpoint Rollback is now available to all customers. This feature is available on Windows OS only.

XDR Alert Simulation

Users can now simulate an XDR alert for Azure Active Directory. You can generate the alert from the Users section in Azure, by creating a new user with the principal name "Cynet360-test" (with a capital letter C).

Removed Limitation When Getting Office 365 Logs to CLM

Removed the limitation requiring a premium license for Office 365 to get logs for CLM. Users can now get logs from Office 365 without the need for a premium license.

SSPM Port Scanner Excel File Enhancements

An issue with viewing Port Scanner results in Excel exports has been resolved, ensuring that the results are now easily viewable.

Audit Logs Enhancement

When sending audit logs to external SIEM, the audit message now includes the ReceiptTime (rt) and DeviceTimeZone (dtz) timestamps.

Features Currently in Beta

Cynet has a number of features currently in Beta. C ontact your Cynet representative to join the beta program .
All-in-One Dashboard
Endpoint Vulnerabilities

All-in-One Dashboard (Beta)

Our new All-in-One dashboard emphasizes Cynet's unique All-in-One cybersecurity platform. The dashboard clearly shows the wide range of coverage Cynet provides and the findings on each protected entity.
The dashboard prominently displays alerts and risks, along with warnings about missing configurations.
You can use the intuitive drill-downs to view details for risks and alerts.
You can view the previous dashboard, which contains the alerts radar and graphs, in the "Main" tab.

Endpoint Vulnerabilities (Beta)

Cynet's new Endpoint Vulnerabilities Management service lets you manage Common Vulnerabilities & Exposures (CVE) risks across the site and on specific endpoints.
The service allows you to view vulnerabilities of unpatched binaries across all endpoints. You can see vulnerable software products such as Adobe, Office, Chrome and many others, view impacted endpoints and priority, as well as drilling down to the specific endpoint level.
The All-in-One Dashboard and Endpoint Vulnerabilities Management service are dependent on migration to the new Users and Sites Management, and as such they are part of the gradual rollout to customers. To expedite your migration, contact your Cynet representative.
The Endpoint Vulnerabilities feature is currently available on Windows OS only.

title: Release Notes V4.22 (SaaS)
content:
Released July 2024. Version 4.22 (SaaS) includes the following features:

Gradual Rollout of New Features

Cynet is gradually migrating customers to our new Users and Sites Management system, essential for new features in versions 4.20 and above. Gradual migration ensures the best possible user experience throughout the transition.
A substantial number of customers have already been migrated, and we hope to complete the migration for all of our customers in the next few months.
Features in Version 4.22 dependent on the migration include:
Users and Sites Management
Email Security
Endpoint Security Posture Management (ESPM)
Single Sign On (SSO) Using Azure ID
All-in-One Dashboard
We appreciate your patience while the rollout is in progress, and look forward to completing the migration. You can contact Cynet to expedite the rollout on your environment.

Endpoint Security Posture Management (ESPM)

Endpoint Security Posture Management (ESPM) has the following risk management features:
Misconfiguration risks : Full visibility to configuration that exposes your organization to security risks, across all endpoints, continuous scanning, ability to drill down to specific endpoints, prioritization, categorization and important context on the risk and its mitigation (fix), including relevant MITRE tactics and techniques. It also includes the ability to export the data to a CSV file.
Vulnerability risks : Unpatched binaries with known CVEs (Common Vulnerabilities & Exposures), across all endpoints, continuous scanning, ability to drill down to specific endpoints.
ESPM is included in the Cynet All-In-One product, and not available otherwise.
For more information on ESPM, see Endpoint Misconfiguration and Endpoint Vulnerabilities Management .

Single Sign On Using Microsoft Entra ID (Formerly known as Azure AD)

Single Sign On (SSO) allows users to authenticate with a single identity provider and access multiple applications without needing to repeatedly enter credentials.
SSO is set first on the site level, and then one the individual user level. For each user, you can select which login method to use - SSO or local.
For more information on SSO including setup and use, see Configuring and Using Single Sign On (SSO) .

All-in-One Dashboard

Our new All-in-One dashboard emphasizes Cynet's unique All-in-One cybersecurity platform. The dashboard clearly shows the wide range of coverage Cynet provides and the findings on each protected entity.
The dashboard prominently displays alerts and risks, along with warnings about missing configurations.
You can use the intuitive drill-downs to view details for risks and alerts.
You can view the previous dashboard, which contains the alerts radar and graphs, in the "Main" tab.
For more information, see All-in-One Dashboard .
Note: All-in-One Dashboard is dependent on the new Users and Site Management, and as such this features is part of the gradual rollout to customers.

Antivirus Scan from Tray Icon Now Supports All Users

It is now possible to select whether to allow all users or only admin users to start an AV scan from the tray icon.
This is set by a new toggle in the Group settings under Manage Agent Settings . When Admins is selected, only local admins can start an AV scan from the tray icon. When All Users is selected, any user will be allowed to start an AV scan from the tray icon.
For more information about Antivirus scanning, see Antivirus Scanning .

Email Security and SSPM in Executive Reports

1. A page about Email Security is now available in the Executive Report of customers who have Email Security turned on.
2. A page about SaaS & Cloud is now available in the Executive Report for customers with an SSPM license.
For more information about Executive Reports, see Executive Summary Report .

Email Security Enhancements

1. Add Email to Blocklist - It is now possible to add entities to the blocklist directly from the Malicious Emails table.
2. Preview Quarantined Email - It is now possible to preview quarantined emails from the Cynet UI. The preview is shown as a PDF file.
For more information about Email Security, see our Email Security documentation.

Allowlist and Exclusion Enhancements

1. Prevent Users Adding System Processes - when adding Values to an Allowlist, Cynet now verifies that the content entered into the Value field is not a system process.
2. Import and Export of Allowlists and Exclusions - it is now possible to import and export Allowlists and Exclusion rules data. The export includes all of the profiles that exist on the current site.
Note: When exporting profiles, details of the Hosts or Groups to which a profile was applied are not included. As such, when importing, the profiles will be applied to all Groups.
For more information about Allowlists and Exclusion profiles, see Allowlisting and Exclusion Profiles .

title: Release Notes V4.20 (On-Premises)
content:
Released August 2024. Version 4.20 (On-Premises) includes the following features:

Allowlisting of Unauthorized File Operation Attempts

You can now Allowlist the Unauthorized File Operation Attempt alert.

Antivirus Scan Updates

A host action can now be invoked to perform a full Antivirus scan from the UI, for a specific endpoint. This can be done through Forensic > Hosts > Actions > Run AntiVirus Scan .
The scan type for an on-demand scan will currently be a full Antivirus scan only. In the future, you will be able to select a scan profile for the job.
When the EPS receives the request, the scan will start running immediately. On-demand scans will stop any other running scan and will start running.
You can now schedule (every day, week, or month) a full AV scan from the UI, per scan group.
You can also exclude specific paths from the scan.
There is also an option to request for the scheduled scan to be gradual. You can decide the gradual scan period, and during this timeframe (in hours), each endpoint on the group will start the scan.
You can cancel an actively running scan from the Action page.
For scans with In Progress status, you can check the scan checkbox and stop the scan by clicking Actions > Cancel Antivirus Scan .

Antivirus Scan Enhancements

Note : These AV-related features are currently Windows only.
You can now specify specific locations to run an AV scheduled scan on. You can choose pre-defined locations as well as desired locations.
To create a custom scan, go to Settings > Profiles > Antivirus .
In the scan profile, under Where to Scan , you can click Custom Scan . You can then click Add Location to apply the scan to specific locations only, including:
External drives
Program folders (Program Data + Program Files)
User profile folders
System drive
System folders
Temp folders
Additionally, you can insert a specific location to scan by choosing "path". You can also include sub-folders here:
You can now choose a special event as a trigger for a scheduled antivirus scan.
To create a scheduled event-based scan, go to Settings > Profiles > Antivirus .
Create a new profile, and under Where to Scan , you can select USB insertion .
This will enable you to run a scan on the USB device upon USB insertion.
You can now run or cancel an on-demand Antivirus scan locally, from the endpoint itself.
When right-clicking the tray icon, an on-demand full Antivirus scan can be triggered or cancelled. You can control whether to enable this functionality by going to Settings > Group .
Within the Agent Settings , you can select Trigger actions from endpoint .

Improved Sysdig Deployment

Sysdig will now be loaded by the Cynet endpoint, even if Sysdig is installed after the endpoint is installed.

Additional Supported Device Control

We now support device control on SCSI removable storage devices . You can do this when creating a storage device exception.

Device Control Support for Mac OS

MAC endpoints will now be able to detect device insertion, and trigger an alert according to the storage device control profile.
You can enable device control per group, in the Group Settings of your Mac devices.
You can choose the device control storage profile that you want to apply to your group.
The storage device control profile allows for the following to be configured:
Alert severity - the profile will specify in which severity the alert will trigger.
Ability to block the device - when enabled, the inserted devices will be blocked.
Authorized/Unauthorized - the ability to define whether all of the inserted devices are authorized or unauthorized.
Ability to create/add an exception: Authorized - if all devices are authorized, the devices under the exception will be unauthorized. Unauthorized - if all devices are unauthorized, the devices under the exception will be authorized.
Authorized - if all devices are authorized, the devices under the exception will be unauthorized.
Unauthorized - if all devices are unauthorized, the devices under the exception will be authorized.
Mac group settings (enable device control):

New Linux Combined Installer Script Implementation

The Linux Cynet installer now automatically installs important libraries, enabling improved security.
The new installer contains:
Sysdig
AuditD (note that this is optional).
Cynet’s Endpoint Security (EPS).
For additional information, see Single Endpoint Installation - Linux .

Add Exception Rule from Device Control Alert

From the Device Control alert, it is now possible to add the ID of a specific device as an exception for the selected profiles.

MDM Integration on MAC

A new Cynet Installer is now available for MDM Integration on MAC. The installer includes a pre-installation script which creates the CynetEPSArguments.txt file in the tmp folder as the first step of the installation. After this, the MDM deploys the uploaded compliant package.

Sysdig Version 0.34.1 Included in New Linux Installations

Cynet now supports Sysdig version 0.34.1, the latest approved version. Starting in Cynet v4.18, new Linux installations include Sysdig version 0.34.1. Note that Cynet still supports Sysdig versions v28.0 and later.

Allowlist and Exclusions Enhancements

Search and Sort abilities have now been added for Allowlist and Exclusion profile management tables.
Addition of Group and Hosts columns. Group shows the group name to which the profile is applied. Hosts shows the hostname on which the profile is applied.
Expand ability has now been added. This allows you to peek into the profile without editing it.

title: Release Notes V4.23 (SaaS)
content:
Released September 2024. Version 4.23 (SaaS) includes the following features:

Gradual Rollout of New Features

We are gradually migrating customers to our new Users and Sites Management system, essential for new features in versions 4.20 and above. Gradual migration ensures the best possible user experience throughout the transition.
A substantial number of customers have already been migrated, and we hope to complete the migration for all of our customers in the next few months.
Features in Version 4.23 dependent on the migration include:
Users and Sites Management
Email Security
Endpoint Management
Endpoint Misconfigurations
Single Sign On (SSO) Using Entra ID (Azure AD)
Reports Scheduling
All-in-One Dashboard
We appreciate your patience while the rollout is in progress, and look forward to completing the migration. You can contact us to expedite the rollout on your environment.

Email Security Enhancements

Manage by Group MSSP Customers who manage clients as a Group can now manage the Email Security integration by Group, rather than by tenant.
Limited Group Onboarding Customers can now choose to turn on Email Security only for a limited group of users, rather than for all users in the organization.
For more information, see Getting Started with Email Security.

Endpoint Management

The new Endpoint Management page is now available for all customers who have been migrated to the new Users and Sites Management system. Endpoint Management is an alternative to the existing Scanner page.
Version 4.23 includes the following enhancements to the Endpoints Management page:
Uninstall Endpoint It is now possible to uninstall an endpoint directly from the Endpoint Management page.
New Filters Replaced the default filters with a full set of standard filters.
Agent Version Added a column in the Endpoint Management page that shows the current version of the Agent that is installed on the endpoint.
Note: Currently the Scanner page and the Endpoint Management page exist side by side. However, we recommend that all customers who have access to the Endpoint Management page begin using it in preference to the Scanner page. Moreover, we strongly recommend against using both pages concurrently to manage the same endpoints.
Eventually, once all customers are migrated to the new Users and Sites Management and have access to the Endpoint Management page, the Scanner page will be deprecated and will no longer be supported.
For more information, see Endpoint Management .

Report Enhancements

It is now possible to select multiple Groups when generating an Executive Report manually.
In the Agent Validation Report , Application Patch Validation , Missing KB on Host , and Unauthorized Applications reports: Only endpoints which were active in the last 30 days and are still installed will be shown. Two new columns have been added: Group Name Site Name
Only endpoints which were active in the last 30 days and are still installed will be shown.
Two new columns have been added: Group Name Site Name
Group Name
Site Name
In the Inventory report, the following columns have been added: Last IP HD Serial Agent version First seen Last seen Active in last 30 days Group Name
Last IP
HD Serial
Agent version
First seen
Last seen
Active in last 30 days
Group Name
For more information, see Executive Summary Report and Advanced Reporting Framework .

API Additions

Generate Agent Install Script from API It is now possible to generate an Agent Install script from our API, using the Get Agent Script API. For more information, see the API Reference Guide .
Isolate and Unisolate Hosts from API It is now possible to isolate or unisolate a host using our API. For more details, see Isolate Host and Unisolate Host .

CLM for MSSPs

MSSPs who manage clients as Groups can now connect each group to its own Microsoft 365 account in order to bring logs into CLM.
For more information, see Managing Integrations by Group for MSSP Users .

New XDR Alerts and Simulated Alerts

Customers now have the ability to simulate an XDR alert for Fortigate firewall by attempting to log into the Fortigate console as user Cynet360-test@cynet.com .
The following new alerts have been added: Microsoft 365 Email Forwarding Rules: Detection of rules that forward emails outside the organization (this technique is used by attackers). Fortigate firewall console brute force attempt Fortigate firewall console user guessing Azure AD suspicious application added
Microsoft 365 Email Forwarding Rules: Detection of rules that forward emails outside the organization (this technique is used by attackers).
Fortigate firewall console brute force attempt
Fortigate firewall console user guessing
Azure AD suspicious application added
For more information, see XDR Alerts .

New Out-Of-The-Box CLM Dashboards

In addition to the existing preconfigured dashboards for CLM, we have now added two new preconfigured dashboards:
FortiAnalyzer
Palo Alto Networks.
Note: CLM dashboards are currently not auto-deployed. To enable them, please contact our support team.
For more information, see CLM Dashboards .

Network Discovery of Endpoints

Version 4.23 brings performance and stability enhancements for network discovery of endpoints in the Cynet Installer. Clicking Network Scan in the Installer scans the network and displays a list of all computers found (regardless of whether or not they have Cynet already installed). You can then select the computers on which you want to install or reinstall Cynet from the list of computers found.
For more information, see Network Installation Guide .

Azure AD Renamed to Entra ID

In the Integrations page, Azure AD has been renamed to Entra ID, to fit with Microsoft's renaming.
For more information, see Supported Services for CSPM & SSPM .

sections.text: Released September 2024. Version 4.23 (SaaS) includes the following features:

sections.heading: Gradual Rollout of New Features
sections.text:
We are gradually migrating customers to our new Users and Sites Management system, essential for new features in versions 4.20 and above. Gradual migration ensures the best possible user experience throughout the transition.
A substantial number of customers have already been migrated, and we hope to complete the migration for all of our customers in the next few months.
Features in Version 4.23 dependent on the migration include:
Users and Sites Management
Email Security
Endpoint Management
Endpoint Misconfigurations
Single Sign On (SSO) Using Entra ID (Azure AD)
Reports Scheduling
All-in-One Dashboard
We appreciate your patience while the rollout is in progress, and look forward to completing the migration. You can contact us to expedite the rollout on your environment.

sections.heading: Email Security Enhancements
sections.text:
Manage by Group MSSP Customers who manage clients as a Group can now manage the Email Security integration by Group, rather than by tenant.
Limited Group Onboarding Customers can now choose to turn on Email Security only for a limited group of users, rather than for all users in the organization.
For more information, see Getting Started with Email Security.

sections.heading: Endpoint Management
sections.text:
The new Endpoint Management page is now available for all customers who have been migrated to the new Users and Sites Management system. Endpoint Management is an alternative to the existing Scanner page.
Version 4.23 includes the following enhancements to the Endpoints Management page:
Uninstall Endpoint It is now possible to uninstall an endpoint directly from the Endpoint Management page.
New Filters Replaced the default filters with a full set of standard filters.
Agent Version Added a column in the Endpoint Management page that shows the current version of the Agent that is installed on the endpoint.
Note: Currently the Scanner page and the Endpoint Management page exist side by side. However, we recommend that all customers who have access to the Endpoint Management page begin using it in preference to the Scanner page. Moreover, we strongly recommend against using both pages concurrently to manage the same endpoints.
Eventually, once all customers are migrated to the new Users and Sites Management and have access to the Endpoint Management page, the Scanner page will be deprecated and will no longer be supported.
For more information, see Endpoint Management .

sections.heading: Report Enhancements
sections.text:
It is now possible to select multiple Groups when generating an Executive Report manually.
In the Agent Validation Report , Application Patch Validation , Missing KB on Host , and Unauthorized Applications reports: Only endpoints which were active in the last 30 days and are still installed will be shown. Two new columns have been added: Group Name Site Name

title: Release Notes V4.24 (SaaS)
content:
Released November 2024. Version 4.24 (SaaS) includes the following features:

XDR Alerts and Remediations in Microsoft 365

XDR Alerts are now available for the following categories:
Microsoft 365: Suspicious Login Attempts – including Impossible Travel (i.e. login attempts from geographical locations within a timeframe that cannot accommodate physical travel between them). Suspicious Permission Modification. Suspicious User Activity – including Suspicious Email Forwarding Rules (i.e. mailbox rules that forward emails to an external domain). Mailbox Manipulation.
Suspicious Login Attempts – including Impossible Travel (i.e. login attempts from geographical locations within a timeframe that cannot accommodate physical travel between them).
Suspicious Permission Modification.
Suspicious User Activity – including Suspicious Email Forwarding Rules (i.e. mailbox rules that forward emails to an external domain).
Mailbox Manipulation.
Firewalls: Command & Control. Suspicious Network Activity.
Command & Control.
Suspicious Network Activity.
VPN: Suspicious Login Attempts.
Suspicious Login Attempts.
VMWare ESXi: Suspicious Login Attempts. Suspicious User Activity.
Suspicious Login Attempts.
Suspicious User Activity.
See full XDR Alert documentation for more information.
Customers can now take the following XDR remediation actions:
Disable a Microsoft 365 user.
Enable a Microsoft 365 user (undo the Disable)
Disable a Microsoft 365 suspicious email rule.
Enable a Microsoft 365 suspicious email rule (undo the Disable).
For additional information on remediation actions, see Remediating Alerts .

Manage Email Recipients for Notifications

You can now manage email recipients for notifications directly from the Cynet console. Under Settings > Alerts , you can now enter one or more email addresses in the Cynet Email Recipients field, each of which will receive alert notifications. Email addresses should be separated by commas, as shown in the image below.
For additional information, see Sending Alerts by Mail .

Allowlisting - Support Wildcards

Allowlisting now supports the use of wildcards in the Value field in the Allowlist page. Supported wildcards are asterisk "*" and question mark "?".
For more information, see Allowlisting .

Endpoint Protection Best Practice Visibility

The All-in-One Dashboard has a new counter for endpoints that are not configured to deploy the Endpoint Protection best practices.
Additionally, the Group Settings page now has a new column called EPP Best Practice , which shows whether the EPP settings for the group are configured according to best practice or not.
For more information, see Endpoint Protection Best Practice .

Endpoint Management - Health Monitoring

A new "Status" column has been added to the Endpoint Management page. This is the initial stage of a new Health Monitoring capability. This column indicates the endpoint's current health status. Endpoint statuses can be one of the following:
OK : Endpoint has no errors/warnings
Warning: Endpoint has no errors, but has 1 or more warnings
Uninstalled: The endpoint was uninstalled
Inactive: The endpoint was last seen 30 days ago or more
For more information, see Endpoint Management .

Decoy Triggered Alert Enhancement

The Decoy Triggered alert is now clearer and easier to understand. Changes have been made in the UI and to the Decoy File Stolen From Host alert, which is now deprecated.
For more information, see Deception Alerts .

Enhanced Management of Users Across Customers (Including MSSPs and Resellers)

Customers can now invite existing users (either a user of a different customer, or of a reseller or MSSP who they do not directly manage) to be a guest on their account. This allows them to control the scope and role of the guest user, and allows MSSPs or resellers to get controlled access to customers not under their direct management.
For more information, see Managing Users and Guest Users .

CLM Logs in Manage by Group Mode

You can now connect multiple groups in the same site to their own Microsoft 365 tenant, to get CLM logs for Microsoft 365 when in Manage by Group mode.
For more information, see How to Send Logs to Cynet's CLM .

Improvements in Endpoint Vulnerabilities (CVE)

Improved the matching algorithm between the details of installed software on endpoints (such as name, version, etc.) and the lookup of that software in NIST. This enables us to more accurately identify vulnerabilities, resulting in fewer false positives.
For more information, see Endpoint Vulnerabilities .

Inactivity Logout Time

You can now adjust the inactivity timeout duration, specifying how long users remain logged in before being automatically logged out. To do this, go to Settings > Users & Roles > Settings tab and enter the desired logout time in minutes.

SSPM API for Getting Risks (Gradual Deployment)

Customers can now view SSPM risk results via API. This includes viewing only, and does not include taking actions such as fix, ignore, or comment.
The API includes the following API calls:
Get Latest SSPM Risks
Get SSPM Risks With Pagination
Get SSPM Risks Count
This feature is being gradually deployed to customers.

Improved User Registration Flow

We have improved the user registration flow to allow for easier self-onboarding.

Remote Desktop Access (Beta)

You can now start a remote desktop session directly from the Cynet UI, for Windows machines. It is supported for Windows Servers running version 2016 and above, and Endpoints running Windows 10 and above.

title: Release Notes V4.23 (On-Premises)
content:
Released December 2024. Version 4.23 (On-Premises) includes the following features and enhancements:

Allowlist and Exclusion Enhancements

1. Allowlisting Supports Wildcards - Allowlisting now supports the use of wildcards in the Value field in the Allowlist page. Supported wildcards are asterisk "*" and question mark "?".
2. Prevent Users Adding System Processes - when adding Values to an Allowlist, Cynet now verifies that the content entered into the Value field is not a system process.
3. Import and Export of Allowlists and Exclusions - it is now possible to import and export Allowlists and Exclusion rules data. The export includes all of the profiles that exist on the current site.
Note: When exporting profiles, details of the Hosts or Groups to which a profile was applied are not included. As such, when importing, the profiles will be applied to all Groups.
For more information about Allowlists and Exclusion profiles, see Allowlisting and Exclusion Profiles .

Report Enhancements

In the Agent Validation Report , Application Patch Validation , Missing KB on Host , and Unauthorized Applications reports: Only endpoints which were active in the last 30 days and are still installed will be shown. A new column for Group Name has been added.
Only endpoints which were active in the last 30 days and are still installed will be shown.
A new column for Group Name has been added.
In the Inventory report, the following columns have been added: Last IP HD Serial Agent version First seen Last seen Active in last 30 days Group Name
Last IP
HD Serial
Agent version
First seen
Last seen
Active in last 30 days
Group Name
For more information, see Executive Summary Report and Advanced Reporting Framework .

Isolate and Unisolate Hosts from API

It is now possible to isolate or unisolate a host using our API. For more details, see Isolate Host and Unisolate Host .

Audit Logs Enhancement

When sending audit logs to external SIEM, the audit message now includes the ReceiptTime (rt) and DeviceTimeZone (dtz) timestamps.

Decoy Triggered Alert Enhancement

The Decoy Triggered alert is now clearer and easier to understand. Changes have been made in the UI and to the Decoy File Stolen From Host alert, which is now deprecated.
For more information, see Deception Alerts .
