title: Release Notes V4.25 (SaaS)
content:
Released January 2025. Version 4.25 (SaaS) includes the following features:

Exporting Misconfigurations

You can now export the list of all endpoint misconfigurations to an Excel file. Additionally, from within the endpoints drilldown, you can export a list of the specific endpoints affected by a particular risk to an Excel file.

Alert Tagging

Customers can now tag alerts using predefined tags by clicking the plus ( + ) icon in an alert. You can choose from the following tags for each alert:
Penetration Testing
False Positive
Requires Details
In Progress
You can search for alerts with a specific tag using the search box at the top of the alerts page, or by selecting which tag to show, from the Tags dropdown at the top of the page.

XDR Alert Support for Certified CLM Data Sources

XDR Alert support has been added for the following firewalls in the Centralized Log Management Certified Data Sources:
Sophos
Watchguard
Meraki
SonicWall
For a full list of Certified Data Sources for which XDR Alerts are supported, see the Certified Data Sources Section in our Centralized Log Management documentation.

Scheduled Antivirus Scan Enhancements

Additional options have been added for scheduled Antivirus scans. Previously it was possible to set a scan to be performed on a specific day of the month (for example the 10th of every month). However there was no contingency for this date occurring on the weekend, when many endpoints may be offline.
Now, it is possible to schedule scans to be run either on a specific day (or days) of the month, as before, (for example, the 1st, 5th and 10th of every month) or to run on a specified day of the month, by day of the week (for example, the first Monday of every month).
Additionally, it is now possible to gradually scan the endpoints over a specified number of hours, in order to help preserve environment performance.

View Alerts and Forensic in Local Time

It is now possible to set the time zone in which the time of Alerts and Forensics are displayed (up to now, it was always displayed as UTC).
To set the preferred time zone, go to Settings > Configuration and select the preferred time zone from the dropdown list under the Time Zone section. If no time zone is specified, the user's local browser time is used.
Notes:
Time display adapts to daylight saving time.
Time zone settings are site-specific and shared across all users.
Migrated customers will be set with UTC as default. New sites will be set with "Local time" as default.
Migrated customers will be set with UTC as default. New sites will be set with "Local time" as default.

SSPM Google Workspace General Availability

The Google Workspace integration is now officially certified by Google. Connecting to it no longer shows a certification warning.

Agent Validation Report Enhancements

A column has been added to the Agent Validation report to show the application version number.

Additional OS Support for Isolate/Unisolate Remediation Action

The Isolate/Unisolate custom remediation action now supports only the following operating systems:
Windows 10 and above for desktop operating systems.
Windows Server 2016 and above for server operating systems.

Added Support for Additional Linux OS

The following Linux operating systems are now supported:
RedHat 9.5
Alma Linux 9 (latest)
Rocky Linux 9 (latest)
Amazon 2023
Oracle Linux 9.4

Updates for Sending Syslog to 3rd Party SIEM

If your organization runs your SIEM solution behind a firewall, it is now required to add the following IPs to the allowed list of IPs that can communicate with the SIEM solution:
EU customers: 18.200.154.75
US customers: 18.210.90.90

Custom XDR Alerts [Beta]

CyOps now offers the ability to manually configure custom XDR alert rules specific to the customer. This ability is available at no additional cost for active, paying customers who already use built-in CLM/XDR alerts.
This beta is available until June 30th, 2025. To be added to the beta, please contact your Cynet representative.

title: Beta Features
content:
Released March 2025. Version 4.26 (SaaS) includes the following features:

XDR Alerts

We now offer full coverage of built-in XDR alerts for all common firewalls.
For a full list of Certified Data Sources for which XDR Alerts are supported, see the Certified Data Sources Section in our Centralized Log Management documentation.

Email Security - Spam Filtering Policy

Added a new Spam Filtering Policy to the Email Security settings page. This setting enables you to differentiate spam emails from malicious emails. You can choose between tagging with a warning banner, moving to the "Junk" folder, and quarantining emails marked as Spam.

MSPs Control Packages and CLM Retention

MSPs now have control over selection of packages and CLM retention for each of their sites. You can set these for all new and existing customer sites from the Site Edit or Add Site dialog in the Sites page.

PSA/RMM Integration Enhancement

In the Integrations screen, integrations are now split into two sections: SaaS & Cloud and PSA/RMM . Each section can be separately set to manage integrations by group. MSPs managing customers per group can now create SaaS & Cloud integrations per customer while managing tickets within a single PSA/RMM integration at the site level.

ConnectWise Integration Flow Updates

We have updated the ConnectWise integration, making it simpler and clearer how to connect the integration. It now contains two distinct steps: First, you enter your connection details and authenticate those details. Next, you configure the specific settings for your ConnectWise instance, including setting the ticket status for new and resolved tickets, and mapping the severity levels in Cynet to the priority level in ConnectWise.

Mobile Security

Our Cynet Mobile app has a new version with improved detection and protection. This includes the following features:
Ability to identify and block non-compliant apps
New on-device privacy-friendly log forensic analysis
Detecting risky iOS shortcuts
PDF deep scan for malicious content and risky links
Network based web content filtering with Android local proxy
Auto-reconnect for on-device VPN
New device attestation threat detections

Device Control - Enforce Without Triggering Alerts

It is now possible to set up device control to track when unauthorized devices are connected, without triggering an alert.
Existing profiles will retain their current settings. To make sure existing profiles will continue to work as configured:
For existing profiles, the Block use of unauthorized devices setting will remain as it was before (if it was checked, it will be blocked, if unchecked only tracked).
For existing profiles, the Trigger an alert when an unauthorized device is connected setting will be checked by default.
Regardless of the alert trigger setting, you can continue to track device insertions from the Forensic page.
Note: For new profiles, the default state will be not to trigger an alert. To trigger alerts on new profiles, you will need to change the checkbox to checked.

Active Endpoints in Group Settings

By default, the Endpoints tab in Group Settings now only shows active endpoints (seen in the past 30 days). You can still choose to show all endpoints by selecting All Endpoints from the dropdown menu. You can also select Inactive Endpoints from the dropdown menu to view only those endpoints that were not active in the past 30 days.

Added Support for Additional Linux OS

Support has been added for the Linux Mint operating system.

Endpoint Alerts in CLM (Analytics View)

Endpoint alerts are now included in the Analytics view. A new index called *siem_ui-alerts* has been added, allowing customers to view in CLM not only Forensic info, but also the alerts that occurred during a specific timeframe. This allows you to get the full picture of detection and forensic events as a whole.
Note that this feature is being added to previous versions as well, and will appear even for customers who have not yet upgraded to v4.26.
Known limitation: The Alert ID is currently not shown on the CLM page.
Note: Since EPS alerts are generated by the Cynet agent on the endpoint, server side alerts and XDR alerts are not included in this feature.

Security Hardening Implemented Increasing Cynet Endpoint Binary Size

We have added new security features which cause an increase in the size of the Cynet binaries (for Windows, Linux and Mac).

Phase-Out of Syslog Tracelog TCP Option

As of version 4.26, it is no longer possible to use the Tracelog TCP connection option in the SIEM Server Settings. Customers currently using this option are requested to change to TCP .

Endpoint Misconfigurations Drilldown Enhancements

When looking at the drilldown of impacted endpoints in the Endpoint Misconfigurations page, you can now see both impacted endpoints and non-impacted endpoints. Additionally, a new Status column has been added to the drilldown page, which shows whether the risk is Open (impacted) or Closed (meaning it was either fixed already, or not impacted to begin with).

Web Content Filtering [Beta]

Web Content Filtering is now included in Cynet. This new feature enables you to create precise, category-based web filtering policies for enhanced control over internet access. You can configure which categories to track or block, directly from the UI.
Note: This feature is currently in beta. To be added to the beta, please contact your Cynet representative.

Endpoint Misconfigurations Fix from UI [Beta]

You can now fix risks found in the Endpoint Misconfigurations page directly from the UI. It is also possible to undo the fix for fixes that were performed by Cynet. To fix or unfix a risk, go to the endpoint drilldown page from the Endpoint Misconfigurations page and click the Fix/Unfix icon on the row of the endpoint you want to fix/unfix. It is also possible to bulk fix/unfix multiple endpoints at the same time, by selecting the endpoints to fix/unfix, and clicking the Fix/Unfix button at the top right-hand side of the page.
Note: The Fix button will only appear for endpoints that are currently online.
Fixing a risk is an asynchronous operation. This means that when you fix a risk, the system sends a command to the endpoint, which can take some time to run.
Note: This feature is currently available only to those participating in the Beta. To join the Beta, please contact your Cynet representative.

Custom XDR Alerts [Beta]

We now offer the ability to manually configure custom XDR alert rules specific to the customer. This ability is available at no additional cost for active, paying customers who already use built-in CLM/XDR alerts.
When a request is received, the CyOps team carefully reviews each request and may approve it, request additional information, or arrange a meeting to discuss it further.
Once a request is approved, we conduct thorough research to align the request with specific events and fields in CLM. We will create and test the CLM query to ensure it works effectively, monitoring it for false positives and noise levels over time. Initially, it will be run in a ‘log only’ mode, meaning it will be active in our backend but not visible to you in your user interface.
As needed, we will collaborate with you to fine-tune the detection algorithm. Ultimately, the approved alerts will be activated in your live console, appearing on your Alerts page just like our built-in XDR alerts.
To participate in the Beta, submit the Custom XDR Alerts Beta form. In the form, explain as accurately and clearly as possible the custom alert requests you require, their rationale, and any relevant details such as the CLM data source. For example, instead of requiring “events”, clarify if it is Windows Events or Microsoft 365 events or both, and if you have the specific technical details please provide them, such as the event identifier or titles.
We will review and communicate back, so make sure to leave accurate contact details.

Endpoint Management for MSPs [Beta]

MSP admins can now manage endpoints across tenants from the Endpoint Management page. Admins can filter by the site name, and can choose multiple sites.
Note: To join the Beta, please contact your Cynet representative.

Enhancements to Sites Page [Beta]

Removed the "Last 30 days" card from the top of the Sites page. Added the following new cards:
Total Unique Integrations: Shows the total number of unique integrations across all sites.
CLM 14 day, 30 day, and 90 day retention: Shows the amount of CLM data ingested in the last 30 days, which is subject to each retention period.

title: Features in Beta
content:
Released May 2025. Version 4.27 (SaaS) includes the following features:

Enhancements to Endpoints Management

Endpoints that are inactive for 30 days are now hidden automatically . If they communicate again, their status returns to active.
In addition, it is now possible to manually remove endpoints immediately, with the new Remove button. Removed endpoints will be uninstalled.
In the Forensics view (for historical data).
In the Endpoint Management page when the default filter is removed.
Exported data is affected by the applied filters.

Endpoint Misconfigurations Fix from UI

You can now fix risks found in the Endpoint Misconfigurations page directly from the UI. It is also possible to undo the fix for fixes that were performed by Cynet. To fix or unfix a risk, go to the endpoint drilldown page from the Endpoint Misconfigurations page and click the Fix/Unfix icon on the row of the endpoint you want to fix/unfix. It is also possible to bulk fix/unfix multiple endpoints at the same time, by selecting the endpoints to fix/unfix, and clicking the Fix/Unfix button at the top right-hand side of the page.
Note: The Fix button will only appear for endpoints that are currently online.
Fixing a risk is an asynchronous operation. This means that when you fix a risk, the system sends a command to the endpoint, which can take some time to run.
This feature is now available for all customers with an All-in-One license.

Remediation Action Enhancements

Isolate and Unisolate now support 32 bit machines in addition to 64 bit machines.
Support for Windows 7 has been added for Isolate and Unisolate.
We have made major improvements to the Remote Shell. The new remote shell is only available for 64 bit machines, and the old remote shell has been renamed "Remote Shell 32 Bit". These improvements include (but are not limited to) the following:
Rich Input Handling: Navigate command history and position the cursor with arrow keys; insert and delete text efficiently; clear screen with commands like 'clear' or 'cls'.
Copy/Paste Functionality: Seamless clipboard integration allows automatic text copying. Use Ctrl+C/Ctrl+V or right-click for pasting.
User Experience Improvements: Connection status messages and a custom disconnection page provide better user feedback.
Technical Improvements: Improved maintainability, faster bug fixes, better debugging and enhanced testing.

CyAI Detection

CyAI, our proprietary AI engine powering NextGen Antivirus with real-time, ML-based detection is now an integral part of Cynet. Its abilities and features include:
Real-Time Analysis : Instantly analyzes executables directly on the endpoints to keep you protected at all times.
Comprehensive Threat Detection : Effective against both known and zero-day threats for thorough protection.
Proven Reliability : Trained on millions of samples to ensure high accuracy and reliability.
Continuous Improvement : Operates within a continuous feedback loop that constantly updates and refines detection capabilities.
Exclusive Innovation : Built entirely in-house, leveraging our unique machine learning infrastructure.

Endpoint Agent Auto-Update Enabled by Default

Auto-Update is a group setting that controls automatic update for all endpoints in a group when a new Cynet SaaS version rolls out. It is now enabled by default in the following cases:
In Cynet's default groups when a new site is created.
When a new group is created.
When clicking to reset settings to Best Practice.

XDR Alerts Per Group for MSPs Managing Customers By Group

MSPs who use groups to manage their customers can now get XDR alerts per group, and see the group name in the alert. MSPs who do not use groups to manage their customers will still see the Cynet Group field in the alert, but the field will be empty.

Operational Notifications

Cynet can now send email notifications to customers about Operational events requiring their attention which are not security incidents (for which alerts are already generated).
Currently, CLM customers will get an email notification when external data sources stop sending data to CLM, which may indicate a configuration or networking issue. These notifications are only sent when the data source stops sending data for a significant length of time (by default, more than 12 hours). Once the data ingestion restarts, an additional email notification is sent.
In future, additional operational notifications will be added to this feature.

Customers Now See CLM Retention in Console

Customers can now see their actual CLM retention in the console, under the XDR tab of the All-in-One Dashboard, and the Analytics page of Forensics. Possible retention values are:
Trial (this is the default if the customer is not licensed for CLM)
14 days
30 days
90 days
Long term

Policy Alert & XDR Alert Description Update for PSA/RMM Integrations

Additional information has been added to the alert description for Policy alerts and XDR alerts generated for PSA/RMM integrations via the API. The description now includes the following information:
Host Name
Host IP
User Name
Group name
Process Path
Process Hash

PSA/RMM Integration Updates

We have updated the Datto integration, making it simpler and clearer how to connect the integration. It now contains two distinct steps: First, authentication, then configuration of specific settings for your Datto instance, including setting the ticket status for new and resolved tickets, and mapping the severity levels in Cynet to the priority level in Datto.
It is now possible to set the ConnectWise integration to close alerts in the Cynet console when they are closed in ConnectWise. To do this, after authenticating your integration, enable the Automatically close Cynet alerts when associated ConnectWise tickets are closed checkbox.

Updated ISO27001 Control References

The information displayed in CSPM and SSPM as it relates to ISO27001 previously displayed the 2013 version. Now it has been updated to the 2022 version.

Enhancement of Anti-Tampering Capabilities

This release brings improvements to our anti-tampering capabilities for Linux operating systems.

New Supported Operating Systems

The following Windows operating systems are now supported:
Windows 11 24H2
Azure Local 22H2

Features in Beta

The following features are available in Beta in version 4.27:

Manage Endpoints Across Sites for MSPs [Beta]

MSP admins can now manage endpoints from all sites using the Endpoint Management page, without going into each individual site. A new column has been added to the Endpoints Management page for MSPs which shows the site name for each endpoint. You can filter the page by site name, and you can also choose multiple sites to view. Users only have access to the sites they have permissions for.
Note: To join the Beta, please contact your Cynet representative.

Schedule Reports Across Sites for MSPs [Beta]

MSPs can now schedule reports across sites, selecting which sites to schedule for, as well as which reports they want to schedule.
Additionally, the following new reports have been added:
Endpoint Usage Report: Account-level report for MSPs which presents endpoint inventory for endpoints across the entire MSP account.
Site Utilization Report: Account-level report for MSPs which presents per-site utilization for active endpoints, CLM data, and unique integrations.
Site Vulnerability Report: Site-level report presenting all CVEs per endpoint. Each row in the report has a single endpoint and a single CVE.
Site Misconfiguration Report: Site-level report presenting all misconfigurations per endpoint. Each row in the report has a single endpoint and a single misconfiguration.

Enhancements to Sites Page [Beta]

Removed the "Last 30 days" card from the top of the Sites page. Added the following new cards:
Total Unique Integrations: Shows the total number of unique integrations across all sites.
CLM 14 day, 30 day, and 90 day retention: Shows the amount of CLM data ingested in the last 30 days, which is subject to each retention period.

New Installer for Linux [Beta]

The new Linux installer enables you to install the Cynet package and its dependencies directly from the installer package, including the sysdig package.
The installer also includes a summary of exactly what was installed and what modes are used, as well as additional helpful information.

Web Content Filtering [Beta]

Web Content Filtering is now included in Cynet. This new feature enables you to create precise, category-based web filtering policies for enhanced control over internet access. You can configure which categories to track or block, directly from the UI.

Custom XDR Alerts [Beta]

We now offer the ability to manually configure custom XDR alert rules specific to the customer. This ability is available at no additional cost for active, paying customers who already use built-in CLM/XDR alerts.
When a request is received, the CyOps team carefully reviews each request and may approve it, request additional information, or arrange a meeting to discuss it further.
Once a request is approved, we conduct thorough research to align the request with specific events and fields in CLM. We will create and test the CLM query to ensure it works effectively, monitoring it for false positives and noise levels over time. Initially, it will be run in a ‘log only’ mode, meaning it will be active in our backend but not visible to you in your user interface.
As needed, we will collaborate with you to fine-tune the detection algorithm. Ultimately, the approved alerts will be activated in your live console, appearing on your Alerts page just like our built-in XDR alerts.
To participate in the Beta, submit the Custom XDR Alerts Beta form. In the form, explain as accurately and clearly as possible the custom alert requests you require, their rationale, and any relevant details such as the CLM data source. For example, instead of requiring “events”, clarify if it is Windows Events or Microsoft 365 events or both, and if you have the specific technical details please provide them, such as the event identifier or titles.
We will review and communicate back, so make sure to leave accurate contact details.

Total Unique Integrations: Shows the total number of unique integrations across all sites.
CLM 14 day, 30 day, and 90 day retention: Shows the amount of CLM data ingested in the last 30 days, which is subject to each retention period.

sections.heading: New Installer for Linux [Beta]
sections.text:
The new Linux installer enables you to install the Cynet package and its dependencies directly from the installer package, including the sysdig package.
The installer also includes a summary of exactly what was installed and what modes are used, as well as additional helpful information.

sections.heading: Web Content Filtering [Beta]
sections.text: Web Content Filtering is now included in Cynet. This new feature enables you to create precise, category-based web filtering policies for enhanced control over internet access. You can configure which categories to track or block, directly from the UI.

sections.heading: Custom XDR Alerts [Beta]
sections.text:
We now offer the ability to manually configure custom XDR alert rules specific to the customer. This ability is available at no additional cost for active, paying customers who already use built-in CLM/XDR alerts.
When a request is received, the CyOps team carefully reviews each request and may approve it, request additional information, or arrange a meeting to discuss it further.
Once a request is approved, we conduct thorough research to align the request with specific events and fields in CLM. We will create and test the CLM query to ensure it works effectively, monitoring it for false positives and noise levels over time. Initially, it will be run in a ‘log only’ mode, meaning it will be active in our backend but not visible to you in your user interface.
As needed, we will collaborate with you to fine-tune the detection algorithm. Ultimately, the approved alerts will be activated in your live console, appearing on your Alerts page just like our built-in XDR alerts.
To participate in the Beta, submit the Custom XDR Alerts Beta form. In the form, explain as accurately and clearly as possible the custom alert requests you require, their rationale, and any relevant details such as the CLM data source. For example, instead of requiring “events”, clarify if it is Windows Events or Microsoft 365 events or both, and if you have the specific technical details please provide them, such as the event identifier or titles.
We will review and communicate back, so make sure to leave accurate contact details.

title: Release notes v4.27 (On-Premises)
content:
Released August 2025. Version 4.27 (On-Premises) includes the following features and enhancements:

Manage Email Recipients for Notifications

It is now possible to manage email recipients for notifications directly from the Cynet console. Under Settings > Alerts , you can now enter one or more email addresses in the Cynet Email Recipients field, each of which will receive alert notifications. Email addresses should be separated by commas, as shown in the image below.
For additional information, see Sending Alerts by Mail .

Allowlisting - Support Wildcards

Allowlisting now supports the use of wildcards in the Value field in the Allowlist page. Supported wildcards are asterisk "*" and question mark "?".
For more information, see Allowlisting .

Decoy Triggered Alert Enhancement

The Decoy Triggered alert is now clearer and easier to understand. Changes have been made in the UI and to the Decoy File Stolen From Host alert, which is now deprecated.
For more information, see Deception Alerts .

Device Control - Enforce Without Triggering Alerts

It is now possible to set up device control to track when unauthorized devices are connected, without triggering an alert.
Existing profiles will retain their current settings. To make sure existing profiles will continue to work as configured:
For existing profiles, the Block use of unauthorized devices setting will remain as it was before (if it was checked, it will be blocked, if unchecked only tracked).
For existing profiles, the Trigger an alert when an unauthorized device is connected setting will be checked by default.
Regardless of the alert trigger setting, you can continue to track device insertions from the Forensic page by going to the Hosts tab, selecting a host, and viewing the Device Control tab under the specific host.
Note: For new profiles, the default state will be not to trigger an alert. To trigger alerts on new profiles, you will need to change the checkbox to checked.

Added Support for Additional Linux OS

Support has been added for the following Linux Operating Systems:
RedHat 9.5
Alma Linux 9 (latest)
Rocky Linux 9 (latest)
Amazon 2023
Oracle Linux 9.4

Alert Tagging

It is now possible to tag alerts using predefined tags by clicking the plus ( + ) icon in an alert. You can choose from the following tags for each alert:
Penetration Testing
False Positive
Requires Details
In Progress
You can search for alerts with a specific tag using the search box at the top of the alerts page, or by selecting which tag to show, from the Tags dropdown at the top of the page.

Scheduled Antivirus Scan Enhancements

Additional options have been added for scheduled Antivirus scans. Previously it was possible to set a scan to be performed on a specific day of the month (for example the 10th of every month). However there was no contingency for this date occurring on the weekend, when many endpoints may be offline.
Now, it is possible to schedule scans to be run either on a specific day (or days) of the month, as before, (for example, the 1st, 5th and 10th of every month) or to run on a specified day of the month, by day of the week (for example, the first Monday of every month).
Additionally, it is now possible to gradually scan the endpoints over a specified number of hours, in order to help preserve environment performance.

View Alerts and Forensic in Local Time

It is now possible to set the time zone in which the time of Alerts and Forensics are displayed (up to now, it was always displayed as UTC).
To set the preferred time zone, go to Settings > Configuration and select the preferred time zone from the dropdown list under the Time Zone section. If no time zone is specified, the user's local browser time is used.
Notes:
Time display adapts to daylight saving time.
Time zone settings are site-specific and shared across all users.
Migrated customers will be set with UTC as default. New sites will be set with "Local time" as default.

Agent Validation Report Enhancements

A column has been added to the Agent Validation report to show the application version number.

Additional OS Support for Isolate/Unisolate Remediation Action

The Isolate/Unisolate custom remediation action now supports only the following operating systems:
Windows 10 and above for desktop operating systems.
Windows Server 2016 and above for server operating systems.

title: Features Being Gradually Deployed
content:
Released July 2025. Version 4.28 (SaaS) includes the following features:

Email Security Protection for Gmail - Early Availability

Email Security protection is now available for Gmail. Gmail integration provides comprehensive email security protection directly within Google Workspace environments. Organizations using Gmail can now benefit from advanced threat detection and prevention capabilities without disrupting existing email workflows or requiring user training on new interfaces.

Active Directory User Decoys

We have now added the ability to add decoy users into the Active Directory (AD). AD user decoys provide enhanced local and network attack detection by creating honeypot accounts that trigger alerts when decoy users are accessed. This proactive security measure enables early detection of lateral movement and privilege escalation attempts, significantly reducing the time attackers can remain undetected within the network environment.

Automatically Close Alert and Tag it As Part of Autoremediation

It is now possible to close an alert and add tags to it automatically as part of an autoremediation. Automated alert closure with appropriate tagging streamlines incident response workflows by automatically resolving successfully remediated security events. This reduces manual alert management overhead and provides clear audit trails for compliance reporting while ensuring security teams can focus on active threats requiring human intervention.

Newly Installed Endpoint Appearance in Management Console After Installation

Newly installed endpoints now appear promptly in the Endpoints Management page following installation, providing immediate visibility into network security posture. This rapid deployment visibility enables security teams to quickly verify successful installations and begin monitoring new assets without delays that could leave systems temporarily unprotected.

Web API Enhancements

The two versions of our API have now been consolidated into a single version which can be accessed using the one token. The consolidated API architecture streamlines integration development and maintenance by combining V1 and V2 API functionalities into a unified interface. Organizations can reduce development complexity and maintenance overhead while ensuring consistent automation capabilities across all platform integrations.
You can now extract MITRE tactic and technique details from alerts using the Web API. Automated MITRE ATT&CK tactic and technique extraction from security alerts through Web API provides security teams with standardized threat intelligence context for faster incident classification. This capability enables more efficient threat hunting and response by automatically correlating detected activities with known attack patterns, reducing analysis time and improving security posture assessment.

New Date Format Alignment for Alerts and Forensics

The date format used across the rest of the console is now used in Alerts and Forensics as well. Standardized date formatting across alerts and forensic data ensures consistency in reporting and analysis for managed service providers. This alignment reduces confusion during incident investigation and enables more efficient correlation of security events across different data sources and time periods.
The date format is as follows:
Day: Number
Month: 3 letters representing the month
Year: 4 numbers representing the year
For example, 23 Jul 2024.

Configuration Version in Alerts & Emails Becomes Agent Configuration Date

The Configuration Version field in alerts (both in the console UI and email alerts) which previously showed a numeric value, has been replaced by a new field called Agent Configuration Date. This new field contains the date and time when the Cynet Cloud Configuration was last updated. The new field is shown in the standard timestamp format of YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ss.sss.

PSA/RMM Enhancements

It is now possible to verify the integration with both Datto and Connectwise is working using the new Send Test Ticket button in the integration. This capability reduces support tickets and troubleshooting time while ensuring critical business management systems maintain reliable data synchronization for optimal operational efficiency.

End-User Approval Required for Remote Desktop Access (Windows Only)

Remote desktop access to endpoints now requires approval from the end user when one is logged in, ensuring secure and transparent operations. This change enhances GDPR compliance by giving control to users over access requests. On Windows servers with multiple connections, all connected users must provide approval before access is granted.

Optimized Communication Between the Endpoint and Cynet

To improve the upload speed from the endpoint to Cynet, an additional network domain is used. To allow this communication, verify that it is permitted on your perimeter firewall.
For US: ws-us.cynet.com
For Europe: ws-eu.cynet.com
To view details of all required firewall configurations, see SaaS Communication Requirements .

New Endpoint Tray Icon

The endpoint tray icon has now been changed to a new, blue icon to match the rest of our UI. Due to caching, the new icon may only come into effect after endpoint restart.

Antivirus Scan Alerts No Longer Trigger for Already Remediated Files

Antivirus scans no longer trigger alerts for Antivirus Scan alerts ( Detection Engine - Malicious Binary - Malicious File Found on Disk ) that have already been remediated. This helps create a more efficient, focused, and user-friendly antivirus experience by eliminating unnecessary noise from already-resolved issues.

Auto-Activation of Features for All-In-One

Customers get access to All-in-One features automatically, when their All-in-One license goes into effect, without the need for manual activation. This includes Email Security, Endpoint Misconfigurations, Endpoint Vulnerabilities, etc.

Mobile Security

Our Cynet Mobile app has a new version with improved capabilities and experience. This includes the following features:
Bulk actions on threats.
SSO improvements
Visibility for external IP addresses.
Web Content Filtering silent mode.
Additional reports for iOS and Android.
Filtering enhancements.

Support for Windows Server 2025

Windows Server 2025 is now officially supported. Windows Server 2025 compatibility ensures you can deploy endpoint protection on the latest Microsoft server operating system without compatibility concerns. This forward-looking support enables you to modernize your infrastructure while maintaining consistent security coverage across all server environments, ensuring protection continuity during technology upgrades.

UI Improvements

As part of the v4.28 release, we are gradually deploying several improvements to the UI. These include the following improvements:
Updated Login Page
Updated site navigation
Updated look and feel
Together with the UI improvements, comes the ability to release services and UI changes more incrementally.
The following features are based on the UI improvements and are being gradually deployed to customers throughout September, October and November:

Endpoints Management Enhancements

We have added an enhanced filtering system to the Endpoints Management page that gives you complete control over how you view and manage your endpoint inventory. This flexibility reduces time spent navigating through endpoints and enables your security teams to focus on the most critical assets requiring attention.
The Endpoints Management page now shows the endpoint's private IP instead of its public IP. Private IP address visibility on the endpoints page provides network administrators with essential information for internal network management and troubleshooting. This additional detail enables faster incident response and network configuration management without requiring administrators to access separate network monitoring tools or endpoint details pages. The public IP can still be seen by expanding the endpoint to see more details.
The Endpoint Misconfigurations page now has enhanced filtering capabilities similar to those found in the Endpoints Management page. These filtering capabilities for endpoint misconfigurations allow you to quickly locate and manage specific risks based on severity, category, product, etc. This reduces time spent searching through large numbers of risks.
Customers can now view security posture of a specific endpoint or a group of endpoints. This is done by filtering the Endpoints Misconfigurations page by Endpoint or by Group. These dedicated filtering options for endpoints and groups provide granular control over endpoint misconfigurations, enabling you to efficiently organize and manage your risk infrastructure.

Microsoft 365 Integration - Least Privilege Access

Our Office 365 integration has now been renamed to Microsoft 365. Customers can now select which feature they want to connect to (SSPM, CLM, or Email Security). The connectivity to Microsoft 365 logs (CLM) requires the "Privileged Role Administrator" role (SSPM and Email Security role requirements remain unchanged). This enables you to implement least privilege access principles while maintaining seamless productivity suite protection. This approach minimizes security exposure while ensuring users retain access to essential Microsoft 365 security capabilities based on their specific role requirements.
Notes: Customers who have previously connected to our Microsoft 365 integration will continue to be connected as previously. Only upon disconnecting will they see the new user experience.

Assign Endpoint Limit Per Site for MSP

Site-specific endpoint limits provide managed service providers with granular control over resource allocation and client service levels. At the end of each month, an email is sent to the MSP about any sites which have gone over their allocated consumption. This capability enables MSPs to optimize service delivery costs while ensuring each client site receives appropriate endpoint protection coverage based on their service agreement and business requirements.

Site Name as Hyperlinks Redirecting to Site

In the Sites page, clicking on a site name now provides direct navigation to the individual client's site. This streamlined navigation reduces the time MSPs spend switching between different client environments and improves operational efficiency when managing multiple customer sites simultaneously.

title: Release Notes V4.29 (SaaS)
content:
Released September 2025. Version 4.29 (SaaS) includes the following features:

Gradual Rollout of UI Improvements

As part of the v4.29 release, we are gradually deploying several improvements to the UI. These enhancements include a redesigned login page, improved site navigation, and a refreshed overall look and feel.
Together with these UI enhancements, comes the ability to release services and UI changes more incrementally.
The following features rely on the recent UI enhancements and are being gradually rolled out to customers between September and November 2025:
Email Security Enhancements
Download Reports from the Account Level (MSP)
Support All Profiles from Site Manager (MSP)
Web Content Filtering Enhancements
Manage Endpoints Across Sites for MSPs
Schedule Reports Across Sites for MSPs
Enhancements to Sites Page
We appreciate your patience while the rollout is in progress, and look forward to its completion.

Email Security Enhancements (Gradual Rollout)

It is now possible to receive a daily or weekly email digest containing a list of all emails that were quarantined, with the option to release spam emails yourself, or request that an admin release emails flagged as malicious.
Admins have the power to:
Approve or deny release requests
Preview message content
Customize the digest email message.
The system also maintains comprehensive audit logs for all release actions. This new capability empowers users to manage their quarantined emails more efficiently, reduces the workload on IT teams, and improves overall email security by ensuring that important messages aren't lost while maintaining robust protection against potential threats.
For additional details, see Email Security - Digest .
Email Security now includes the ability to automatically resolve and reveal the real destination behind shortened URLs (such as those from bit.ly) in emails, revealing the true destination behind these potentially deceptive links.
The system seamlessly integrates this unmasked URL into existing threat detection workflows, allowing for thorough analysis of the actual destination for malicious content. This feature works both during initial email scanning and in real-time protection scenarios, ensuring comprehensive coverage.
By automatically exposing hidden destinations, this tool significantly improves your SOC team's ability to identify and prevent phishing attempts that leverage URL shorteners to disguise malicious websites. With robust error handling and optimized performance, this feature empowers your security analysts to work more efficiently and effectively in safeguarding your organization against evolving cyber threats.
ARC has now been implemented for Email Security. ARC is a method that preserves the trustworthiness of an email’s authentication results across multiple hops. By implementing ARC, Cynet signs the chain of authentication results, which allows Microsoft to trust that the original authentication checks (SPF, DKIM, DMARC) were not tampered with, even if the message was modified while passing through Cynet.

Web Content Filtering (Gradual Rollout)

Web Content Filtering is now included in Cynet. This new feature enables you to create precise, category-based web filtering policies for enhanced control over internet access. You can configure which categories to track or block, directly from the UI.
Web Content Filtering will be available for all our customers once they are moved over to the improved UI. It includes the following new functionalities that have been added since the Beta:
Users can now add domain-based blocklists to be used on different profiles, which in turn can be applied to groups. This enables customers to add their own custom blocklists, in addition to the existing categories.
This feature displays a pop-up notification when a user attempts to access a domain restricted by your organization's WCF policy. The pop-up provides information about the blocked domain, including the action taken, domain category, process involved, timestamp, and a description of why access was restricted.
Pop-up alert notifications are easily configurable through group settings, as part of the general endpoint alert message settings. To configure, go to Settings > Groups , and edit the group for which you want to configure. Click on Manage Agent Settings , and toggle the Display Alerts on Endpoint setting to On .
Note: In the first phase, local alert messages will be triggered only for domains that match predefined categories. In the next version, we will also include domains from the blocklist.
We have added additional categories to our Web Content Filtering feature. A full list of available categories can be found in the Web Content Filtering documentation .
For additional information, see Web Content Filtering .

XDR Alerts - Allowlisting

It is now possible to allowlist XDR alerts by IP, User, or Other Fields. This feature allows you to filter out known benign activities or trusted sources, reducing alert fatigue and helping you focus on critical security events. Allowlisted alerts are still generated but only visible to Cynet, not displayed in your UI or sent via email. You can easily add multiple entries, and when inputting IP addresses, they are validated before you can continue to create the allowlist. By implementing this granular control at the XDR alert level, you can maintain comprehensive security logs while streamlining the alerts that require your immediate attention, ultimately improving your security operations efficiency.
For additional information, see Allowlisting XDR Alerts .

Download Reports from the Account Level (MSP) (Gradual Rollout)

It is now possible for MSPs to download specific reports from the account level or for selected sites, enhancing the reporting capabilities for our MSP users. This powerful tool allows users to download specific reports for multiple selected sites or for an entire account, streamlining the process of gathering and analyzing crucial security data.
The system intelligently adapts to your subscription level, displaying appropriate options and informative messages based on whether sites are using All-in-One (AIO) or Elite packages (note that Misconfiguration and Vulnerability reports are only available for AIO customers).
Additionally, the Executive Report now distinguishes between AIO and Elite sites, ensuring that the information presented is tailored to each site's capabilities. With customizable period selection and an intuitive interface, this feature empowers MSPs and administrators to efficiently gather, compare, and analyze security data across their managed sites, ultimately enhancing their ability to make informed decisions and maintain robust security postures for their clients.

For more information, see Reports at the Account Level . Run Antivirus Scan as Part of an Auto-Remediation Rule

For more information, see Reports at the Account Level .
It is now possible to add running an antivirus scan as part of an auto-remediation rule. This addition allows users to incorporate antivirus scans directly into their auto-remediation rules, providing a more comprehensive and proactive approach to threat management.
Now, when configuring Host Remediation actions, you'll find the option to trigger an AV Scan as part of the automated response workflow. This feature empowers security teams to create more dynamic and thorough remediation strategies, automatically initiating antivirus scans in response to specific security events or alerts. By integrating AV Scans into your auto-remediation rules, you can ensure faster response times, reduce manual intervention, and maintain a more robust security posture.
For additional information on auto-remediation rules, see Automatically Remediating Alerts .

API Enhancements

It is now possible to add a new group using the "Add Group" API , designed to enhance the flexibility and automation capabilities of your deployment. This powerful addition to our API suite allows users to programmatically create new groups within their environment, streamlining the process of managing and organizing endpoints.
The API accepts a simple POST request with key parameters including:
Tenant ID (which is token-based for security)
Group Name
Operating System
As an added benefit, all newly created groups are automatically configured with our "Best Practice" settings, ensuring optimal security posture from the outset.
It is now possible to add comments to alerts via the API using the "Update Alert Status" API . This new feature allows users to improve collaboration and streamline alert management workflows. Now, when updating alert status through the API, users can simultaneously append comments to provide context or notes about the alert.

Profile Page Reordering - Allowlist & Exclusions (Gradual Rollout)

The Allowlist & Exclusions page has now moved from within the Profiles page to its own unique page within the Settings list. Remaining in the Profiles page are Antivirus, Windows Events, File Monitor and Storage Device Control.
For more information, see Allowlisting and Exclusions .

Manage Endpoints Across Sites for MSPs (Gradual Rollout)

MSP admins can now manage endpoints from all sites using the Endpoint Management page, without going into each individual site. A new column has been added to the Endpoints Management page for MSPs which shows the site name for each endpoint. You can filter the page by site name, and you can also choose multiple sites to view. Users only have access to the sites they have permissions for.
For more information, see Managing Endpoints Across Sites .

Schedule Reports Across Sites for MSPs (Gradual Rollout)

MSPs can now schedule reports across sites, selecting which sites to schedule for, as well as which reports they want to schedule.
Additionally, the following new reports have been added:
Endpoint Usage Report: Account-level report for MSPs which presents endpoint inventory for endpoints across the entire MSP account.
Site Utilization Report: Account-level report for MSPs which presents per-site utilization for active endpoints, CLM data, and unique integrations.
Site Vulnerability Report: Site-level report presenting all CVEs per endpoint. Each row in the report has a single endpoint and a single CVE.
Site Misconfiguration Report: Site-level report presenting all misconfigurations per endpoint. Each row in the report has a single endpoint and a single misconfiguration.
For more information, see Reports at the Account Level .

Enhancements to Sites Page (Gradual Rollout)

Removed the "Last 30 days" card from the top of the Sites page. Added the following new cards:
Total Unique Integrations: Shows the total number of unique integrations across all sites.
CLM 14 day, 30 day, and 90 day retention: Shows the amount of CLM data ingested in the last 30 days, which is subject to each retention period.
For more information, see Managing Sites and Resource Usage .

Added Support for Additional Windows OS

Support has been added for the Windows 11 25H2 operating system.

sections.text: Released September 2025. Version 4.29 (SaaS) includes the following features:

sections.heading: Gradual Rollout of UI Improvements
sections.text:
As part of the v4.29 release, we are gradually deploying several improvements to the UI. These enhancements include a redesigned login page, improved site navigation, and a refreshed overall look and feel.
Together with these UI enhancements, comes the ability to release services and UI changes more incrementally.
The following features rely on the recent UI enhancements and are being gradually rolled out to customers between September and November 2025:
Email Security Enhancements
Download Reports from the Account Level (MSP)
Support All Profiles from Site Manager (MSP)
Web Content Filtering Enhancements
Manage Endpoints Across Sites for MSPs
Schedule Reports Across Sites for MSPs
Enhancements to Sites Page
We appreciate your patience while the rollout is in progress, and look forward to its completion.

sections.heading: Email Security Enhancements (Gradual Rollout)
sections.text:
It is now possible to receive a daily or weekly email digest containing a list of all emails that were quarantined, with the option to release spam emails yourself, or request that an admin release emails flagged as malicious.
Admins have the power to:
Approve or deny release requests
Preview message content
Customize the digest email message.
The system also maintains comprehensive audit logs for all release actions. This new capability empowers users to manage their quarantined emails more efficiently, reduces the workload on IT teams, and improves overall email security by ensuring that important messages aren't lost while maintaining robust protection against potential threats.
For additional details, see Email Security - Digest .
Email Security now includes the ability to automatically resolve and reveal the real destination behind shortened URLs (such as those from bit.ly) in emails, revealing the true destination behind these potentially deceptive links.
The system seamlessly integrates this unmasked URL into existing threat detection workflows, allowing for thorough analysis of the actual destination for malicious content. This feature works both during initial email scanning and in real-time protection scenarios, ensuring comprehensive coverage.
By automatically exposing hidden destinations, this tool significantly improves your SOC team's ability to identify and prevent phishing attempts that leverage URL shorteners to disguise malicious websites. With robust error handling and optimized performance, this feature empowers your security analysts to work more efficiently and effectively in safeguarding your organization against evolving cyber threats.
ARC has now been implemented for Email Security. ARC is a method that preserves the trustworthiness of an email’s authentication results across multiple hops. By implementing ARC, Cynet signs the chain of authentication results, which allows Microsoft to trust that the original authentication checks (SPF, DKIM, DMARC) were not tampered with, even if the message was modified while passing through Cynet.

sections.heading: Web Content Filtering (Gradual Rollout)
sections.text:
Web Content Filtering is now included in Cynet. This new feature enables you to create precise, category-based web filtering policies for enhanced control over internet access. You can configure which categories to track or block, directly from the UI.
Web Content Filtering will be available for all our customers once they are moved over to the improved UI. It includes the following new functionalities that have been added since the Beta:
Users can now add domain-based blocklists to be used on different profiles, which in turn can be applied to groups. This enables customers to add their own custom blocklists, in addition to the existing categories.
This feature displays a pop-up notification when a user attempts to access a domain restricted by your organization's WCF policy. The pop-up provides information about the blocked domain, including the action taken, domain category, process involved, timestamp, and a description of why access was restricted.
Pop-up alert notifications are easily configurable through group settings, as part of the general endpoint alert message settings. To configure, go to Settings > Groups , and edit the group for which you want to configure. Click on Manage Agent Settings , and toggle the Display Alerts on Endpoint setting to On .
Note: In the first phase, local alert messages will be triggered only for domains that match predefined categories. In the next version, we will also include domains from the blocklist.
We have added additional categories to our Web Content Filtering feature. A full list of available categories can be found in the Web Content Filtering documentation .
For additional information, see Web Content Filtering .

sections.heading: XDR Alerts - Allowlisting
sections.text:
It is now possible to allowlist XDR alerts by IP, User, or Other Fields. This feature allows you to filter out known benign activities or trusted sources, reducing alert fatigue and helping you focus on critical security events. Allowlisted alerts are still generated but only visible to Cynet, not displayed in your UI or sent via email. You can easily add multiple entries, and when inputting IP addresses, they are validated before you can continue to create the allowlist. By implementing this granular control at the XDR alert level, you can maintain comprehensive security logs while streamlining the alerts that require your immediate attention, ultimately improving your security operations efficiency.
For additional information, see Allowlisting XDR Alerts .

title: Release Notes V4.30 (SaaS)
content:
Released November 2025. Version 4.30 (SaaS) includes the following features:

GenAI Alert Summaries to Assist User Understanding (Gradual Rollout)*

Alerts in the console now include a GenAI description of the alert which translates complex technical data into easy to understand insights. This can accelerate incident response and reduce analyst training requirements by providing intelligent explanations that help security teams quickly understand significance.
Note: Not all alerts have GenAI alert summaries available yet, but those which don't will be added in the coming months.

New Manual Remediation: Disable/Enable Active Directory Domain User (Gradual Rollout)*

Using a new manual remediation, it is now possible to disable or enable an Active Directory Domain user in the On-Premises Active Directory. This means that we now support disabling and enabling Active Directory Domain users in the following environments:
On-premises: Disables/Enables the user account in Active Directory Domain Services (AD DS)
Hybrid: Disables/Enables the user account in both Active Directory Domain Services and Microsoft Entra ID (synchronized via Microsoft Entra Connect)
A prerequisite for this feature to work is that Cynet Agent is installed on the primary Domain Controller of the local AD.
For more information, see Disable/Enable Active Directory User .

Proactive CyOps Incident Response (Gradual Rollout)*

Elite and All-in-One customers can now choose whether or not they would like CyOps to be proactive in remediating their incidents. Enabling this allows CyOps to remediate incidents faster, and increases the security level provided by CyOps.
To enable proactive CyOps as an MSP, navigate to the new CyOps tab under Settings in the Cynet toolbar, and select the Proactive radio button in the CyOps Incidence Response Settings page. If you are a direct customer, please open a support ticket requesting to enable it.
Once enabled, you can select the remediation actions you want CyOps to perform proactively. Additionally, if you want certain actions to be performed only on specific hosts or sites, you can exclude these hosts or sites from proactive remediation.
For more information, see CyOps Incident Response Settings .

Enable Customers to Discover Endpoints on Which the Cynet Agent is Not Installed (Gradual Rollout)*

Customers who have Microsoft 365 integrated with Cynet for SSPM now have the ability to view all machines on their Entra ID domain which have logged in during the past 30 days, and do not yet have the Cynet agent installed on them (known as non-deployed). This enables the security admin to reduce the organization's attack surface by bringing these devices under security management.
Endpoints that do not have the Cynet agent installed are shown in the new Non-Deployed Endpoints tab in the Endpoint Management page. For each non-deployed endpoint, you can see the endpoint name, ID, OS, OS version, Discovery type, Cynet Agent status, and when the endpoint was last seen by Entra ID.
Notes:
This feature requires integration with Microsoft 365 using the SSPM toggle, due to the permissions required to view non-deployed endpoints, which are used in the Microsoft 365 integration.
When searching for non-deployed endpoints on the local AD, Cynet can only see existing endpoints on which the installed version of the Cynet agent is v4.30 or above.
For more information, see Non-Deployed Endpoint Management .

Endpoint Vulnerabilities Enhancements

When generating a vulnerabilities report from the Endpoint Vulnerabilities page, the exported file now generates a CSV file with all risks and all endpoints impacted by each risk. Impacted endpoints are shown each on their own line under the risk.
The Endpoint Vulnerabilities page now has enhanced filtering capabilities similar to those found in the Endpoints Management and Endpoints Misconfigurations pages. These filtering capabilities for endpoint vulnerabilities allow you to quickly locate and manage specific vulnerabilities based on severity, category, product, etc. This reduces time spent searching through large numbers of vulnerabilities.
You can now view vulnerabilities of a specific endpoint or a group of endpoints. This is done by filtering the Endpoints Vulnerabilities page by Endpoint or by Group. These dedicated filtering options for endpoints and groups provide granular control over endpoint vulnerabilities, enabling you to efficiently organize and manage your vulnerability infrastructure.
For more information, see Endpoint Vulnerabilities .

Endpoint Vulnerabilities and Misconfigurations in All-in-One Dashboard

Endpoint vulnerability and misconfiguration highlights are now shown in the Risk Categories list in the Endpoint Security section of the All-in-One dashboard.
For more information, see All-in-One Dashboard .

Ignoring Endpoint Misconfigurations

You can now ignore endpoint misconfigurations by clicking on the Ignore button. Ignoring a misconfiguration can be done at the organization level or at the endpoint level. If you ignore a misconfiguration at the organization level, it is still possible to unignore it for specific endpoints. This allows you to avoid being notified about misconfigurations that are not of interest to you and simplifies your alert management.
For more information, see Endpoint Misconfigurations .

Enable PSA/RMM Integrations at the Account Level (MSP)

It is now possible for MSP users to enable PSA/RMM integrations directly from the account level. This means that:
Once the integration is configured, all sites (existing and new) will get the default configuration.
Site level company and board can be tuned to allow mapping of each site to a different board.
Users can edit the integration per site and disconnect from the default settings.
For more information, see Managing Integrations at the Account Level .

New Icons for NinjaOne and N-able Integrations

The NinjaOne and N-able integrations now have their own icons in the PSA/RMM section of the Integrations page. They currently are available only at the account level, and clicking on the icon takes you to the integration documentation.
For more information, see Managing Integrations at the Account Level .

API Enhancements

We have added a new API called Move Host Between Groups that enables administrators to move endpoints between groups on the same tenant. This streamlines operational workflows and improves endpoint lifecycle management, particularly for structured organizations or MSPs managing multiple client environments within a single tenant.
Note: Only users with administrator or group-move privileges can use this API.
We have added new fields to the Get Host Properties API to provide details of the antivirus database date and version for each host. The following fields have been added to the response in a new Antivirus section:
Status
DatabaseDate
DatabaseVersion

Ransomware Decoy Files - New File Types Added

We have added additional file types to the available ransomware decoy files. This strengthens the ability of the decoys to successfully prevent ransomware attacks.
For more information, see Deception .

Antivirus Version Shown in UI for Linux and Mac

For Linux and Mac endpoints, we have added Antivirus details in the Forensics page endpoint drilldown. These details include the antivirus status, database version and database date (the time the antivirus database was updated). This gives users an additional way to verify that the antivirus is functioning properly and up to date.
The antivirus status can be seen by going to Forensic > Hosts , and selecting a host to view. The antivirus information appears on the right-hand side of the Details tab.
For more information, see Investigating Hosts .

New SSPM Compliance Standards Added

The following new compliance standards have been added for SSPM:
CAF
Cyber Essentials
NIS2
For more information, see Cloud & Saas Security Posture Management .

New CLM Data Sources Added

The following new data sources have been added for CLM:
Check Point Quantum
Check Point Spark
Cisco ISE
For more information, see Centralized Log Management .

Added Support for Additional Windows OS

Support has been added for the Windows 11 25H2 operating system.
For more information, see Supported Operating Systems .

Endpoint Management - Health Monitoring Enhancements (Gradual Rollout)*

In the Endpoint Managment page, a new Error status has been added to the Status column and in the endpoint drilldown. When an error or warning is present, specifics of the error or warning details can be found in the System error field after expanding the endpoint to see the full endpoint details.
For more information, see Endpoint Management .

* Note Regarding Gradual Rollout of New Features

Features marked as Gradual Rollout are being released to customers on a gradual basis during the weeks following the release.
We appreciate your patience while the rollout is in progress, and look forward to its completion.
